[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#rocket---core-api-documentation)
Rocket - Core API Documentation
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hello, and welcome to the core Rocket API documentation!

This API documentation is highly technical and is purely a reference. There’s an [overview](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/overview)
 of Rocket on the main site as well as a [full, detailed guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide)
. If you’d like pointers on getting started, see the [quickstart](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/quickstart)
 or [getting started](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/getting-started)
 chapters of the guide.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/#usage)
Usage

Depend on `rocket` in `Cargo.toml`:

    [dependencies]
    rocket = "0.5.1"

Note that development versions, tagged with `-dev`, are not published and need to be specified as [git dependencies](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/specifying-dependencies.html#specifying-dependencies-from-git-repositories)
.

See the [guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide)
 for more information on how to write Rocket applications. Here’s a simple example to get you started:

    #[macro_use] extern crate rocket;
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn hello() -> &'static str {
        "Hello, world!"
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().mount("/", routes![hello])
    }

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/#features)
Features

To avoid compiling unused dependencies, Rocket gates certain features. With the exception of `http2`, all are disabled by default:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `secrets` | Support for authenticated, encrypted [private cookies](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/requests/#private-cookies)<br>. |
| `tls` | Support for [TLS](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/#tls)<br> encrypted connections. |
| `mtls` | Support for verified clients via [mutual TLS](https://api.rocket.rs/mtls/index.html "mod rocket::mtls")<br>. |
| `http2` | Support for HTTP/2 (enabled by default). |
| `json` | Support for [JSON (de)serialization](https://api.rocket.rs/serde/json/index.html "mod rocket::serde::json")<br>. |
| `msgpack` | Support for [MessagePack (de)serialization](https://api.rocket.rs/serde/msgpack/index.html "mod rocket::serde::msgpack")<br>. |
| `uuid` | Support for [UUID value parsing and (de)serialization](https://api.rocket.rs/serde/uuid/index.html "mod rocket::serde::uuid")<br>. |

Disabled features can be selectively enabled in `Cargo.toml`:

    [dependencies]
    rocket = { version = "0.5.1", features = ["secrets", "tls", "json"] }

Conversely, HTTP/2 can be disabled:

    [dependencies]
    rocket = { version = "0.5.1", default-features = false }

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/#configuration)
Configuration

Rocket offers a rich, extensible configuration system built on [Figment](https://docs.rs/figment)
. By default, Rocket applications are configured via a `Rocket.toml` file and/or `ROCKET_{PARAM}` environment variables, but applications may configure their own sources. See the [configuration guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/)
 for full details.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/#testing)
Testing

The [`local`](https://api.rocket.rs/local/index.html "mod rocket::local")
 module contains structures that facilitate unit and integration testing of a Rocket application. The top-level [`local`](https://api.rocket.rs/local/index.html "mod rocket::local")
 module documentation and the [testing guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/testing/#testing)
 include detailed examples.

Re-exports[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#reexports)

------------------------------------------------

`pub use [futures](https://docs.rs/futures/0.3.31/futures/index.html "mod futures") ;`

`pub use [tokio](https://docs.rs/tokio/1.48.0/tokio/index.html "mod tokio") ;`

`pub use [figment](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/index.html "mod figment") ;`

`pub use [time](https://docs.rs/time/0.3.44/time/index.html "mod time") ;`

Modules[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#modules)

-------------------------------------------

[catcher](https://api.rocket.rs/catcher/index.html "mod rocket::catcher")

Types and traits for error catchers and their handlers and return types.

[config](https://api.rocket.rs/config/index.html "mod rocket::config")

Server and application configuration.

[data](https://api.rocket.rs/data/index.html "mod rocket::data")

Types and traits for handling incoming body data.

[error](https://api.rocket.rs/error/index.html "mod rocket::error")

Types representing various errors that can occur in a Rocket application.

[fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/fairing/index.html "mod rocket::fairing")

Fairings: callbacks at launch, liftoff, request, and response time.

[form](https://api.rocket.rs/form/index.html "mod rocket::form")

Parsing and validation of HTTP forms and fields.

[fs](https://api.rocket.rs/fs/index.html "mod rocket::fs")

File serving, file accepting, and file metadata types.

[http](https://api.rocket.rs/http/index.html "mod rocket::http")

Types that map to concepts in HTTP.

[local](https://api.rocket.rs/local/index.html "mod rocket::local")

Structures for local dispatching of requests, primarily for testing.

[mtls](https://api.rocket.rs/mtls/index.html "mod rocket::mtls")
`mtls`

Support for mutual TLS client certificates.

[outcome](https://api.rocket.rs/outcome/index.html "mod rocket::outcome")

Success, error, and forward handling.

[request](https://api.rocket.rs/request/index.html "mod rocket::request")

Types and traits for request parsing and handling.

[response](https://api.rocket.rs/response/index.html "mod rocket::response")

Types and traits to build and send responses.

[route](https://api.rocket.rs/route/index.html "mod rocket::route")

Types and traits for routes and their request handlers and return types.

[serde](https://api.rocket.rs/serde/index.html "mod rocket::serde")

Serialization and deserialization support.

[shield](https://api.rocket.rs/shield/index.html "mod rocket::shield")

Security and privacy headers for all outgoing responses.

Macros[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#macros)

-----------------------------------------

[catchers](https://api.rocket.rs/macro.catchers.html "macro rocket::catchers")

Generates a `Vec` of [`Catcher`](https://api.rocket.rs/rocket/struct.Catcher.html)
s from a set of catcher paths.

[routes](https://api.rocket.rs/macro.routes.html "macro rocket::routes")

Generates a `Vec` of [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/rocket/struct.Route.html)
s from a set of route paths.

[uri](https://api.rocket.rs/macro.uri.html "macro rocket::uri")

Type-safe, encoding-safe route and non-route URI generation.

Structs[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#structs)

-------------------------------------------

[Catcher](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Catcher.html "struct rocket::Catcher")

An error catching route.

[Config](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Rocket server configuration.

[Data](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Data.html "struct rocket::Data")

Type representing the body data of a request.

[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Error.html "struct rocket::Error")

An error that occurs during launch.

[Request](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Request.html "struct rocket::Request")

The type of an incoming web request.

[Response](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Response.html "struct rocket::Response")

A response, as returned by types implementing [`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
.

[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")

The application server itself.

[Route](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::Route")

A request handling route.

[Shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::Shutdown")

A request guard and future for graceful shutdown.

[State](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.State.html "struct rocket::State")

Request guard to retrieve managed state.

Enums[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#enums)

---------------------------------------

[Build](https://api.rocket.rs/enum.Build.html "enum rocket::Build")

The initial launch [`Phase`](https://api.rocket.rs/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::Phase")
. See [Rocket#build](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html#build "struct rocket::Rocket")
 for phase details.

[Ignite](https://api.rocket.rs/enum.Ignite.html "enum rocket::Ignite")

The second launch [`Phase`](https://api.rocket.rs/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::Phase")
: post-build but pre-orbit. See [Rocket#ignite](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html#ignite "struct rocket::Rocket")
 for details.

[Orbit](https://api.rocket.rs/enum.Orbit.html "enum rocket::Orbit")

The final launch [`Phase`](https://api.rocket.rs/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::Phase")
. See [Rocket#orbit](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html#orbit "struct rocket::Rocket")
 for details.

Traits[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#traits)

-----------------------------------------

[Phase](https://api.rocket.rs/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::Phase")

A marker trait for Rocket’s launch phases.

[Sentinel](https://api.rocket.rs/trait.Sentinel.html "trait rocket::Sentinel")

An automatic last line of defense against launching an invalid [`Rocket`](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
.

Functions[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#functions)

-----------------------------------------------

[build](https://api.rocket.rs/fn.build.html "fn rocket::build")

Creates a [`Rocket`](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
 instance with the default config provider: aliases [`Rocket::build()`](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html#method.build "associated function rocket::Rocket::build")
.

[custom](https://api.rocket.rs/fn.custom.html "fn rocket::custom")

Creates a [`Rocket`](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
 instance with a custom config provider: aliases [`Rocket::custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/struct.Rocket.html#method.custom "associated function rocket::Rocket::custom")
.

[execute](https://api.rocket.rs/fn.execute.html "fn rocket::execute")

Executes a `future` to completion on a new tokio-based Rocket async runtime.

Attribute Macros[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#attributes)

-------------------------------------------------------

[async\_test](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.async_test.html "attr rocket::async_test")

Retrofits supports for `async fn` in unit tests.

[async\_trait](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.async_trait.html "attr rocket::async_trait")

Retrofits support for `async fn` in trait impls and declarations.

[catch](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.catch.html "attr rocket::catch")

Attribute to generate a [`Catcher`](https://api.rocket.rs/rocket/struct.Catcher.html)
 and associated metadata.

[delete](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.delete.html "attr rocket::delete")

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

[get](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.get.html "attr rocket::get")

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

[head](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.head.html "attr rocket::head")

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

[launch](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.launch.html "attr rocket::launch")

Generates a `main` function that launches a returned `Rocket<Build>`.

[main](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.main.html "attr rocket::main")

Retrofits `async fn` support in `main` functions.

[options](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.options.html "attr rocket::options")

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

[patch](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.patch.html "attr rocket::patch")

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

[post](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.post.html "attr rocket::post")

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

[put](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.put.html "attr rocket::put")

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

[route](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html "attr rocket::route")

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

Derive Macros[§](https://api.rocket.rs/#derives)

-------------------------------------------------

[FromForm](https://api.rocket.rs/derive.FromForm.html "derive rocket::FromForm")

Derive for the [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html)
 trait.

[FromFormField](https://api.rocket.rs/derive.FromFormField.html "derive rocket::FromFormField")

Derive for the [`FromFormField`](https://api.rocket.rs/rocket/form/trait.FromFormField.html)
 trait.

[Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/derive.Responder.html "derive rocket::Responder")

Derive for the [`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html)
 trait.

[UriDisplayPath](https://api.rocket.rs/derive.UriDisplayPath.html "derive rocket::UriDisplayPath")

Derive for the [`UriDisplay<Path>`](https://api.rocket.rs/rocket/http/uri/fmt/trait.UriDisplay.html)
 trait.

[UriDisplayQuery](https://api.rocket.rs/derive.UriDisplayQuery.html "derive rocket::UriDisplayQuery")

Derive for the [`UriDisplay<Query>`](https://api.rocket.rs/rocket/http/uri/fmt/trait.UriDisplay.html)
 trait.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/?search=#rocket---core-api-documentation)
Rocket - Core API Documentation
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hello, and welcome to the core Rocket API documentation!

This API documentation is highly technical and is purely a reference. There’s an [overview](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/overview)
 of Rocket on the main site as well as a [full, detailed guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide)
. If you’d like pointers on getting started, see the [quickstart](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/quickstart)
 or [getting started](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/getting-started)
 chapters of the guide.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/?search=#usage)
Usage

Depend on `rocket` in `Cargo.toml`:

    [dependencies]
    rocket = "0.5.1"

Note that development versions, tagged with `-dev`, are not published and need to be specified as [git dependencies](https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/specifying-dependencies.html#specifying-dependencies-from-git-repositories)
.

See the [guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide)
 for more information on how to write Rocket applications. Here’s a simple example to get you started:

    #[macro_use] extern crate rocket;
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn hello() -> &'static str {
        "Hello, world!"
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().mount("/", routes![hello])
    }

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/?search=#features)
Features

To avoid compiling unused dependencies, Rocket gates certain features. With the exception of `http2`, all are disabled by default:

| Feature | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `secrets` | Support for authenticated, encrypted [private cookies](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/requests/#private-cookies)<br>. |
| `tls` | Support for [TLS](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/#tls)<br> encrypted connections. |
| `mtls` | Support for verified clients via [mutual TLS](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/index.html "mod rocket::mtls")<br>. |
| `http2` | Support for HTTP/2 (enabled by default). |
| `json` | Support for [JSON (de)serialization](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/json/index.html "mod rocket::serde::json")<br>. |
| `msgpack` | Support for [MessagePack (de)serialization](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/index.html "mod rocket::serde::msgpack")<br>. |
| `uuid` | Support for [UUID value parsing and (de)serialization](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/index.html "mod rocket::serde::uuid")<br>. |

Disabled features can be selectively enabled in `Cargo.toml`:

    [dependencies]
    rocket = { version = "0.5.1", features = ["secrets", "tls", "json"] }

Conversely, HTTP/2 can be disabled:

    [dependencies]
    rocket = { version = "0.5.1", default-features = false }

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/?search=#configuration)
Configuration

Rocket offers a rich, extensible configuration system built on [Figment](https://docs.rs/figment)
. By default, Rocket applications are configured via a `Rocket.toml` file and/or `ROCKET_{PARAM}` environment variables, but applications may configure their own sources. See the [configuration guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/)
 for full details.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/?search=#testing)
Testing

The [`local`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/index.html "mod rocket::local")
 module contains structures that facilitate unit and integration testing of a Rocket application. The top-level [`local`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/index.html "mod rocket::local")
 module documentation and the [testing guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/testing/#testing)
 include detailed examples.

`pub use [tokio](https://docs.rs/tokio/1.48.0/tokio/index.html "mod tokio") ;`

[response](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/index.html "mod rocket::response")

Types and traits for routes and their request handlers and return types.

 for details.

# rocket_dyn_templates - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Crate rocket\_dyn\_templates](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/all.html "show sidebar")

Crate rocket\_dyn\_templates Copy item path
===========================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/lib.rs.html#1-209)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

Dynamic templating engine support for Rocket.

This crate adds support for dynamic template rendering to Rocket. It automatically discovers templates, provides a `Responder` to render templates, and automatically reloads templates when compiled in debug mode. At present, it supports [Handlebars](https://docs.rs/crate/handlebars/5)
 and [Tera](https://docs.rs/crate/tera/1)
.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#usage)
Usage
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Depend on `rocket_dyn_templates`. Enable the feature(s) corresponding to your templating engine(s) of choice:
    
        [dependencies.rocket_dyn_templates]
        version = "0.2.0"
        features = ["handlebars", "tera", "minijinja"]
    
2.  Write your templates inside of the [configurable](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#configuration)
     `${ROCKET_ROOT}/templates`. The filename _must_ end with an extension corresponding to an enabled engine. The second-to-last extension should correspond to the file’s type:
    
    | Engine | Extension | Example |
    | --- | --- | --- |
    | [Tera](https://docs.rs/crate/tera/1) | `.tera` | `${ROCKET_ROOT}/templates/index.html.tera` |
    | [Handlebars](https://docs.rs/crate/handlebars/5) | `.hbs` | `${ROCKET_ROOT}/templates/index.html.hbs` |
    | [MiniJinja](https://docs.rs/minijinja/2) | `.j2` | `${ROCKET_ROOT}/templates/index.html.j2` |
    
3.  Attach `Template::fairing()` and return a [`Template`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Template")
     from your routes via [`Template::render()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.render "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::render")
    , supplying the name of the template file **minus the last two extensions**:
    
        use rocket_dyn_templates::{Template, context};
        
        #[get("/")]
        fn index() -> Template {
            Template::render("index", context! { field: "value" })
        }
        
        #[launch]
        fn rocket() -> _ {
            rocket::build().attach(Template::fairing())
        }
    

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#configuration)
Configuration

This crate reads one configuration parameter from the configured figment:

*   `template_dir` (**default: `templates/`**)
    
    A path to a directory to search for template files in. Relative paths are considered relative to the configuration file, or there is no file, the current working directory.
    

For example, to change the default and set `template_dir` to different values based on whether the application was compiled for debug or release from a `Rocket.toml` file (read by the default figment), you might write:

    [debug]
    template_dir = "static/templates"
    
    [release]
    template_dir = "/var/opt/www/templates"

**Note:** `template_dir` defaults to `templates/`. It _does not_ need to be specified if the default suffices.

See the [configuration chapter](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration)
 of the guide for more information on configuration.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#template-naming-and-content-types)
Template Naming and Content-Types

Templates are rendered by _name_ via [`Template::render()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.render "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::render")
, which returns a [`Template`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Template")
 responder. The _name_ of the template is the path to the template file, relative to `template_dir`, minus at most two extensions.

The `Content-Type` of the response is automatically determined by the non-engine extension using [`ContentType::from_extension()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html#method.from_extension)
. If there is no such extension or it is unknown, `text/plain` is used.

The following table contains examples:

| template path | [`Template::render()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.render "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::render")<br> call | content-type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| {template\_dir}/index.html.hbs | `render("index")` | HTML |
| {template\_dir}/index.tera | `render("index")` | `text/plain` |
| {template\_dir}/index.hbs | `render("index")` | `text/plain` |
| {template\_dir}/dir/index.hbs | `render("dir/index")` | `text/plain` |
| {template\_dir}/dir/data.json.tera | `render("dir/data")` | JSON |
| {template\_dir}/data.template.xml.hbs | `render("data.template")` | XML |
| {template\_dir}/subdir/index.template.html.hbs | `render("subdir/index.template")` | HTML |

The recommended naming scheme is to use two extensions: one for the file type, and one for the template extension. This means that template extensions should look like: `.html.hbs`, `.html.tera`, `.xml.hbs`, and so on.

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#rendering-context)
Rendering Context

In addition to a name, [`Template::render()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.render "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::render")
 requires a context to use during rendering. The context can be any \[`Serialize`\] type that serializes to an `Object` (a dictionary) value. The [`context!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/macro.context.html "macro rocket_dyn_templates::context")
 macro can be used to create inline `Serialize`\-able context objects.

    use rocket::serde::Serialize;
    use rocket_dyn_templates::{Template, context};
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn index() -> Template {
        // Using the `context! { }` macro.
        Template::render("index", context! {
            site_name: "Rocket - Home Page",
            version: 127,
        })
    }
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn index2() -> Template {
        #[derive(Serialize)]
        #[serde(crate = "rocket::serde")]
        struct IndexContext {
            site_name: &'static str,
            version: u8
        }
    
        // Using an existing `IndexContext`, which implements `Serialize`.
        Template::render("index", IndexContext {
            site_name: "Rocket - Home Page",
            version: 127,
        })
    }

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#discovery-automatic-reloads-and-engine-customization)
Discovery, Automatic Reloads, and Engine Customization

As long as one of [`Template::fairing()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.fairing "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::fairing")
, [`Template::custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.custom "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::custom")
, or [`Template::try_custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.try_custom "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::try_custom")
 is [attached](rocket::attach())
, any file in the configured `template_dir` ending with a known engine extension (as described in the [usage section](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#usage)
) can be rendered. The latter two fairings allow customizations such as registering helpers and templates from strings.

_**Note:** Templates that are registered directly via [`Template::custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.custom "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::custom")
, use whatever name provided during that registration; no extensions are automatically removed._

In debug mode (without the `--release` flag passed to `cargo`), templates are **automatically reloaded** from disk when changes are made. In release builds, template reloading is disabled to improve performance and cannot be enabled.

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#metadata-and-rendering-to-string)
Metadata and Rendering to `String`

The [`Metadata`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Metadata")
 request guard allows dynamically querying templating metadata, such as whether a template is known to exist ([`Metadata::contains_template()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata.html#method.contains_template "method rocket_dyn_templates::Metadata::contains_template")
), and to render templates to `String` ([`Metadata::render()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata.html#method.render "method rocket_dyn_templates::Metadata::render")
).

Modules[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[handlebars](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/handlebars/index.html "mod rocket_dyn_templates::handlebars")

The handlebars templating engine library, reexported.

[minijinja](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/index.html "mod rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja")

The minijinja templating engine library, reexported.

[tera](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/tera/index.html "mod rocket_dyn_templates::tera")

The tera templating engine library, reexported.

Macros[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html#macros)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# rocket_sync_db_pools - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Crate rocket\_sync\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/all.html "show sidebar")

Crate rocket\_sync\_db\_pools Copy item path
============================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/lib.rs.html#1-596)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

Traits, utilities, and a macro for easy database connection pooling.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#overview)
Overview
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This crate provides traits, utilities, and a procedural macro for configuring and accessing database connection pools in Rocket. A _database connection pool_ is a data structure that maintains active database connections for later use in the application. This implementation is backed by [`r2d2`](https://docs.rs/r2d2/0.8/r2d2/index.html "mod r2d2")
 and exposes connections through request guards.

Databases are individually configured through Rocket’s regular configuration mechanisms. Connecting a Rocket application to a database using this library occurs in three simple steps:

1.  Configure your databases in `Rocket.toml`. (see [Configuration](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#configuration)
    )
2.  Associate a request guard type and fairing with each database. (see [Guard Types](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#guard-types)
    )
3.  Use the request guard to retrieve a connection in a handler. (see [Handlers](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#handlers)
    )

For a list of supported databases, see [Provided Databases](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#provided)
. This support can be easily extended by implementing the [`Poolable`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable.html "trait rocket_sync_db_pools::Poolable")
 trait. See [Extending](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#extending)
 for more.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#example)
Example

Before using this library, the feature corresponding to your database type in `rocket_sync_db_pools` must be enabled:

    [dependencies.rocket_sync_db_pools]
    version = "0.1.0"
    features = ["diesel_sqlite_pool"]

See [Provided](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#provided)
 for a list of supported database and their associated feature name.

In whichever configuration source you choose, configure a `databases` dictionary with an internal dictionary for each database, here `sqlite_logs` in a TOML source:

    [default.databases]
    sqlite_logs = { url = "/path/to/database.sqlite" }

In your application’s source code, one-time:

    use rocket_sync_db_pools::{database, diesel};
    
    #[database("sqlite_logs")]
    struct LogsDbConn(diesel::SqliteConnection);
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().attach(LogsDbConn::fairing())
    }

Whenever a connection to the database is needed:

    #[get("/logs/<id>")]
    async fn get_logs(conn: LogsDbConn, id: usize) -> Result<Logs> {
        conn.run(|c| Logs::by_id(c, id)).await
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#usage)
Usage
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#configuration)
Configuration

Databases can be configured as any other values. Using the default configuration provider, either via `Rocket.toml` or environment variables. You can also use a custom provider.

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#rockettoml)
`Rocket.toml`

To configure a database via `Rocket.toml`, add a table for each database to the `databases` table where the key is a name of your choice. The table should have a `url` key and, optionally, `pool_size` and `timeout` keys. This looks as follows:

    # Option 1:
    [global.databases]
    sqlite_db = { url = "db.sqlite" }
    
    # Option 2:
    [global.databases.my_db]
    url = "postgres://root:root@localhost/my_db"
    
    # With `pool_size` and `timeout` keys:
    [global.databases.sqlite_db]
    url = "db.sqlite"
    pool_size = 20
    timeout = 5

The table _requires_ one key:

*   `url` - the URl to the database

Additionally, all configurations accept the following _optional_ keys:

*   `pool_size` - the size of the pool, i.e., the number of connections to pool (defaults to the configured number of workers \* 4)
*   `timeout` - max number of seconds to wait for a connection to become available (defaults to `5`)

Additional options may be required or supported by other adapters.

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#procedurally)
Procedurally

Databases can also be configured procedurally via `rocket::custom()`. The example below does just this:

    use rocket::figment::{value::{Map, Value}, util::map};
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        let db: Map<_, Value> = map! {
            "url" => "db.sqlite".into(),
            "pool_size" => 10.into(),
            "timeout" => 5.into(),
        };
    
        let figment = rocket::Config::figment()
            .merge(("databases", map!["my_db" => db]));
    
        rocket::custom(figment)
    }

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#environment-variables)
Environment Variables

Lastly, databases can be configured via environment variables by specifying the `databases` table as detailed in the [Environment Variables configuration guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/#environment-variables)
:

    ROCKET_DATABASES='{my_db={url="db.sqlite"}}'

Multiple databases can be specified in the `ROCKET_DATABASES` environment variable as well by comma separating them:

    ROCKET_DATABASES='{my_db={url="db.sqlite"},my_pg_db={url="postgres://root:root@localhost/my_pg_db"}}'

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#guard-types)
Guard Types

Once a database has been configured, the `#[database]` attribute can be used to tie a type in your application to a configured database. The database attribute accepts a single string parameter that indicates the name of the database. This corresponds to the database name set as the database’s configuration key.

See [`ExampleDb`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb.html "struct rocket_sync_db_pools::example::ExampleDb")
 for everything that the macro generates. Specifically, it generates:

*   A [`FromRequest`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/from_request/trait.FromRequest.html "trait rocket::request::from_request::FromRequest")
     implementation for the decorated type.
*   A [`Sentinel`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/sentinel/trait.Sentinel.html "trait rocket::sentinel::Sentinel")
     implementation for the decorated type.
*   A [`fairing()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb.html#method.fairing "associated function rocket_sync_db_pools::example::ExampleDb::fairing")
     method to initialize the database.
*   A [`run()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb.html#method.run "method rocket_sync_db_pools::example::ExampleDb::run")
     method to execute blocking database operations in an `async`\-safe manner.
*   A [`pool()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb.html#method.pool "associated function rocket_sync_db_pools::example::ExampleDb::pool")
     method to retrieve the backing connection pool.

The attribute can only be applied to tuple structs with one field. The internal type of the structure must implement [`Poolable`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable.html "trait rocket_sync_db_pools::Poolable")
.

    use rocket_sync_db_pools::diesel;
    
    #[database("my_db")]
    struct MyDatabase(diesel::SqliteConnection);

Other databases can be used by specifying their respective [`Poolable`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable.html "trait rocket_sync_db_pools::Poolable")
 type:

    use rocket_sync_db_pools::postgres;
    
    #[database("my_pg_db")]
    struct MyPgDatabase(postgres::Client);

The fairing returned from the generated `fairing()` method _must_ be attached for the request guard implementation to succeed. Putting the pieces together, a use of the `#[database]` attribute looks as follows:

    use rocket_sync_db_pools::diesel;
    
    #[database("my_db")]
    struct MyDatabase(diesel::SqliteConnection);
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::custom(figment).attach(MyDatabase::fairing())
    }

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#handlers)
Handlers

Finally, use your type as a request guard in a handler to retrieve a connection wrapper for the database:

    #[database("my_db")]
    struct MyDatabase(diesel::SqliteConnection);
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn my_handler(conn: MyDatabase) {
        // ...
    }

A connection can be retrieved and used with the `run()` method:

    #[database("my_db")]
    struct MyDatabase(diesel::SqliteConnection);
    
    fn load_from_db(conn: &diesel::SqliteConnection) -> Data {
        // Do something with connection, return some data.
    }
    
    #[get("/")]
    async fn my_handler(mut conn: MyDatabase) -> Data {
        conn.run(|c| load_from_db(c)).await
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#database-support)
Database Support
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Built-in support is provided for many popular databases and drivers. Support can be easily extended by [`Poolable`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable.html "trait rocket_sync_db_pools::Poolable")
 implementations.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#provided)
Provided

The list below includes all presently supported database adapters and their corresponding [`Poolable`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable.html "trait rocket_sync_db_pools::Poolable")
 type.

| Kind | Driver | Version | `Poolable` Type | Feature |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Sqlite | [Diesel](https://diesel.rs/) | `2` | [`diesel::SqliteConnection`](https://docs.rs/diesel/2/diesel/sqlite/struct.SqliteConnection.html) | `diesel_sqlite_pool` |
| Postgres | [Diesel](https://diesel.rs/) | `2` | [`diesel::PgConnection`](https://docs.rs/diesel/2/diesel/pg/struct.PgConnection.html) | `diesel_postgres_pool` |
| MySQL | [Diesel](https://diesel.rs/) | `2` | [`diesel::MysqlConnection`](https://docs.rs/diesel/2/diesel/mysql/struct.MysqlConnection.html) | `diesel_mysql_pool` |
| Postgres | [Rust-Postgres](https://github.com/sfackler/rust-postgres) | `0.19` | [`postgres::Client`](https://docs.rs/postgres/0.19/postgres/struct.Client.html) | `postgres_pool` |
| Sqlite | [`Rusqlite`](https://github.com/jgallagher/rusqlite) | `0.27` | [`rusqlite::Connection`](https://docs.rs/rusqlite/0.27/rusqlite/struct.Connection.html) | `sqlite_pool` |
| Memcache | [`memcache`](https://github.com/aisk/rust-memcache) | `0.15` | [`memcache::Client`](https://docs.rs/memcache/0.15/memcache/struct.Client.html) | `memcache_pool` |

The above table lists all the supported database adapters in this library. In order to use particular `Poolable` type that’s included in this library, you must first enable the feature listed in the “Feature” column. The interior type of your decorated database type should match the type in the “`Poolable` Type” column.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#extending)
Extending

Extending Rocket’s support to your own custom database adapter (or other database-like struct that can be pooled by `r2d2`) is as easy as implementing the [`Poolable`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable.html "trait rocket_sync_db_pools::Poolable")
 trait. See the documentation for [`Poolable`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable.html "trait rocket_sync_db_pools::Poolable")
 for more details on how to implement it.

Re-exports[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#reexports)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use [diesel](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/index.html "mod diesel") ;``diesel_sqlite_pool` or `diesel_postgres_pool` or `diesel_mysql_pool`

`pub use [postgres](https://docs.rs/postgres/0.19.12/postgres/index.html "mod postgres") ;``postgres_pool`

`pub use [r2d2_postgres](https://docs.rs/r2d2_postgres/0.18.2/r2d2_postgres/index.html "mod r2d2_postgres") ;``postgres_pool`

`pub use [rusqlite](https://docs.rs/rusqlite/0.29.0/rusqlite/index.html "mod rusqlite") ;``sqlite_pool`

`pub use [r2d2_sqlite](https://docs.rs/r2d2_sqlite/0.22.0/r2d2_sqlite/index.html "mod r2d2_sqlite") ;``sqlite_pool`

`pub use [memcache](https://docs.rs/memcache/0.15.2/memcache/index.html "mod memcache") ;``memcache_pool`

`pub use [r2d2_memcache](https://docs.rs/r2d2-memcache/0.6.0/r2d2_memcache/index.html "mod r2d2_memcache") ;``memcache_pool`

`pub use [r2d2](https://docs.rs/r2d2/0.8/r2d2/index.html "mod r2d2") ;`

Modules[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html#modules)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# rocket::serde::msgpack - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Module msgpack](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[serde](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/index.html)

Module msgpack Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/serde/msgpack.rs.html#1-356)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Available on **crate feature `msgpack`** only.

Expand description

Automatic MessagePack (de)serialization support.

See [`MsgPack`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/struct.MsgPack.html "struct rocket::serde::msgpack::MsgPack")
 for further details.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/index.html#enabling)
Enabling
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This module is only available when the `msgpack` feature is enabled. Enable it in `Cargo.toml` as follows:

    [dependencies.rocket]
    version = "0.5.1"
    features = ["msgpack"]

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/index.html#testing)
Testing
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [`LocalRequest`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/blocking/struct.LocalRequest.html "struct rocket::local::blocking::LocalRequest")
 and [`LocalResponse`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/blocking/struct.LocalResponse.html "struct rocket::local::blocking::LocalResponse")
 types provide [`msgpack()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/blocking/struct.LocalRequest.html#method.msgpack "method rocket::local::blocking::LocalRequest::msgpack")
 and [`into_msgpack()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/blocking/struct.LocalResponse.html#method.into_msgpack "method rocket::local::blocking::LocalResponse::into_msgpack")
 methods to create a request with serialized MessagePack and deserialize a response as MessagePack, respectively.

Structs[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/index.html#structs)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The MessagePack guard: easily consume and return MessagePack.

Enums[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/index.html#enums)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/enum.Error.html "enum rocket::serde::msgpack::Error")

Enum representing errors that can occur while decoding MessagePack data.

Functions[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/index.html#functions)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[from\_slice](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/fn.from_slice.html "fn rocket::serde::msgpack::from_slice")

Deserialize an instance of type `T` from MessagePack encoded bytes.

[to\_compact\_vec](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/fn.to_compact_vec.html "fn rocket::serde::msgpack::to_compact_vec")

Serialize a `T` into a MessagePack byte vector with compact representation.

# rocket_db_pools - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Crate rocket\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/all.html "show sidebar")

Crate rocket\_db\_pools Copy item path
======================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/lib.rs.html#1-262)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

Asynchronous database driver connection pooling integration for Rocket.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#quickstart)
Quickstart
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Add `rocket_db_pools` as a dependency with one or more [database driver features](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#supported-drivers)
     enabled:
    
        [dependencies.rocket_db_pools]
        version = "0.2.0"
        features = ["sqlx_sqlite"]
    
2.  Choose a name for your database, here `sqlite_logs`. [Configure](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#configuration)
     _at least_ a URL for the database:
    
        [default.databases.sqlite_logs]
        url = "/path/to/database.sqlite"
    
3.  [Derive](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/derive.Database.html "derive rocket_db_pools::Database")
     [`Database`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Database")
     for a unit type (`Logs` here) which wraps the selected driver’s [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")
     type (see [the driver table](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#supported-drivers)
    ) and is decorated with `#[database("name")]`. Attach `Type::init()` to your application’s `Rocket` to initialize the database pool:
    
        use rocket_db_pools::{sqlx, Database};
        
        #[derive(Database)]
        #[database("sqlite_logs")]
        struct Logs(sqlx::SqlitePool);
        
        #[launch]
        fn rocket() -> _ {
            rocket::build().attach(Logs::init())
        }
    
4.  Use [`Connection<Type>`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Connection")
     as a request guard to retrieve an active database connection, which dereferences to the native type in the [`Connection` deref](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#supported-drivers)
     column.
    
        use rocket_db_pools::Connection;
        use rocket_db_pools::sqlx::Row;
        
        #[get("/<id>")]
        async fn read(mut db: Connection<Logs>, id: i64) -> Option<Log> {
            sqlx::query("SELECT content FROM logs WHERE id = ?").bind(id)
                .fetch_one(&mut **db).await
                .and_then(|r| Ok(Log(r.try_get(0)?)))
                .ok()
        }
    
    Alternatively, use a reference to the database type as a request guard to retrieve the entire pool, but note that unlike retrieving a `Connection`, doing so does _not_ guarantee that a connection is available:
    
        use rocket_db_pools::sqlx::Row;
        
        #[get("/<id>")]
        async fn read(db: &Logs, id: i64) -> Option<Log> {
            sqlx::query("SELECT content FROM logs WHERE id = ?").bind(id)
                .fetch_one(&db.0).await
                .and_then(|r| Ok(Log(r.try_get(0)?)))
                .ok()
        }
    

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#supported-drivers)
Supported Drivers
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

At present, this crate supports _four_ drivers: [`deadpool`](https://docs.rs/deadpool/0.12.3/deadpool/index.html "mod deadpool")
, [`sqlx`](https://docs.rs/sqlx/0.7.2/sqlx/index.html "mod sqlx")
, [`mongodb`](https://docs.rs/mongodb/2.8.2/mongodb/index.html "mod mongodb")
, and [`diesel`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/index.html "mod rocket_db_pools::diesel")
. Each driver may support multiple databases. Drivers have a varying degree of support for graceful shutdown, affected by the `Type::init()` fairing on Rocket shutdown.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#deadpool-v013)
`deadpool` (v0.13)

| Database | Feature | [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")<br> Type | [`Connection`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Connection")<br> Deref |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Postgres | `deadpool_postgres` (v0.13) | [`deadpool_postgres::Pool`](https://docs.rs/deadpool-postgres/0.13.2/deadpool_postgres/type.Pool.html "type deadpool_postgres::Pool") | [`deadpool_postgres::ClientWrapper`](https://docs.rs/deadpool-postgres/0.13.2/deadpool_postgres/struct.ClientWrapper.html "struct deadpool_postgres::ClientWrapper") |
| Redis | `deadpool_redis` (v0.15) | [`deadpool_redis::Pool`](https://docs.rs/deadpool-redis/0.15.1/deadpool_redis/type.Pool.html "type deadpool_redis::Pool") | [`deadpool_redis::Connection`](https://docs.rs/deadpool-redis/0.15.1/deadpool_redis/struct.Connection.html "struct deadpool_redis::Connection") |

On shutdown, new connections are denied. Shutdown _does not_ wait for connections to be returned.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#sqlx-v07)
`sqlx` (v0.7)

| Database | Feature | [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")<br> Type | [`Connection`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Connection")<br> Deref |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Postgres | `sqlx_postgres` | [`sqlx::PgPool`](https://docs.rs/sqlx/0.6/sqlx/type.PgPool.html) | [`sqlx::pool::PoolConnection<Postgres>`](https://docs.rs/sqlx/0.6/sqlx/pool/struct.PoolConnection.html) |
| MySQL | `sqlx_mysql` | [`sqlx::MySqlPool`](https://docs.rs/sqlx/0.6/sqlx/type.MySqlPool.html) | [`sqlx::pool::PoolConnection<MySql>`](https://docs.rs/sqlx/0.6/sqlx/pool/struct.PoolConnection.html) |
| SQLite | `sqlx_sqlite` | [`sqlx::SqlitePool`](https://docs.rs/sqlx/0.6/sqlx/type.SqlitePool.html) | [`sqlx::pool::PoolConnection<Sqlite>`](https://docs.rs/sqlx/0.6/sqlx/pool/struct.PoolConnection.html) |

On shutdown, new connections are denied. Shutdown waits for connections to be returned.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#mongodb-v2)
`mongodb` (v2)

| Database | Feature | [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")<br> Type and [`Connection`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Connection")<br> Deref |
| --- | --- | --- |
| MongoDB | `mongodb` | [`mongodb::Client`](https://docs.rs/mongodb/2.8.2/mongodb/client/struct.Client.html "struct mongodb::client::Client") |

Graceful shutdown is not supported.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#diesel-v2)
`diesel` (v2)

| Database | Feature | [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")<br> Type | [`Connection`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Connection")<br> Deref |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Postgres | `diesel_postgres` | [`diesel::PgPool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/type.PgPool.html "type rocket_db_pools::diesel::PgPool") | [`diesel::AsyncPgConnection`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/struct.AsyncPgConnection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::diesel::AsyncPgConnection") |
| MySQL | `diesel_mysql` | [`diesel::MysqlPool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/type.MysqlPool.html "type rocket_db_pools::diesel::MysqlPool") | [`diesel::AsyncMysqlConnection`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/struct.AsyncMysqlConnection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::diesel::AsyncMysqlConnection") |

See [`diesel`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/index.html "mod rocket_db_pools::diesel")
 for usage details.

On shutdown, new connections are denied. Shutdown _does not_ wait for connections to be returned.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#enabling-additional-driver-features)
Enabling Additional Driver Features

Only the minimal features for each driver crate are enabled by `rocket_db_pools`. To use additional driver functionality exposed via its crate’s features, you’ll need to depend on the crate directly with those features enabled in `Cargo.toml`:

    [dependencies.sqlx]
    version = "0.7"
    default-features = false
    features = ["macros", "migrate"]
    
    [dependencies.rocket_db_pools]
    version = "0.2.0"
    features = ["sqlx_sqlite"]

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#configuration)
Configuration
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Configuration for a database named `db_name` is deserialized from a `databases.db_name` configuration parameter into a [`Config`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Config")
 structure via Rocket’s [configuration facilities](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/index.html "mod rocket::config")
. By default, configuration can be provided in `Rocket.toml`:

    [default.databases.db_name]
    url = "db.sqlite"
    
    # Only `url` is required. These have sane defaults and are optional.
    min_connections = 64
    max_connections = 1024
    connect_timeout = 5
    idle_timeout = 120
    
    # This option is only supported by the `sqlx_sqlite` driver.
    extensions = ["memvfs", "rot13"]

Or via environment variables:

    ROCKET_DATABASES='{db_name={url="db.sqlite",idle_timeout=120}}'

See [`Config`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Config")
 for details on configuration parameters.

**Note:** `deadpool` and `diesel` drivers do not support and thus ignore the `min_connections` value.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#driver-defaults)
Driver Defaults

Some drivers provide configuration defaults different from the underlying database’s defaults. A best-effort attempt is made to document those differences below:

*   `sqlx_sqlite`
    
    *   foreign keys : `enabled`
    *   journal mode : `WAL`
    *   create-missing : `enabled`
    *   synchronous : `full` (even when `WAL`)
    *   busy timeout : `connection_timeout`
*   `sqlx_postgres`
    
    *   sslmode : `prefer`
    *   statement-cache-capacity : `100`
    *   user : result of `whoami`
*   `sqlx_mysql`
    
    *   sslmode : `PREFERRED`
    *   statement-cache-capacity : `100`

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#extending)
Extending
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Any database driver can implement support for this library by implementing the [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")
 trait.

Re-exports[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#reexports)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`pub use [rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html "mod rocket") ;`

`pub use [deadpool_postgres](https://docs.rs/deadpool-postgres/0.13.2/deadpool_postgres/index.html "mod deadpool_postgres") ;`

`pub use [deadpool_redis](https://docs.rs/deadpool-redis/0.15.1/deadpool_redis/index.html "mod deadpool_redis") ;`

`pub use [mongodb](https://docs.rs/mongodb/2.8.2/mongodb/index.html "mod mongodb") ;`

`pub use [sqlx](https://docs.rs/sqlx/0.7.2/sqlx/index.html "mod sqlx") ;`

Modules[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#modules)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[diesel](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/index.html "mod rocket_db_pools::diesel")

Re-export of [`diesel`](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/index.html "mod diesel")
 with prelude types overridden with `async` variants from [`diesel_async`](https://docs.rs/diesel-async/0.4.1/diesel_async/index.html "mod diesel_async")
.

[figment](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/figment/index.html "mod rocket_db_pools::figment")

Re-export of the `figment` crate. Semi-hierarchical configuration so con-free, it’s unreal.

Structs[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#structs)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Config")

Base configuration for all database drivers.

[Connection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Connection")

A request guard which retrieves a single connection to a [`Database`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Database")
.

[Initializer](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Initializer.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Initializer")

A [`Fairing`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
 which initializes a [`Database`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Database")
 and its connection pool.

----------------------------------------------------------------------

A general error type for use by [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html#implementing "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")

 request guard.

Traits[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#traits)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

 with a configuration name.

# rocket_ws - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Crate rocket\_ws](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/index.html#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/all.html "show sidebar")

Crate rocket\_ws Copy item path
===============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_ws/lib.rs.html#1-326)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

WebSocket support for Rocket.

This crate implements support for WebSockets via Rocket’s [connection upgrade API](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/response/struct.Response.html#upgrading "struct rocket::response::response::Response")
 and [tungstenite](https://docs.rs/tokio-tungstenite/0.21.0/tokio_tungstenite/index.html "mod tokio_tungstenite")
.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/index.html#usage)
Usage
----------------------------------------------------------------

Depend on the crate. Here, we rename the dependency to `ws` for convenience:

    [dependencies]
    ws = { package = "rocket_ws", version = "0.1.1" }

Then, use [`WebSocket`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html "struct rocket_ws::WebSocket")
 as a request guard in any route and either call [`WebSocket::channel()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html#method.channel "method rocket_ws::WebSocket::channel")
 or return a stream via [`Stream!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/macro.Stream.html "macro rocket_ws::Stream")
 or [`WebSocket::stream()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html#method.stream "method rocket_ws::WebSocket::stream")
 in the handler. The examples below are equivalent:

    #[get("/echo?channel")]
    fn echo_channel(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Channel<'static> {
        use rocket::futures::{SinkExt, StreamExt};
    
        ws.channel(move |mut stream| Box::pin(async move {
            while let Some(message) = stream.next().await {
                let _ = stream.send(message?).await;
            }
    
            Ok(())
        }))
    }
    
    #[get("/echo?stream")]
    fn echo_stream(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Stream!['static] {
        ws::Stream! { ws =>
            for await message in ws {
                yield message?;
            }
        }
    }
    
    #[get("/echo?compose")]
    fn echo_compose(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Stream!['static] {
        ws.stream(|io| io)
    }

WebSocket connections are configurable via [`WebSocket::config()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html#method.config "method rocket_ws::WebSocket::config")
:

    #[get("/echo")]
    fn echo_stream(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Stream!['static] {
        let ws = ws.config(ws::Config {
            max_send_queue: Some(5),
            ..Default::default()
        });
    
        ws::Stream! { ws =>
            for await message in ws {
                yield message?;
            }
        }
    }

Modules[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/index.html#modules)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

[frame](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/frame/index.html "mod rocket_ws::frame")

Structures for constructing raw WebSocket frames.

[result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/result/index.html "mod rocket_ws::result")

Library [`Error`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/result/enum.Error.html "enum rocket_ws::result::Error")
 and [`Result`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/result/type.Result.html "type rocket_ws::result::Result")
 types.

[stream](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/stream/index.html "mod rocket_ws::stream")

Types representing incoming and/or outgoing `async` [`Message`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/enum.Message.html "enum rocket_ws::Message")
 streams.

Macros[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/index.html#macros)

------------------------------------------------------------------

[Stream](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/macro.Stream.html "macro rocket_ws::Stream")

Type and expression macro for `async` WebSocket [`Message`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/enum.Message.html "enum rocket_ws::Message")
 streams.

Structs[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/index.html#structs)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

[Channel](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.Channel.html "struct rocket_ws::Channel")

A streaming channel, returned by [`WebSocket::channel()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html#method.channel "method rocket_ws::WebSocket::channel")
.

[Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_ws::Config")

WebSocket connection configuration.

[WebSocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html "struct rocket_ws::WebSocket")

A request guard identifying WebSocket requests. Converts into a [`Channel`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.Channel.html "struct rocket_ws::Channel")
 or [`MessageStream`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/stream/struct.MessageStream.html "struct rocket_ws::stream::MessageStream")
.

Enums[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/index.html#enums)

----------------------------------------------------------------

[Message](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/enum.Message.html "enum rocket_ws::Message")

A WebSocket message.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/mtls.rs.html#1-25)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Available on **crate feature `mtls`** only.

Expand description

Support for mutual TLS client certificates.

For details on how to configure mutual TLS, see [`MutualTls`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls.html "struct rocket::config::MutualTls")
 and the [TLS guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/#tls)
. See [`Certificate`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/struct.Certificate.html "struct rocket::mtls::Certificate")
 for a request guard that validated, verifies, and retrieves client certificates.

.

Lower-level OID types re-exported from [`oid_registry`](https://docs.rs/oid-registry/0.4)
 and [`der-parser`](https://docs.rs/der-parser/7)
.

.

.

Enums[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/index.html#enums)

------------------------------------------------------------------

# rocket::serde::uuid - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Module uuid](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[serde](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/index.html)

Module uuid Copy item path
==========================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/serde/uuid.rs.html#1-122)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Available on **crate feature `uuid`** only.

Expand description

UUID path/query parameter and form value parsing support.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/index.html#enabling)
Enabling
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This module is only available when the `uuid` feature is enabled. Enable it in `Cargo.toml` as follows:

    [dependencies.rocket]
    version = "0.5.1"
    features = ["uuid"]

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/index.html#usage)
Usage
------------------------------------------------------------------------

`Uuid` implements [`FromParam`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromParam.html "trait rocket::request::FromParam")
 and [`FromFormField`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromFormField.html "trait rocket::form::FromFormField")
 (i.e, [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html "trait rocket::form::FromForm")
), allowing UUID values to be accepted directly in paths, queries, and forms. You can use the `Uuid` type directly as a target of a dynamic parameter:

    use rocket::serde::uuid::Uuid;
    
    #[get("/users/<id>")]
    fn user(id: Uuid) -> String {
        format!("We found: {}", id)
    }

You can also use the `Uuid` as a form value, including in query strings:

    use rocket::serde::uuid::Uuid;
    
    #[get("/user?<id>")]
    fn user(id: Uuid) -> String {
        format!("User ID: {}", id)
    }

Additionally, `Uuid` implements `UriDisplay<P>` for all `P`. As such, route URIs including `Uuid`s can be generated in a type-safe manner:

    use rocket::serde::uuid::Uuid;
    use rocket::response::Redirect;
    
    #[get("/user/<id>")]
    fn user(id: Uuid) -> String {
        format!("User ID: {}", id)
    }
    
    #[get("/user?<id>")]
    fn old_user_path(id: Uuid) -> Redirect {
        Redirect::to(uri!(user(id)))
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/index.html#extra-features)
Extra Features
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [`uuid`](https://docs.rs/uuid/1)
 crate exposes extra `v{n}` features for generating UUIDs which are not enabled by Rocket. To enable these features, depend on `uuid` directly. The extra functionality can be accessed via both `rocket::serde::uuid::Uuid` or the direct `uuid::Uuid`; the types are one and the same.

    [dependencies.uuid]
    version = "1"
    features = ["v1", "v4"]

Modules[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/index.html#modules)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[fmt](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/fmt/index.html "mod rocket::serde::uuid::fmt")

Adapters for alternative string formats.

Macros[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/index.html#macros)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[uuid](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/uuid/macro.uuid.html "macro rocket::serde::uuid::uuid")

Parse [`Uuid`](https://docs.rs/uuid/*/uuid/struct.Uuid.html)
s from string literals at compile time.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/serde/json.rs.html#1-654)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Available on **crate feature `json`** only.

Expand description

Automatic JSON (de)serialization support.

See [`Json`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/json/struct.Json.html "struct rocket::serde::json::Json")
 for details.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/json/index.html#enabling)
Enabling
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This module is only available when the `json` feature is enabled. Enable it in `Cargo.toml` as follows:

    [dependencies.rocket]
    version = "0.5.1"
    features = ["json"]

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/json/index.html#testing)
Testing
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [`LocalRequest`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/blocking/struct.LocalRequest.html "struct rocket::local::blocking::LocalRequest")
 and [`LocalResponse`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/blocking/struct.LocalResponse.html "struct rocket::local::blocking::LocalResponse")
 types provide [`json()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/blocking/struct.LocalRequest.html#method.json "method rocket::local::blocking::LocalRequest::json")
 and [`into_json()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/local/blocking/struct.LocalResponse.html#method.into_json "method rocket::local::blocking::LocalResponse::into_json")
 methods to create a request with serialized JSON and deserialize a response as JSON, respectively.

# rocket::fairing - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Module fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/index.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Module fairing Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/fairing/mod.rs.html#1-567)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

Expand description

Fairings: callbacks at launch, liftoff, request, and response time.

Fairings allow for structured interposition at various points in the application lifetime. Fairings can be seen as a restricted form of “middleware”. A fairing is an arbitrary structure with methods representing callbacks that Rocket will run at requested points in a program. You can use fairings to rewrite or record information about requests and responses, or to perform an action once a Rocket application has launched.

To learn more about writing a fairing, see the [`Fairing`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
 trait documentation. You can also use [`AdHoc`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/struct.AdHoc.html "struct rocket::fairing::AdHoc")
 to create a fairing on-the-fly from a closure or function.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/index.html#attaching)
Attaching

You must inform Rocket about fairings that you wish to be active by calling [`Rocket::attach()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.attach "method rocket::Rocket::attach")
 method on the application’s [`Rocket`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
 instance and passing in the appropriate [`Fairing`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
. For instance, to attach fairings named `req_fairing` and `res_fairing` to a new Rocket instance, you might write:

    let rocket = rocket::build()
        .attach(req_fairing)
        .attach(res_fairing);

Once a fairing is attached, Rocket will execute it at the appropriate time, which varies depending on the fairing implementation. See the [`Fairing`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
 trait documentation for more information on the dispatching of fairing methods.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/index.html#ordering)
Ordering

`Fairing`s are executed in the order in which they are attached: the first attached fairing has its callbacks executed before all others. A fairing can be attached any number of times. Except for [singleton fairings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html#singletons "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
, all attached instances are polled at runtime. Fairing callbacks may not be commutative; the order in which fairings are attached may be significant. It is thus important to communicate specific fairing functionality clearly.

Furthermore, a `Fairing` should take care to act locally so that the actions of other `Fairings` are not jeopardized. For instance, unless it is made abundantly clear, a fairing should not rewrite every request.

Structs[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/index.html#structs)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

[AdHoc](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/struct.AdHoc.html "struct rocket::fairing::AdHoc")

A ad-hoc fairing that can be created from a function or closure.

[Info](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/struct.Info.html "struct rocket::fairing::Info")

Information about a [`Fairing`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
.

[Kind](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/struct.Kind.html "struct rocket::fairing::Kind")

A bitset representing the kinds of callbacks a [`Fairing`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
 wishes to receive.

Traits[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/index.html#traits)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[Fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")

Trait implemented by fairings: Rocket’s structured middleware.

Type Aliases[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/index.html#types)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/type.Result.html "type rocket::fairing::Result")

A type alias for the return `Result` type of [`Fairing::on_ignite()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html#method.on_ignite "method rocket::fairing::Fairing::on_ignite")
.
