# Rocket in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#)

----------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Struct Rocket Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#136)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    pub struct Rocket<P: Phase>(/* private fields */);

Expand description

The application server itself.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#phases)
Phases
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

A `Rocket` instance represents a web server and its state. It progresses through three statically-enforced phases: build, ignite, orbit.

*   **Build**: _application and server configuration_
    
    This phase enables:
    
    *   setting configuration options
    *   mounting/registering routes/catchers
    *   managing state
    *   attaching fairings
    
    This is the _only_ phase in which an instance can be modified. To finalize changes, an instance is ignited via [`Rocket::ignite()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.ignite "method rocket::Rocket::ignite")
    , progressing it into the _ignite_ phase, or directly launched into orbit with [`Rocket::launch()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.launch "method rocket::Rocket::launch")
     which progress the instance through ignite into orbit.
    
*   **Ignite**: _verification and finalization of configuration_
    
    An instance in the [`Ignite`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/enum.Ignite.html "enum rocket::Ignite")
     phase is in its final configuration, available via [`Rocket::config()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.config "method rocket::Rocket::config")
    . Barring user-supplied interior mutation, application state is guaranteed to remain unchanged beyond this point. An instance in the ignite phase can be launched into orbit to serve requests via [`Rocket::launch()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.launch "method rocket::Rocket::launch")
    .
    
*   **Orbit**: _a running web server_
    
    An instance in the [`Orbit`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/enum.Orbit.html "enum rocket::Orbit")
     phase represents a _running_ application, actively serving requests.
    

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#launching)
Launching
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

To launch a `Rocket` application, the suggested approach is to return an instance of `Rocket<Build>` from a function named `rocket` marked with the [`#[launch]`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.launch.html "attr rocket::launch")
 attribute:

    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
    }

This generates a `main` function with an `async` runtime that runs the returned `Rocket` instance.

*   **Manual Launching**
    
    To launch an instance of `Rocket`, it _must_ progress through all three phases. To progress into the ignite or launch phases, a tokio `async` runtime is required. The [`#[main]`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.main.html "attr rocket::main")
     attribute initializes a Rocket-specific tokio runtime and runs the attributed `async fn` inside of it:
    
        #[rocket::main]
        async fn main() -> Result<(), rocket::Error> {
            let _rocket = rocket::build()
                .ignite().await?
                .launch().await?;
        
            Ok(())
        }
    
    Note that [`Rocket::launch()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.launch "method rocket::Rocket::launch")
     automatically progresses an instance of `Rocket` from any phase into orbit:
    
        #[rocket::main]
        async fn main() -> Result<(), rocket::Error> {
            let _rocket = rocket::build().launch().await?;
            Ok(())
        }
    
    For extreme and rare cases in which [`#[main]`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.main.html "attr rocket::main")
     imposes obstinate restrictions, use [`rocket::execute()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fn.execute.html "fn rocket::execute")
     to execute Rocket’s `launch()` future.
    
*   **Automatic Launching**
    
    Manually progressing an instance of Rocket though its phases is only necessary when either an instance’s finalized state is to be inspected (in the _ignite_ phase) or the instance is expected to deorbit due to [`Rocket::shutdown()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.shutdown "method rocket::Rocket::shutdown")
    . In the more common case when neither is required, the [`#[launch]`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.launch.html "attr rocket::launch")
     attribute can be used. When applied to a function that returns a `Rocket<Build>`, it automatically initializes an `async` runtime and launches the function’s returned instance:
    
        use rocket::{Rocket, Build};
        
        #[launch]
        fn rocket() -> Rocket<Build> {
            rocket::build()
        }
    
    To avoid needing to import _any_ items in the common case, the `launch` attribute will infer a return type written as `_` as `Rocket<Build>`:
    
        #[launch]
        fn rocket() -> _ {
            rocket::build()
        }
    

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#implementations)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#138-570)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#impl-Rocket%3CBuild%3E)

### impl [Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
<[Build](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/enum.Build.html "enum rocket::Build")
\>

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#156-158)

#### pub fn [build](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.build)
() -> Self

Create a new `Rocket` application using the default configuration provider, [`Config::figment()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.figment "associated function rocket::Config::figment")
.

This method is typically called through the [`rocket::build()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fn.build.html "fn rocket::build")
 alias.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#examples)
Examples

    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#182-193)

#### pub fn [custom](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.custom)
<T: [Provider](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/provider/trait.Provider.html "trait figment::provider::Provider")
\>(provider: T) -> Self

Creates a new `Rocket` application using the supplied configuration provider.

This method is typically called through the [`rocket::custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fn.custom.html "fn rocket::custom")
 alias.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example)
Example

    use rocket::figment::{Figment, providers::{Toml, Env, Format}};
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        let figment = Figment::from(rocket::Config::default())
            .merge(Toml::file("MyApp.toml").nested())
            .merge(Env::prefixed("MY_APP_").global());
    
        rocket::custom(figment)
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#240-243)

#### pub fn [configure](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.configure)
<T: [Provider](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/provider/trait.Provider.html "trait figment::provider::Provider")
\>(self, provider: T) -> Self

Sets the configuration provider in `self` to `provider`.

A [`Figment`](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/figment/struct.Figment.html "struct figment::figment::Figment")
 generated from the current `provider` can _always_ be retrieved via [`Rocket::figment()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.figment "method rocket::Rocket::figment")
. However, because the provider can be changed at any point prior to ignition, a [`Config`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")
 can only be retrieved in the ignite or orbit phases, or by manually extracting one from a particular figment.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    
    let config = Config {
        port: 7777,
        address: Ipv4Addr::new(18, 127, 0, 1).into(),
        temp_dir: "/tmp/config-example".into(),
        ..Config::debug_default()
    };
    
    let rocket = rocket::custom(&config).ignite().await?;
    assert_eq!(rocket.config().port, 7777);
    assert_eq!(rocket.config().address, Ipv4Addr::new(18, 127, 0, 1));
    assert_eq!(rocket.config().temp_dir.relative(), Path::new("/tmp/config-example"));
    
    // Create a new figment which modifies _some_ keys the existing figment:
    let figment = rocket.figment().clone()
        .merge((Config::PORT, 8888))
        .merge((Config::ADDRESS, "171.64.200.10"));
    
    let rocket = rocket::custom(&config)
        .configure(figment)
        .ignite().await?;
    
    assert_eq!(rocket.config().port, 8888);
    assert_eq!(rocket.config().address, Ipv4Addr::new(171, 64, 200, 10));
    assert_eq!(rocket.config().temp_dir.relative(), Path::new("/tmp/config-example"));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#341-349)

#### pub fn [mount](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.mount)
<'a, B, R>(self, base: B, routes: R) -> Self

where B: [TryInto](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.TryInto.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::TryInto")
<[Origin](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Origin.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Origin")
<'a>> + [Clone](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/clone/trait.Clone.html "trait core::clone::Clone")
 + [Display](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/fmt/trait.Display.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::fmt::Display")
, B::[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.TryInto.html#associatedtype.Error "type rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::TryInto::Error")
: [Display](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/fmt/trait.Display.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::fmt::Display")
, R: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Vec](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::vec::Vec")
<[Route](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::Route")
\>>,

Mounts all of the routes in the supplied vector at the given `base` path. Mounting a route with path `path` at path `base` makes the route available at `base/path`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#panics)
Panics

Panics if either:

*   the `base` mount point is not a valid static path: a valid origin URI without dynamic parameters.
    
*   any route’s URI is not a valid origin URI.
    
    **Note:** _This kind of panic is guaranteed not to occur if the routes were generated using Rocket’s code generation._
    

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#examples-1)
Examples

Use the `routes!` macro to mount routes created using the code generation facilities. Requests to the `/hello/world` URI will be dispatched to the `hi` route.

    #[get("/world")]
    fn hi() -> &'static str {
        "Hello!"
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().mount("/hello", routes![hi])
    }

Manually create a route named `hi` at path `"/world"` mounted at base `"/hello"`. Requests to the `/hello/world` URI will be dispatched to the `hi` route.

    use rocket::{Request, Route, Data, route};
    use rocket::http::Method;
    
    fn hi<'r>(req: &'r Request, _: Data<'r>) -> route::BoxFuture<'r> {
        route::Outcome::from(req, "Hello!").pin()
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        let hi_route = Route::new(Method::Get, "/world", hi);
        rocket::build().mount("/hello", vec![hi_route])
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#380-388)

#### pub fn [register](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.register)
<'a, B, C>(self, base: B, catchers: C) -> Self

where B: [TryInto](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.TryInto.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::TryInto")
<[Origin](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Origin.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Origin")
<'a>> + [Clone](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/clone/trait.Clone.html "trait core::clone::Clone")
 + [Display](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/fmt/trait.Display.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::fmt::Display")
, B::[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.TryInto.html#associatedtype.Error "type rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::TryInto::Error")
: [Display](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/fmt/trait.Display.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::fmt::Display")
, C: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Vec](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/vec/struct.Vec.html "struct rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::vec::Vec")
<[Catcher](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Catcher.html "struct rocket::Catcher")
\>>,

Registers all of the catchers in the supplied vector, scoped to `base`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#panics-1)
Panics

Panics if `base` is not a valid static path: a valid origin URI without dynamic parameters.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#examples-2)
Examples

    use rocket::Request;
    
    #[catch(500)]
    fn internal_error() -> &'static str {
        "Whoops! Looks like we messed up."
    }
    
    #[catch(404)]
    fn not_found(req: &Request) -> String {
        format!("I couldn't find '{}'. Try something else?", req.uri())
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().register("/", catchers![internal_error, not_found])
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#432-442)

#### pub fn [manage](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.manage)
<T>(self, state: T) -> Self

where T: [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + 'static,

Add `state` to the state managed by this instance of Rocket.

This method can be called any number of times as long as each call refers to a different `T`.

Managed state can be retrieved by any request handler via the [`State`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html "struct rocket::State")
 request guard. In particular, if a value of type `T` is managed by Rocket, adding `State<T>` to the list of arguments in a request handler instructs Rocket to retrieve the managed value.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#panics-2)
Panics

Panics if state of type `T` is already being managed.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-2)
Example

    use rocket::State;
    
    struct MyInt(isize);
    struct MyString(String);
    
    #[get("/int")]
    fn int(state: &State<MyInt>) -> String {
        format!("The stateful int is: {}", state.0)
    }
    
    #[get("/string")]
    fn string(state: &State<MyString>) -> &str {
        &state.0
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
            .manage(MyInt(10))
            .manage(MyString("Hello, managed state!".to_string()))
            .mount("/", routes![int, string])
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#466-469)

#### pub fn [attach](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.attach)
<F: [Fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
\>(self, fairing: F) -> Self

Attaches a fairing to this instance of Rocket. No fairings are eagerly executed; fairings are executed at their appropriate time.

If the attached fairing is _fungible_ and a fairing of the same name already exists, this fairing replaces it.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-3)
Example

    use rocket::Rocket;
    use rocket::fairing::AdHoc;
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
            .attach(AdHoc::on_liftoff("Liftoff Message", |_| Box::pin(async {
                println!("We have liftoff!");
            })))
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#513-569)

#### pub async fn [ignite](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.ignite)
(self) -> [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/result/enum.Result.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::result::Result")
<[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
<[Ignite](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/enum.Ignite.html "enum rocket::Ignite")
\>, [Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Error.html "struct rocket::Error")
\>

Returns a `Future` that transitions this instance of `Rocket` into the _ignite_ phase.

When `await`ed, the future runs all _ignite_ fairings in serial, [attach](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.attach "method rocket::Rocket::attach")
 order, and verifies that `self` represents a valid instance of `Rocket` ready for launch. This means that:

*   All ignite fairings succeeded.
*   A valid [`Config`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")
     was extracted from [`Rocket::figment()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.figment "method rocket::Rocket::figment")
    .
*   If `secrets` are enabled, the extracted `Config` contains a safe secret key.
*   There are no [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Route.html#collisions "struct rocket::Route")
     or [`Catcher`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Catcher.html#collisions "struct rocket::Catcher")
     collisions.
*   No [`Sentinel`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/trait.Sentinel.html "trait rocket::Sentinel")
     triggered an abort.

If any of these conditions fail to be met, a respective [`Error`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Error.html "struct rocket::Error")
 is returned.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-4)
Example

    use rocket::fairing::AdHoc;
    
    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rocket::Error> {
        let rocket = rocket::build()
            .attach(AdHoc::on_ignite("Manage State", |rocket| async move {
                rocket.manage(String::from("managed string"))
            }));
    
        // No fairings are run until ignition occurs.
        assert!(rocket.state::<String>().is_none());
    
        let rocket = rocket.ignite().await?;
        assert_eq!(rocket.state::<String>().unwrap(), "managed string");
    
        Ok(())
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#588-676)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#impl-Rocket%3CIgnite%3E)

### impl [Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
<[Ignite](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/enum.Ignite.html "enum rocket::Ignite")
\>

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#602-604)

#### pub fn [config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.config)
(&self) -> &[Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Returns the finalized, active configuration. This is guaranteed to remain stable through ignition and into orbit.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-5)
Example

    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rocket::Error> {
        let rocket = rocket::build().ignite().await?;
        let config = rocket.config();
        Ok(())
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#638-640)

#### pub fn [shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.shutdown)
(&self) -> [Shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::Shutdown")
 [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#)

Returns a handle which can be used to trigger a shutdown and detect a triggered shutdown.

A completed graceful shutdown resolves the future returned by [`Rocket::launch()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.launch "method rocket::Rocket::launch")
. If [`Shutdown::notify()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.notify "method rocket::Shutdown::notify")
 is called _before_ an instance is launched, it will be immediately shutdown after liftoff. See [`Shutdown`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::Shutdown")
 and [`config::Shutdown`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::config::Shutdown")
 for details on graceful shutdown.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-6)
Example

    use rocket::tokio::{self, time};
    
    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rocket::Error> {
        let rocket = rocket::build().ignite().await?;
    
        let shutdown = rocket.shutdown();
        tokio::spawn(async move {
            time::sleep(time::Duration::from_secs(5)).await;
            shutdown.notify();
        });
    
        // The `launch()` future resolves after ~5 seconds.
        let result = rocket.launch().await;
        assert!(result.is_ok());
    
        Ok(())
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#678-742)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#impl-Rocket%3COrbit%3E)

### impl [Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
<[Orbit](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/enum.Orbit.html "enum rocket::Orbit")
\>

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#708-710)

#### pub fn [config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.config-1)
(&self) -> &[Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Returns the finalized, active configuration. This is guaranteed to remain stable after [`Rocket::ignite()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.ignite "method rocket::Rocket::ignite")
, through ignition and into orbit.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-7)
Example

    use rocket::fairing::AdHoc;
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
            .attach(AdHoc::on_liftoff("Config", |rocket| Box::pin(async move {
                println!("Rocket launch config: {:?}", rocket.config());
            })))
    }

#### pub fn [shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.shutdown-1)
(&self) -> [Shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::Shutdown")
 [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#)

Returns a handle which can be used to trigger a shutdown and detect a triggered shutdown.

A completed graceful shutdown resolves the future returned by [`Rocket::launch()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.launch "method rocket::Rocket::launch")
. See [`Shutdown`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::Shutdown")
 and [`config::Shutdown`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::config::Shutdown")
 for details on graceful shutdown.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-8)
Example

    use rocket::tokio::{self, time};
    use rocket::fairing::AdHoc;
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
            .attach(AdHoc::on_liftoff("Shutdown", |rocket| Box::pin(async move {
                let shutdown = rocket.shutdown();
                tokio::spawn(async move {
                    time::sleep(time::Duration::from_secs(5)).await;
                    shutdown.notify();
                });
            })))
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#744-906)

### impl<P: [Phase](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::Phase")
\> [Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
<P>

#### pub fn [routes](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.routes)
(&self) -> impl [Iterator](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/iter/trait.Iterator.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::iter::Iterator")
<Item = &[Route](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::Route")
\>

Returns an iterator over all of the routes mounted on this instance of Rocket. The order is unspecified.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-9)
Example

    use rocket::Rocket;
    use rocket::fairing::AdHoc;
    
    #[get("/hello")]
    fn hello() -> &'static str {
        "Hello, world!"
    }
    
    let rocket = rocket::build()
        .mount("/", routes![hello])
        .mount("/hi", routes![hello]);
    
    assert_eq!(rocket.routes().count(), 2);
    assert!(rocket.routes().any(|r| r.uri == "/hello"));
    assert!(rocket.routes().any(|r| r.uri == "/hi/hello"));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#799-805)

#### pub fn [catchers](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.catchers)
(&self) -> impl [Iterator](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/iter/trait.Iterator.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::iter::Iterator")
<Item = &[Catcher](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Catcher.html "struct rocket::Catcher")
\>

Returns an iterator over all of the catchers registered on this instance of Rocket. The order is unspecified.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-10)
Example

    use rocket::Rocket;
    use rocket::fairing::AdHoc;
    
    #[catch(404)] fn not_found() -> &'static str { "Nothing here, sorry!" }
    #[catch(500)] fn just_500() -> &'static str { "Whoops!?" }
    #[catch(default)] fn some_default() -> &'static str { "Everything else." }
    
    let rocket = rocket::build()
        .register("/foo", catchers![not_found])
        .register("/", catchers![just_500, some_default]);
    
    assert_eq!(rocket.catchers().count(), 3);
    assert!(rocket.catchers().any(|c| c.code == Some(404) && c.base == "/foo"));
    assert!(rocket.catchers().any(|c| c.code == Some(500) && c.base == "/"));
    assert!(rocket.catchers().any(|c| c.code == None && c.base == "/"));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#819-825)

#### pub fn [state](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.state)
<T: [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + 'static>(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `Some` of the managed state value for the type `T` if it is being managed by `self`. Otherwise, returns `None`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-11)
Example

    #[derive(PartialEq, Debug)]
    struct MyState(&'static str);
    
    let rocket = rocket::build().manage(MyState("hello!"));
    assert_eq!(rocket.state::<MyState>().unwrap(), &MyState("hello!"));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#837-843)

#### pub fn [figment](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.figment)
(&self) -> &[Figment](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/figment/struct.Figment.html "struct figment::figment::Figment")

Returns the figment derived from the configuration provider set for `self`. To extract a typed config, prefer to use [`AdHoc::config()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/struct.AdHoc.html#method.config "associated function rocket::fairing::AdHoc::config")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-12)
Example

    let rocket = rocket::build();
    let figment = rocket.figment();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/rkt.rs.html#899-905)

#### pub async fn [launch](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.launch)
(self) -> [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/result/enum.Result.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::result::Result")
<[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
<[Ignite](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/enum.Ignite.html "enum rocket::Ignite")
\>, [Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Error.html "struct rocket::Error")
\>

Returns a `Future` that transitions this instance of `Rocket` from any phase into the _orbit_ phase. When `await`ed, the future drives the server forward, listening for and dispatching requests to mounted routes and catchers.

In addition to all of the processes that occur during [ignition](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.ignite "method rocket::Rocket::ignite")
, a successful launch results in _liftoff_ fairings being executed _after_ binding to any respective network interfaces but before serving the first request. Liftoff fairings are run concurrently; resolution of all fairings is `await`ed before resuming request serving.

The `Future` resolves as an `Err` if any of the following occur:

*   there is an error igniting; see [`Rocket::ignite()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.ignite "method rocket::Rocket::ignite")
    .
*   there is an I/O error starting the server.
*   an unrecoverable, system-level error occurs while running.

The `Future` resolves as an `Ok` if any of the following occur:

*   graceful shutdown via [`Shutdown::notify()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.notify "method rocket::Shutdown::notify")
     completes.

The returned value on `Ok(())` is previously running instance.

The `Future` does not resolve otherwise.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#error)
Error

If there is a problem starting the application or the application fails unexpectedly while running, an [`Error`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Error.html "struct rocket::Error")
 is returned. Note that a value of type `Error` panics if dropped without first being inspected. See the [`Error`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Error.html "struct rocket::Error")
 documentation for more information.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-13)
Example

    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() {
        let result = rocket::build().launch().await;
    
        // this is reachable only after `Shutdown::notify()` or `Ctrl+C`.
        println!("Rocket: deorbit.");
    }

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#134)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#impl-Paint-for-T)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-14)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

\>

 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

Example

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-34)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

\>

Returns `self` with the [`bg()`](self::bg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

Example

    println!("{}", value.on_primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-54)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-55)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-64)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

\>

 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

    println!("{}", value.mask());

 set to \[`Quirk :: Linger`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-67)
Example

    println!("{}", value.linger());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

\>

👎Deprecated since 1.0.1: renamed to `resetting()` due to conflicts with `Vec::clear()`. The `clear()` method will be removed in a future release.

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())
 set to \[`Quirk :: Clear`\].

(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")

Example

    println!("{}", value.on_bright());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#example-72)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)

,

# State in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[State](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Struct State Copy item path
===========================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/state.rs.html#117)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    pub struct State<T: Send + Sync + 'static>(/* private fields */);

Expand description

Request guard to retrieve managed state.

A reference `&State<T>` type is a request guard which retrieves the managed state managing for some type `T`. A value for the given type must previously have been registered to be managed by Rocket via [`Rocket::manage()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.manage "method rocket::Rocket::manage")
. The type being managed must be thread safe and sendable across thread boundaries as multiple handlers in multiple threads may be accessing the value at once. In other words, it must implement [`Send`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [`Sync`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + `'static`.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example)
Example
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Imagine you have some configuration struct of the type `MyConfig` that you’d like to initialize at start-up and later access it in several handlers. The following example does just this:

    use rocket::State;
    
    // In a real application, this would likely be more complex.
    struct MyConfig {
        user_val: String
    }
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn index(state: &State<MyConfig>) -> String {
        format!("The config value is: {}", state.user_val)
    }
    
    #[get("/raw")]
    fn raw_config_value(state: &State<MyConfig>) -> &str {
        &state.user_val
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
            .mount("/", routes![index, raw_config_value])
            .manage(MyConfig { user_val: "user input".to_string() })
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#within-request-guards)
Within Request Guards
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Because `State` is itself a request guard, managed state can be retrieved from another request guard’s implementation using either [`Request::guard()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.guard "method rocket::Request::guard")
 or [`Rocket::state()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.state "method rocket::Rocket::state")
. In the following code example, the `Item` request guard retrieves `MyConfig` from managed state:

    use rocket::State;
    use rocket::request::{self, Request, FromRequest};
    use rocket::outcome::IntoOutcome;
    use rocket::http::Status;
    
    struct Item<'r>(&'r str);
    
    #[rocket::async_trait]
    impl<'r> FromRequest<'r> for Item<'r> {
        type Error = ();
    
        async fn from_request(request: &'r Request<'_>) -> request::Outcome<Self, ()> {
            // Using `State` as a request guard. Use `inner()` to get an `'r`.
            let outcome = request.guard::<&State<MyConfig>>().await
                .map(|my_config| Item(&my_config.user_val));
    
            // Or alternatively, using `Rocket::state()`:
            let outcome = request.rocket().state::<MyConfig>()
                .map(|my_config| Item(&my_config.user_val))
                .or_forward(Status::InternalServerError);
    
            outcome
        }
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#testing-with-state)
Testing with `State`
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When unit testing your application, you may find it necessary to manually construct a type of `State` to pass to your functions. To do so, use the [`State::get()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.get "associated function rocket::State::get")
 static method or the `From<&T>` implementation:

    use rocket::State;
    
    struct MyManagedState(usize);
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn handler(state: &State<MyManagedState>) -> String {
        state.0.to_string()
    }
    
    let mut rocket = rocket::build().manage(MyManagedState(127));
    let state = State::get(&rocket).expect("managed `MyManagedState`");
    assert_eq!(handler(state), "127");
    
    let managed = MyManagedState(77);
    assert_eq!(handler(State::from(&managed)), "77");

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#implementations)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/state.rs.html#119-186)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#impl-State%3CT%3E)

### impl<T: [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + 'static> [State](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html "struct rocket::State")
<T>

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/state.rs.html#143-145)

#### pub fn [get](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.get)
<P: [Phase](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::Phase")
\>(rocket: &[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
<P>) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<&[State](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html "struct rocket::State")
<T>>

Returns the managed state value in `rocket` for the type `T` if it is being managed by `rocket`. Otherwise, returns `None`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::State;
    
    #[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
    struct Managed(usize);
    
    #[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
    struct Unmanaged(usize);
    
    let rocket = rocket::build().manage(Managed(7));
    
    let state: Option<&State<Managed>> = State::get(&rocket);
    assert_eq!(state.map(|s| s.inner()), Some(&Managed(7)));
    
    let state: Option<&State<Unmanaged>> = State::get(&rocket);
    assert_eq!(state, None);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/state.rs.html#183-185)

#### pub fn [inner](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.inner)
(&self) -> [&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)

Borrow the inner value.

Using this method is typically unnecessary as `State` implements [`Deref`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.Deref.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::Deref")
 with a [`Deref::Target`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.Deref.html#associatedtype.Target "associated type rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::Deref::Target")
 of `T`. This means Rocket will automatically coerce a `State<T>` to an `&T` as required. This method should only be used when a longer lifetime is required.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-2)
Example

    use rocket::State;
    
    #[derive(Clone)]
    struct MyConfig {
        user_val: String
    }
    
    fn handler1<'r>(config: &State<MyConfig>) -> String {
        let config = config.inner().clone();
        config.user_val
    }
    
    // Use the `Deref` implementation which coerces implicitly
    fn handler2(config: &State<MyConfig>) -> String {
        config.user_val.clone()
    }

Trait Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#trait-implementations)

#### fn [into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.From.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#impl-Paint-for-T)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-3)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.primary)

(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-4)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-23)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-43)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.bold)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-44)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.dim)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-53)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.State.html#example-61)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)

# async_trait in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[async\_trait](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.async_trait.html#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Attribute Macro async\_trait Copy item path
===========================================

[Source](https://docs.rs/async-trait/0.1.89/src/async_trait/lib.rs.html#252)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.async_trait?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    #[async_trait]

Expand description

Retrofits support for `async fn` in trait impls and declarations.

Any trait declaration or trait `impl` decorated with `#[async_trait]` is retrofitted with support for `async fn`s:

    #[async_trait]
    trait MyAsyncTrait {
        async fn do_async_work();
    }
    
    #[async_trait]
    impl MyAsyncTrait for () {
        async fn do_async_work() { /* .. */ }
    }

All `impl`s for a trait declared with `#[async_trait]` must themselves be decorated with `#[async_trait]`. Many of Rocket’s traits, such as [`FromRequest`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromRequest.html "trait rocket::request::FromRequest")
 and [`Fairing`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")
 are `async`. As such, implementations of said traits must be decorated with `#[async_trait]`. See the individual trait docs for trait-specific details.

For more details on `#[async_trait]`, see [`async_trait`](https://docs.rs/async-trait/0.1.89/async_trait/index.html "mod async_trait")
.

---

# Shutdown in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Struct Shutdown Copy item path
==============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/shutdown.rs.html#68)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    pub struct Shutdown(/* private fields */);

Expand description

A request guard and future for graceful shutdown.

A server shutdown is manually requested by calling [`Shutdown::notify()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.notify "method rocket::Shutdown::notify")
 or, if enabled, through [automatic triggers](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html#triggers "struct rocket::config::Shutdown")
 like `Ctrl-C`. Rocket will stop accepting new requests, finish handling any pending requests, wait a grace period before cancelling any outstanding I/O, and return `Ok()` to the caller of [`Rocket::launch()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.launch "method rocket::Rocket::launch")
. Graceful shutdown is configured via [`config::Shutdown`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::config::Shutdown")
.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#detecting-shutdown)
Detecting Shutdown
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`Shutdown` is also a future that resolves when [`Shutdown::notify()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.notify "method rocket::Shutdown::notify")
 is called. This can be used to detect shutdown in any part of the application:

    use rocket::Shutdown;
    
    #[get("/wait/for/shutdown")]
    async fn wait_for_shutdown(shutdown: Shutdown) -> &'static str {
        shutdown.await;
        "Somewhere, shutdown was requested."
    }

See the [`stream`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/stream/index.html#graceful-shutdown "mod rocket::response::stream")
 docs for an example of detecting shutdown in an infinite responder.

Additionally, a completed shutdown request resolves the future returned from [`Rocket::launch()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.launch "method rocket::Rocket::launch")
:

    use rocket::Shutdown;
    
    #[get("/shutdown")]
    fn shutdown(shutdown: Shutdown) -> &'static str {
        shutdown.notify();
        "Shutting down..."
    }
    
    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() {
        let result = rocket::build()
            .mount("/", routes![shutdown])
            .launch()
            .await;
    
        // If the server shut down (by visiting `/shutdown`), `result` is `Ok`.
        result.expect("server failed unexpectedly");
    }

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#implementations)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/shutdown.rs.html#70-92)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#impl-Shutdown)

### impl [Shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::Shutdown")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/shutdown.rs.html#89-91)

#### pub fn [notify](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.notify)
(self)

Notify the application to shut down gracefully.

This function returns immediately; pending requests will continue to run until completion or expiration of the grace period, which ever comes first, before the actual shutdown occurs. The grace period can be configured via [`Shutdown::grace`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html#structfield.grace "field rocket::config::Shutdown::grace")
.

    use rocket::Shutdown;
    
    #[get("/shutdown")]
    fn shutdown(shutdown: Shutdown) -> &'static str {
        shutdown.notify();
        "Shutting down..."
    }

Trait Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#trait-implementations)

Fuse a future such that `poll` will never again be called once it has completed. This method can be used to turn any `Future` into a `FusedFuture`. [Read more](https://docs.rs/futures-util/0.3.31/futures_util/future/future/trait.FutureExt.html#method.fuse)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

,

 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

\>::[IntoFuture](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/future/into_future/trait.IntoFuture.html#associatedtype.IntoFuture "type core::future::into_future::IntoFuture::IntoFuture")

Creates a future from a value. [Read more](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/future/into_future/trait.IntoFuture.html#tymethod.into_future)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#impl-Paint-for-T)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.primary)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example-1)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example-20)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`bg()`](self::bg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

Example

    println!("{}", value.on_primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example-40)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example-41)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example-50)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

\>

 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example-51)
Example

    println!("{}", value.mask());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())
 set to \[`Quirk :: Linger`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example-53)
Example

    println!("{}", value.linger());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Shutdown.html#example-58)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)
