# catch in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[catch](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.catch.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Attribute Macro catch Copy item path
====================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_codegen/lib.rs.html#355)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.catch?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    #[catch]

Expand description

Attribute to generate a [`Catcher`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Catcher.html)
 and associated metadata.

This attribute can only be applied to free functions:

    use rocket::Request;
    use rocket::http::Status;
    
    #[catch(404)]
    fn not_found(req: &Request) -> String {
        format!("Sorry, {} does not exist.", req.uri())
    }
    
    #[catch(default)]
    fn default(status: Status, req: &Request) -> String {
        format!("{} ({})", status, req.uri())
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.catch.html#grammar)
Grammar
----------------------------------------------------------------------

The grammar for the `#[catch]` attributes is defined as:

    catch := STATUS | 'default'
    
    STATUS := valid HTTP status code (integer in [200, 599])

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.catch.html#typing-requirements)
Typing Requirements
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The decorated function may take zero, one, or two arguments. It’s type signature must be one of the following, where `R:`[`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html)
:

*   `fn() -> R`
*   `fn(`[`&Request`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html)
    `) -> R`
*   `fn(`[`Status`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Status.html)
    `,` [`&Request`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html)
    `) -> R`

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.catch.html#semantics)
Semantics
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

The attribute generates two items:

1.  An error [`Handler`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/trait.Handler.html)
    .
    
    The generated handler calls the decorated function, passing in the [`Status`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Status.html)
     and [`&Request`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html)
     values if requested. The returned value is used to generate a [`Response`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html)
     via the type’s [`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html)
     implementation.
    
2.  A static structure used by [`catchers!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/macro.catchers.html)
     to generate a [`Catcher`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Catcher.html)
    .
    
    The static structure (and resulting [`Catcher`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Catcher.html)
    ) is populated with the name (the function’s name) and status code from the route attribute or `None` if `default`. The handler is set to the generated handler.

---

# Response in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Response](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Struct Response Copy item path
==============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#487-492)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    pub struct Response<'r> { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

A response, as returned by types implementing [`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
.

See [`Builder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Builder.html "struct rocket::response::Builder")
 for docs on how a `Response` is typically created and the [module docs](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/index.html "mod rocket::response")
 for notes on composing responses

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#upgrading)
Upgrading

A response may optionally register [`IoHandler`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.IoHandler.html "trait rocket::data::IoHandler")
s for upgraded requests via [`Response::add_upgrade()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.add_upgrade "method rocket::Response::add_upgrade")
 or the corresponding builder method [`Builder::upgrade()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Builder.html#method.upgrade "method rocket::response::Builder::upgrade")
. If the incoming request 1) requests an upgrade via a `Connection: Upgrade` header _and_ 2) includes a protocol in its `Upgrade` header that is registered by the returned `Response`, the connection will be upgraded. An upgrade response is sent to the client, and the registered `IoHandler` for the client’s preferred protocol is invoked with an [`IoStream`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/struct.IoStream.html "struct rocket::data::IoStream")
 representing a raw byte stream to the client. Note that protocol names are treated case-insensitively during matching.

If a connection is upgraded, Rocket automatically set the following in the upgrade response:

*   The response status to `101 Switching Protocols`.
*   The `Connection: Upgrade` header.
*   The `Upgrade` header’s value to the selected protocol.

As such, a response **should never** set a `101` status nor the `Connection` or `Upgrade` headers: Rocket handles this automatically. Instead, it should set a status and headers to use in case the connection is not upgraded, either due to an error or because the client did not request an upgrade.

If a connection _is not_ upgraded due to an error, even though there was a matching, registered protocol, the `IoHandler` is not invoked, and the original response is sent to the client without alteration.

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#implementations)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#494-1115)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#impl-Response%3C'r%3E)

### impl<'r> [Response](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html "struct rocket::Response")
<'r>

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#513-515)

#### pub fn [new](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.new)
() -> [Response](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html "struct rocket::Response")
<'r>

Creates a new, empty `Response` without a status, body, or headers. Because all HTTP responses must have a status, if a default `Response` is written to the client without a status, the status defaults to `200 Ok`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::Status;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    
    assert_eq!(response.status(), Status::Ok);
    assert_eq!(response.headers().len(), 0);
    assert!(response.body().is_none());

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#528-530)

#### pub fn [build](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.build)
() -> [Builder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Builder.html "struct rocket::response::Builder")
<'r>

Returns a `Builder` with a base of `Response::new()`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    
    let builder = Response::build();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#544-546)

#### pub fn [build\_from](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.build_from)
(other: [Response](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html "struct rocket::Response")
<'r>) -> [Builder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Builder.html "struct rocket::response::Builder")
<'r>

Returns a `Builder` with a base of `other`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-2)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    
    let other = Response::new();
    let builder = Response::build_from(other);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#563-565)

#### pub fn [status](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.status)
(&self) -> [Status](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Status.html "struct rocket::http::Status")

Returns the status of `self`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-3)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::Status;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    assert_eq!(response.status(), Status::Ok);
    
    response.set_status(Status::NotFound);
    assert_eq!(response.status(), Status::NotFound);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#580-582)

#### pub fn [set\_status](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_status)
(&mut self, status: [Status](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Status.html "struct rocket::http::Status")
)

Sets the status of `self` to `status`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-4)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::Status;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.set_status(Status::ImATeapot);
    assert_eq!(response.status(), Status::ImATeapot);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#598-600)

#### pub fn [content\_type](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.content_type)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[ContentType](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html "struct rocket::http::ContentType")
\>

Returns the Content-Type header of `self`. If the header is not present or is malformed, returns `None`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-5)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::ContentType;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.set_header(ContentType::HTML);
    assert_eq!(response.content_type(), Some(ContentType::HTML));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#616-620)

#### pub fn [cookies](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.cookies)
(&self) -> impl [Iterator](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/iter/trait.Iterator.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::iter::Iterator")
<Item = [Cookie](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Cookie.html "struct rocket::http::Cookie")
<'\_>>

Returns an iterator over the cookies in `self` as identified by the `Set-Cookie` header. Malformed cookies are skipped.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-6)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::Cookie;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.set_header(Cookie::new("hello", "world!"));
    let cookies: Vec<_> = response.cookies().collect();
    assert_eq!(cookies, vec![Cookie::new("hello", "world!")]);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#640-642)

#### pub fn [headers](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.headers)
(&self) -> &[HeaderMap](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.HeaderMap.html "struct rocket::http::HeaderMap")
<'r>

Returns a [`HeaderMap`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.HeaderMap.html "struct rocket::http::HeaderMap")
 of all of the headers in `self`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-7)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::Header;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.adjoin_raw_header("X-Custom", "1");
    response.adjoin_raw_header("X-Custom", "2");
    
    let mut custom_headers = response.headers().iter();
    assert_eq!(custom_headers.next(), Some(Header::new("X-Custom", "1")));
    assert_eq!(custom_headers.next(), Some(Header::new("X-Custom", "2")));
    assert_eq!(custom_headers.next(), None);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#667-669)

#### pub fn [set\_header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_header)
<'h: 'r, H: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Header.html "struct rocket::http::Header")
<'h>>>(&mut self, header: H) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Sets the header `header` in `self`. Any existing headers with the name `header.name` will be lost, and only `header` will remain. The type of `header` can be any type that implements `Into<Header>`. This includes `Header` itself, [`ContentType`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html "struct rocket::http::ContentType")
 and [`hyper::header` types](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/hyper/header/index.html "mod rocket::http::hyper::header")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-8)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::ContentType;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    
    response.set_header(ContentType::HTML);
    assert_eq!(response.headers().iter().next(), Some(ContentType::HTML.into()));
    assert_eq!(response.headers().len(), 1);
    
    response.set_header(ContentType::JSON);
    assert_eq!(response.headers().iter().next(), Some(ContentType::JSON.into()));
    assert_eq!(response.headers().len(), 1);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#693-697)

#### pub fn [set\_raw\_header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_raw_header)
<'a: 'r, 'b: 'r, N, V>( &mut self, name: N, value: V, ) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

where N: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Cow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/enum.Cow.html "enum alloc::borrow::Cow")
<'a, [str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>, V: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Cow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/enum.Cow.html "enum alloc::borrow::Cow")
<'b, [str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>,

Sets the custom header with name `name` and value `value` in `self`. Any existing headers with the same `name` will be lost, and the new custom header will remain. This method should be used sparingly; prefer to use [set\_header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_header)
 instead.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-9)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::Header;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    
    response.set_raw_header("X-Custom", "1");
    assert_eq!(response.headers().get_one("X-Custom"), Some("1"));
    assert_eq!(response.headers().len(), 1);
    
    response.set_raw_header("X-Custom", "2");
    assert_eq!(response.headers().get_one("X-Custom"), Some("2"));
    assert_eq!(response.headers().len(), 1);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#723-725)

#### pub fn [adjoin\_header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.adjoin_header)
<'h: 'r, H: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Header.html "struct rocket::http::Header")
<'h>>>(&mut self, header: H)

Adds the header `header` to `self`. If `self` contains headers with the name `header.name`, another header with the same name and value `header.value` is added. The type of `header` can be any type that implements `Into<Header>`. This includes `Header` itself, [`ContentType`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html "struct rocket::http::ContentType")
 and [`hyper::header` types](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/hyper/header/index.html "mod rocket::http::hyper::header")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-10)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::Header;
    use rocket::http::hyper::header::ACCEPT;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.adjoin_header(Header::new(ACCEPT.as_str(), "application/json"));
    response.adjoin_header(Header::new(ACCEPT.as_str(), "text/plain"));
    
    let mut accept_headers = response.headers().iter();
    assert_eq!(accept_headers.next(), Some(Header::new(ACCEPT.as_str(), "application/json")));
    assert_eq!(accept_headers.next(), Some(Header::new(ACCEPT.as_str(), "text/plain")));
    assert_eq!(accept_headers.next(), None);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#750-754)

#### pub fn [adjoin\_raw\_header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.adjoin_raw_header)
<'a: 'r, 'b: 'r, N, V>(&mut self, name: N, value: V)

where N: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Cow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/enum.Cow.html "enum alloc::borrow::Cow")
<'a, [str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>, V: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Cow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/enum.Cow.html "enum alloc::borrow::Cow")
<'b, [str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>,

Adds a custom header with name `name` and value `value` to `self`. If `self` already contains headers with the name `name`, another header with the same `name` and `value` is added. The type of `header` can be any type implements `Into<Header>`. This includes `Header` itself, [`ContentType`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html "struct rocket::http::ContentType")
 and [`hyper::header` types](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/hyper/header/index.html "mod rocket::http::hyper::header")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-11)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::Header;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.adjoin_raw_header("X-Custom", "one");
    response.adjoin_raw_header("X-Custom", "two");
    
    let mut custom_headers = response.headers().iter();
    assert_eq!(custom_headers.next(), Some(Header::new("X-Custom", "one")));
    assert_eq!(custom_headers.next(), Some(Header::new("X-Custom", "two")));
    assert_eq!(custom_headers.next(), None);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#774-776)

#### pub fn [remove\_header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.remove_header)
(&mut self, name: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
)

Removes all headers with the name `name`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-12)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    
    response.adjoin_raw_header("X-Custom", "one");
    response.adjoin_raw_header("X-Custom", "two");
    response.adjoin_raw_header("X-Other", "hi");
    assert_eq!(response.headers().len(), 3);
    
    response.remove_header("X-Custom");
    assert_eq!(response.headers().len(), 1);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#796-798)

#### pub fn [body](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.body)
(&self) -> &[Body](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Body.html "struct rocket::response::Body")
<'r>

Returns an immutable borrow of the body of `self`, if there is one.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-13)
Example

    use std::io::Cursor;
    use rocket::Response;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    assert!(response.body().is_none());
    
    let string = "Hello, world!";
    response.set_sized_body(string.len(), Cursor::new(string));
    assert!(response.body().is_some());

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#857-859)

#### pub fn [upgrade](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.upgrade)
(&mut self, proto: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[Pin](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/pin/struct.Pin.html "struct core::pin::Pin")
<&mut (dyn [IoHandler](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.IoHandler.html "trait rocket::data::IoHandler")
 + 'r)>>

Returns the [`IoHandler`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.IoHandler.html "trait rocket::data::IoHandler")
 for the protocol `proto`.

Returns `Some` if such a handler was registered via [`Response::add_upgrade()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.add_upgrade "method rocket::Response::add_upgrade")
 or the corresponding builder method [`upgrade()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Builder.html#method.upgrade "method rocket::response::Builder::upgrade")
. Otherwise returns `None`.

    use std::pin::Pin;
    
    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::data::{IoHandler, IoStream};
    use rocket::tokio::io;
    
    struct EchoHandler;
    
    #[rocket::async_trait]
    impl IoHandler for EchoHandler {
        async fn io(self: Pin<Box<Self>>, io: IoStream) -> io::Result<()> {
            let (mut reader, mut writer) = io::split(io);
            io::copy(&mut reader, &mut writer).await?;
            Ok(())
        }
    }
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    assert!(response.upgrade("raw-echo").is_none());
    
    response.add_upgrade("raw-echo", EchoHandler);
    assert!(response.upgrade("raw-echo").is_some());

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#881-883)

#### pub fn [body\_mut](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.body_mut)
(&mut self) -> &mut [Body](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Body.html "struct rocket::response::Body")
<'r>

Returns a mutable borrow of the body of `self`, if there is one. A mutable borrow allows for reading the body.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-14)
Example

    use std::io::Cursor;
    use rocket::Response;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    assert!(response.body().is_none());
    
    let string = "Hello, world!";
    response.set_sized_body(string.len(), Cursor::new(string));
    let string = response.body_mut().to_string().await;
    assert_eq!(string.unwrap(), "Hello, world!");

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#913-918)

#### pub fn [set\_sized\_body](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_sized_body)
<B, S>(&mut self, size: S, body: B)

where B: [AsyncRead](https://docs.rs/tokio/1.48.0/tokio/io/async_read/trait.AsyncRead.html "trait tokio::io::async_read::AsyncRead")
 + [AsyncSeek](https://docs.rs/tokio/1.48.0/tokio/io/async_seek/trait.AsyncSeek.html "trait tokio::io::async_seek::AsyncSeek")
 + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'r, S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
\>>,

Sets the body of `self` to be the fixed-sized `body` with size `size`, which may be `None`. If `size` is `None`, the body’s size will be computing with calls to `seek` just before being written out in a response.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-15)
Example

    use std::io;
    use rocket::Response;
    
    let string = "Hello, world!";
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.set_sized_body(string.len(), io::Cursor::new(string));
    assert_eq!(response.body_mut().to_string().await?, "Hello, world!");

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#941-945)

#### pub fn [set\_streamed\_body](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_streamed_body)
<B>(&mut self, body: B)

where B: [AsyncRead](https://docs.rs/tokio/1.48.0/tokio/io/async_read/trait.AsyncRead.html "trait tokio::io::async_read::AsyncRead")
 + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'r,

Sets the body of `self` to `body`, which will be streamed.

The max chunk size is configured via [`Response::set_max_chunk_size()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_max_chunk_size "method rocket::Response::set_max_chunk_size")
 and defaults to [`Body::DEFAULT_MAX_CHUNK`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Body.html#associatedconstant.DEFAULT_MAX_CHUNK "associated constant rocket::response::Body::DEFAULT_MAX_CHUNK")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-16)
Example

    use tokio::io::{repeat, AsyncReadExt};
    use rocket::Response;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.set_streamed_body(repeat(97).take(5));
    assert_eq!(response.body_mut().to_string().await?, "aaaaa");

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#990-994)

#### pub fn [add\_upgrade](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.add_upgrade)
<N, H>(&mut self, protocol: N, handler: H)

where N: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Uncased](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uncased/struct.Uncased.html "struct rocket::http::uncased::Uncased")
<'r>>, H: [IoHandler](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.IoHandler.html "trait rocket::data::IoHandler")
 + 'r,

Registers `handler` as the I/O handler for upgrade protocol `protocol`.

Responses registering I/O handlers for upgraded protocols **should not** set the response status to `101`, nor set the `Connection` or `Upgrade` headers. Rocket automatically sets these headers as needed. See [`Response`#upgrading](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#upgrading "struct rocket::Response")
 for details.

If a handler was previously registered for `protocol`, this `handler` replaces it. If the connection is upgraded to `protocol`, the last `handler` registered for the protocol is used to handle the connection. See [`IoHandler`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.IoHandler.html "trait rocket::data::IoHandler")
 for details on implementing an I/O handler. For details on connection upgrading, see [`Response`#upgrading](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#upgrading "struct rocket::Response")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-17)
Example

    use std::pin::Pin;
    
    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::data::{IoHandler, IoStream};
    use rocket::tokio::io;
    
    struct EchoHandler;
    
    #[rocket::async_trait]
    impl IoHandler for EchoHandler {
        async fn io(self: Pin<Box<Self>>, io: IoStream) -> io::Result<()> {
            let (mut reader, mut writer) = io::split(io);
            io::copy(&mut reader, &mut writer).await?;
            Ok(())
        }
    }
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    assert!(response.upgrade("raw-echo").is_none());
    
    response.add_upgrade("raw-echo", EchoHandler);
    assert!(response.upgrade("raw-echo").is_some());

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#1022-1024)

#### pub fn [set\_max\_chunk\_size](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_max_chunk_size)
(&mut self, size: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
)

Sets the body’s maximum chunk size to `size` bytes.

The default max chunk size is [`Body::DEFAULT_MAX_CHUNK`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Body.html#associatedconstant.DEFAULT_MAX_CHUNK "associated constant rocket::response::Body::DEFAULT_MAX_CHUNK")
. The max chunk size is a property of the body and is thus reset whenever a body is set via [`Response::set_streamed_body()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_streamed_body "method rocket::Response::set_streamed_body")
, [`Response::set_sized_body()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.set_sized_body "method rocket::Response::set_sized_body")
, or the corresponding builder methods.

This setting does not typically need to be changed. Configuring a high value can result in high memory usage. Similarly, configuring a low value can result in excessive network writes. When unsure, leave the value unchanged.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-18)
Example

    use tokio::io::{repeat, AsyncReadExt};
    use rocket::Response;
    
    let mut response = Response::new();
    response.set_streamed_body(repeat(97).take(5));
    response.set_max_chunk_size(3072);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#1057-1069)

#### pub fn [merge](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.merge)
(&mut self, other: [Response](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html "struct rocket::Response")
<'r>)

Replaces this response’s status and body with that of `other`, if they exist in `other`. Any headers that exist in `other` replace the ones in `self`. Any in `self` that aren’t in `other` remain in `self`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-19)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::{Status, ContentType};
    
    let base = Response::build()
        .status(Status::NotFound)
        .header(ContentType::HTML)
        .raw_header("X-Custom", "value 1")
        .finalize();
    
    let response = Response::build()
        .status(Status::ImATeapot)
        .raw_header("X-Custom", "value 2")
        .raw_header_adjoin("X-Custom", "value 3")
        .merge(base)
        .finalize();
    
    assert_eq!(response.status(), Status::NotFound);
    
    let ctype: Vec<_> = response.headers().get("Content-Type").collect();
    assert_eq!(ctype, vec![ContentType::HTML.to_string()]);
    
    let custom_values: Vec<_> = response.headers().get("X-Custom").collect();
    assert_eq!(custom_values, vec!["value 1"]);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/response.rs.html#1102-1114)

#### pub fn [join](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.join)
(&mut self, other: [Response](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html "struct rocket::Response")
<'r>)

Sets `self`’s status and body to that of `other` if they are not already set in `self`. Any headers present in both `other` and `self` are adjoined.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-20)
Example

    use rocket::Response;
    use rocket::http::{Status, ContentType};
    
    let other = Response::build()
        .status(Status::NotFound)
        .header(ContentType::HTML)
        .raw_header("X-Custom", "value 1")
        .finalize();
    
    let response = Response::build()
        .status(Status::ImATeapot)
        .raw_header("X-Custom", "value 2")
        .raw_header_adjoin("X-Custom", "value 3")
        .join(other)
        .finalize();
    
    assert_eq!(response.status(), Status::ImATeapot);
    
    let ctype: Vec<_> = response.headers().get("Content-Type").collect();
    assert_eq!(ctype, vec![ContentType::HTML.to_string()]);
    
    let custom_values: Vec<_> = response.headers().get("X-Custom").collect();
    assert_eq!(custom_values, vec!["value 2", "value 3", "value 1"]);

#### fn [into\_collection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/ext/trait.IntoCollection.html#tymethod.into_collection)
<A>(self) -> [SmallVec](https://docs.rs/smallvec/1.15.1/smallvec/struct.SmallVec.html "struct smallvec::SmallVec")
<A>

where A: [Array](https://docs.rs/smallvec/1.15.1/smallvec/trait.Array.html "trait smallvec::Array")
<Item = T>,

Converts `self` into a collection.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-21)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-22)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-41)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.on_primary)

#### fn [on\_primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.on_primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`bg()`](self::bg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-42)
Example

    println!("{}", value.on_primary());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.on_fixed)

#### fn [on\_fixed](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.on_fixed)
(&self, color: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-61)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-62)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-71)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.mask)

#### fn [mask](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.mask)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())
 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-72)
Example

    println!("{}", value.mask());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html#example-79)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)

# Request in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Request](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#)

------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Struct Request Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#28-34)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    pub struct Request<'r> { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

The type of an incoming web request.

This should be used sparingly in Rocket applications. In particular, it should likely only be used when writing [`FromRequest`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromRequest.html "trait rocket::request::FromRequest")
 implementations. It contains all of the information for a given web request except for the body data. This includes the HTTP method, URI, cookies, headers, and more.

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#implementations)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#81-919)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#impl-Request%3C'r%3E)

### impl<'r> [Request](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html "struct rocket::Request")
<'r>

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#123-125)

#### pub fn [method](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.method)
(&self) -> [Method](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/enum.Method.html "enum rocket::http::Method")

Retrieve the method from `self`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example)
Example

    use rocket::http::Method;
    
    assert_eq!(get("/").method(), Method::Get);
    assert_eq!(post("/").method(), Method::Post);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#143-145)

#### pub fn [set\_method](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.set_method)
(&mut self, method: [Method](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/enum.Method.html "enum rocket::http::Method")
)

Set the method of `self` to `method`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::http::Method;
    
    assert_eq!(request.method(), Method::Get);
    
    request.set_method(Method::Post);
    assert_eq!(request.method(), Method::Post);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#158-160)

#### pub fn [uri](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.uri)
(&self) -> &[Origin](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Origin.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Origin")
<'r>

Borrow the [`Origin`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Origin.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Origin")
 URI from `self`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-2)
Example

    assert_eq!(get("/hello/rocketeer").uri().path(), "/hello/rocketeer");
    assert_eq!(get("/hello").uri().query(), None);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#183-185)

#### pub fn [set\_uri](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.set_uri)
(&mut self, uri: [Origin](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Origin.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Origin")
<'r>)

Set the URI in `self` to `uri`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-3)
Example

    use rocket::http::uri::Origin;
    
    let uri = Origin::parse("/hello/Sergio?type=greeting").unwrap();
    request.set_uri(uri);
    assert_eq!(request.uri().path(), "/hello/Sergio");
    assert_eq!(request.uri().query().unwrap(), "type=greeting");
    
    let new_uri = request.uri().map_path(|p| format!("/foo{}", p)).unwrap();
    request.set_uri(new_uri);
    assert_eq!(request.uri().path(), "/foo/hello/Sergio");
    assert_eq!(request.uri().query().unwrap(), "type=greeting");

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#275-277)

#### pub fn [host](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.host)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<&[Host](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Host.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Host")
<'r>>

Returns the [`Host`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Host.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Host")
 identified in the request, if any.

If the request is made via HTTP/1.1 (or earlier), this method returns the value in the `HOST` header without the deprecated `user_info` component. Otherwise, this method returns the contents of the `:authority` pseudo-header request field.

Note that this method _only_ reflects the `HOST` header in the _initial_ request and not any changes made thereafter. To change the value returned by this method, use [`Request::set_host()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.set_host "method rocket::Request::set_host")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#-danger-)
⚠️ DANGER ⚠️

Using the user-controlled `host` to construct URLs is a security hazard! _Never_ do so without first validating the host against a whitelist. For this reason, Rocket disallows constructing host-prefixed URIs with [`uri!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/macro.uri.html "macro rocket::uri")
. _Always_ use [`uri!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/macro.uri.html "macro rocket::uri")
 to construct URIs.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-4)
Example

Retrieve the raw host, unusable to construct safe URIs:

    use rocket::http::uri::Host;
    
    assert_eq!(request.host(), None);
    
    request.set_host(Host::from(uri!("rocket.rs")));
    let host = request.host().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(host.domain(), "rocket.rs");
    assert_eq!(host.port(), None);
    
    request.set_host(Host::from(uri!("rocket.rs:2392")));
    let host = request.host().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(host.domain(), "rocket.rs");
    assert_eq!(host.port(), Some(2392));

Retrieve the raw host, check it against a whitelist, and construct a URI:

    use rocket::http::uri::Host;
    
    // A sensitive URI we want to prefix with safe hosts.
    #[get("/token?<secret>")]
    fn token(secret: Token) { /* .. */ }
    
    // Whitelist of known hosts. In a real setting, you might retrieve this
    // list from config at ignite-time using tools like `AdHoc::config()`.
    const WHITELIST: [Host<'static>; 3] = [\
        Host::new(uri!("rocket.rs")),\
        Host::new(uri!("rocket.rs:443")),\
        Host::new(uri!("guide.rocket.rs:443")),\
    ];
    
    // A request with a host of "rocket.rs". Note the case-insensitivity.
    request.set_host(Host::from(uri!("ROCKET.rs")));
    let prefix = request.host().and_then(|h| h.to_absolute("https", &WHITELIST));
    
    // `rocket.rs` is in the whitelist, so we'll get back a `Some`.
    assert!(prefix.is_some());
    if let Some(prefix) = prefix {
        // We can use this prefix to safely construct URIs.
        let uri = uri!(prefix, token("some-secret-token"));
        assert_eq!(uri, "https://ROCKET.rs/token?secret=some-secret-token");
    }
    
    // A request with a host of "attacker-controlled.com".
    request.set_host(Host::from(uri!("attacker-controlled.com")));
    let prefix = request.host().and_then(|h| h.to_absolute("https", &WHITELIST));
    
    // `attacker-controlled.come` is _not_ on the whitelist.
    assert!(prefix.is_none());
    assert!(request.host().is_some());

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#300-302)

#### pub fn [set\_host](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.set_host)
(&mut self, host: [Host](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Host.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Host")
<'r>)

Sets the host of `self` to `host`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-5)
Example

Set the host to `rocket.rs:443`.

    use rocket::http::uri::Host;
    
    assert_eq!(request.host(), None);
    
    request.set_host(Host::from(uri!("rocket.rs:443")));
    let host = request.host().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(host.domain(), "rocket.rs");
    assert_eq!(host.port(), Some(443));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#333-335)

#### pub fn [remote](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.remote)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[SocketAddr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/net/socket_addr/enum.SocketAddr.html "enum core::net::socket_addr::SocketAddr")
\>

Returns the raw address of the remote connection that initiated this request if the address is known. If the address is not known, `None` is returned.

Because it is common for proxies to forward connections for clients, the remote address may contain information about the proxy instead of the client. For this reason, proxies typically set a “X-Real-IP” header [`ip_header`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.ip_header "field rocket::Config::ip_header")
 with the client’s true IP. To extract this IP from the request, use the [`real_ip()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.real_ip)
 or [`client_ip()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.client_ip)
 methods.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-6)
Example

    use std::net::{SocketAddrV4, Ipv4Addr};
    
    assert_eq!(request.remote(), None);
    
    let localhost = SocketAddrV4::new(Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST, 8000).into();
    request.set_remote(localhost);
    assert_eq!(request.remote(), Some(localhost));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#356-358)

#### pub fn [set\_remote](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.set_remote)
(&mut self, address: [SocketAddr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/net/socket_addr/enum.SocketAddr.html "enum core::net::socket_addr::SocketAddr")
)

Sets the remote address of `self` to `address`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-7)
Example

Set the remote address to be 127.0.0.1:8000:

    use std::net::{SocketAddrV4, Ipv4Addr};
    
    assert_eq!(request.remote(), None);
    
    let localhost = SocketAddrV4::new(Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST, 8000).into();
    request.set_remote(localhost);
    assert_eq!(request.remote(), Some(localhost));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#378-387)

#### pub fn [real\_ip](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.real_ip)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[IpAddr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/net/ip_addr/enum.IpAddr.html "enum core::net::ip_addr::IpAddr")
\>

Returns the IP address of the configured [`ip_header`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.ip_header "field rocket::Config::ip_header")
 of the request if such a header is configured, exists and contains a valid IP address.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-8)
Example

    use std::net::Ipv4Addr;
    use rocket::http::Header;
    
    assert_eq!(req.real_ip(), None);
    
    // `ip_header` defaults to `X-Real-IP`.
    let req = req.header(Header::new("X-Real-IP", "127.0.0.1"));
    assert_eq!(req.real_ip(), Some(Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST.into()));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#430-432)

#### pub fn [client\_ip](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.client_ip)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[IpAddr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/net/ip_addr/enum.IpAddr.html "enum core::net::ip_addr::IpAddr")
\>

Attempts to return the client’s IP address by first inspecting the [`ip_header`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.ip_header "field rocket::Config::ip_header")
 and then using the remote connection’s IP address. Note that the built-in `IpAddr` request guard can be used to retrieve the same information in a handler:

    use std::net::IpAddr;
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn get_ip(client_ip: IpAddr) { /* ... */ }
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn try_get_ip(client_ip: Option<IpAddr>) { /* ... */ }

If the `ip_header` exists and contains a valid IP address, that address is returned. Otherwise, if the address of the remote connection is known, that address is returned. Otherwise, `None` is returned.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-9)
Example

    
    // starting without an "X-Real-IP" header or remote address
    assert!(request.client_ip().is_none());
    
    // add a remote address; this is done by Rocket automatically
    request.set_remote("127.0.0.1:8000".parse().unwrap());
    assert_eq!(request.client_ip(), Some("127.0.0.1".parse().unwrap()));
    
    // now with an X-Real-IP header, the default value for `ip_header`.
    request.add_header(Header::new("X-Real-IP", "8.8.8.8"));
    assert_eq!(request.client_ip(), Some("8.8.8.8".parse().unwrap()));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#456-458)

#### pub fn [cookies](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.cookies)
(&self) -> &[CookieJar](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.CookieJar.html "struct rocket::http::CookieJar")
<'r>

Returns a wrapped borrow to the cookies in `self`.

[`CookieJar`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.CookieJar.html "struct rocket::http::CookieJar")
 implements internal mutability, so this method allows you to get _and_ add/remove cookies in `self`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-10)
Example

Add a new cookie to a request’s cookies:

    use rocket::http::Cookie;
    
    req.cookies().add(("key", "val"));
    req.cookies().add(("ans", format!("life: {}", 38 + 4)));
    
    assert_eq!(req.cookies().get_pending("key").unwrap().value(), "val");
    assert_eq!(req.cookies().get_pending("ans").unwrap().value(), "life: 42");

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#475-477)

#### pub fn [headers](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.headers)
(&self) -> &[HeaderMap](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.HeaderMap.html "struct rocket::http::HeaderMap")
<'r>

Returns a [`HeaderMap`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.HeaderMap.html "struct rocket::http::HeaderMap")
 of all of the headers in `self`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-11)
Example

    use rocket::http::{Accept, ContentType};
    
    assert!(get("/").headers().is_empty());
    
    let req = get("/").header(Accept::HTML).header(ContentType::HTML);
    assert_eq!(req.headers().len(), 2);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#498-502)

#### pub fn [add\_header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.add_header)
<'h: 'r, H: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Header.html "struct rocket::http::Header")
<'h>>>(&mut self, header: H)

Add `header` to `self`’s headers. The type of `header` can be any type that implements the `Into<Header>` trait. This includes common types such as [`ContentType`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html "struct rocket::http::ContentType")
 and [`Accept`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Accept.html "struct rocket::http::Accept")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-12)
Example

    use rocket::http::ContentType;
    
    assert!(request.headers().is_empty());
    
    request.add_header(ContentType::HTML);
    assert!(request.headers().contains("Content-Type"));
    assert_eq!(request.headers().len(), 1);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#527-531)

#### pub fn [replace\_header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.replace_header)
<'h: 'r, H: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Header.html "struct rocket::http::Header")
<'h>>>(&mut self, header: H)

Replaces the value of the header with name `header.name` with `header.value`. If no such header exists, `header` is added as a header to `self`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-13)
Example

    use rocket::http::ContentType;
    
    assert!(request.headers().is_empty());
    
    request.add_header(ContentType::Any);
    assert_eq!(request.headers().get_one("Content-Type"), Some("*/*"));
    assert_eq!(request.content_type(), Some(&ContentType::Any));
    
    request.replace_header(ContentType::PNG);
    assert_eq!(request.headers().get_one("Content-Type"), Some("image/png"));
    assert_eq!(request.content_type(), Some(&ContentType::PNG));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#549-553)

#### pub fn [content\_type](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.content_type)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<&[ContentType](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html "struct rocket::http::ContentType")
\>

Returns the Content-Type header of `self`. If the header is not present, returns `None`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-14)
Example

    use rocket::http::ContentType;
    
    assert_eq!(get("/").content_type(), None);
    
    let req = get("/").header(ContentType::JSON);
    assert_eq!(req.content_type(), Some(&ContentType::JSON));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#569-573)

#### pub fn [accept](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.accept)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<&[Accept](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Accept.html "struct rocket::http::Accept")
\>

Returns the Accept header of `self`. If the header is not present, returns `None`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-15)
Example

    use rocket::http::Accept;
    
    assert_eq!(get("/").accept(), None);
    assert_eq!(get("/").header(Accept::JSON).accept(), Some(&Accept::JSON));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#610-622)

#### pub fn [format](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.format)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<&[MediaType](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.MediaType.html "struct rocket::http::MediaType")
\>

Returns the media type “format” of the request.

The “format” of a request is either the Content-Type, if the request methods indicates support for a payload, or the preferred media type in the Accept header otherwise. If the method indicates no payload and no Accept header is specified, a media type of `Any` is returned.

The media type returned from this method is used to match against the `format` route attribute.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-16)
Example

    use rocket::http::{Accept, ContentType, MediaType};
    
    // Non-payload-bearing: format is accept header.
    let req = get("/").header(Accept::HTML);
    assert_eq!(req.format(), Some(&MediaType::HTML));
    
    let req = get("/").header(ContentType::JSON).header(Accept::HTML);
    assert_eq!(req.format(), Some(&MediaType::HTML));
    
    // Payload: format is content-type header.
    let req = post("/").header(ContentType::HTML);
    assert_eq!(req.format(), Some(&MediaType::HTML));
    
    let req = post("/").header(ContentType::JSON).header(Accept::HTML);
    assert_eq!(req.format(), Some(&MediaType::JSON));
    
    // Non-payload-bearing method and no accept header: `Any`.
    assert_eq!(get("/").format(), Some(&MediaType::Any));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#643-645)

#### pub fn [rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.rocket)
(&self) -> &'r [Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
<[Orbit](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/enum.Orbit.html "enum rocket::Orbit")
\>

Returns the [`Rocket`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::Rocket")
 instance that is handling this request.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-17)
Example

    // Retrieve the application config via `Rocket::config()`.
    let config = request.rocket().config();
    
    // Retrieve managed state via `Rocket::state()`.
    let state = request.rocket().state::<Pool>();
    
    // Get a list of all of the registered routes and catchers.
    let routes = request.rocket().routes();
    let catchers = request.rocket().catchers();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#672-674)

#### pub fn [limits](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.limits)
(&self) -> &'r [Limits](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/struct.Limits.html "struct rocket::data::Limits")

Returns the configured application data limits.

This is convenience function equivalent to:

    &request.rocket().config().limits

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-18)
Example

    use rocket::data::ToByteUnit;
    
    // This is the default `form` limit.
    assert_eq!(request.limits().get("form"), Some(32.kibibytes()));
    
    // Retrieve the limit for files with extension `.pdf`; etails to 1MiB.
    assert_eq!(request.limits().get("file/pdf"), Some(1.mebibytes()));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#690-692)

#### pub fn [route](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.route)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<&'r [Route](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::Route")
\>

Get the presently matched route, if any.

This method returns `Some` any time a handler or its guards are being invoked. This method returns `None` _before_ routing has commenced; this includes during request fairing callbacks.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-19)
Example

    let route = request.route();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#709-713)

#### pub fn [guard](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.guard)
<'z, 'a, T>(&'a self) -> [BoxFuture](https://docs.rs/futures-core/0.3.31/futures_core/future/type.BoxFuture.html "type futures_core::future::BoxFuture")
<'z, [Outcome](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/type.Outcome.html "type rocket::request::Outcome")
<T, T::[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromRequest.html#associatedtype.Error "type rocket::request::FromRequest::Error")
\>>

where T: [FromRequest](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromRequest.html "trait rocket::request::FromRequest")
<'a> + 'z, 'a: 'z, 'r: 'z,

Invokes the request guard implementation for `T`, returning its outcome.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-20)
Example

Assuming a `User` request guard exists, invoke it:

    let outcome = request.guard::<User>().await;

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#741-750)

#### pub fn [local\_cache](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.local_cache)
<T, F>(&self, f: F) -> [&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
() -> T, T: [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + 'static,

Retrieves the cached value for type `T` from the request-local cached state of `self`. If no such value has previously been cached for this request, `f` is called to produce the value which is subsequently returned.

Different values of the same type _cannot_ be cached without using a proxy, wrapper type. To avoid the need to write these manually, or for libraries wishing to store values of public types, use the [`local_cache!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/macro.local_cache.html "macro rocket::request::local_cache")
 or [`local_cache_once!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/macro.local_cache_once.html "macro rocket::request::local_cache_once")
 macros to generate a locally anonymous wrapper type, store, and retrieve the wrapped value from request-local cache.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-21)
Example

    // The first store into local cache for a given type wins.
    let value = request.local_cache(|| "hello");
    assert_eq!(*request.local_cache(|| "hello"), "hello");
    
    // The following return the cached, previously stored value for the type.
    assert_eq!(*request.local_cache(|| "goodbye"), "hello");

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#775-786)

#### pub async fn [local\_cache\_async](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.local_cache_async)
<'a, T, F>(&'a self, fut: F) -> [&'a T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)

where F: [Future](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/future/future/trait.Future.html "trait core::future::future::Future")
<Output = T>, T: [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + 'static,

Retrieves the cached value for type `T` from the request-local cached state of `self`. If no such value has previously been cached for this request, `fut` is `await`ed to produce the value which is subsequently returned.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-22)
Example

    async fn current_user<'r>(request: &Request<'r>) -> User {
        // validate request for a given user, load from database, etc
    }
    
    let current_user = request.local_cache_async(async {
        current_user(&request).await
    }).await;

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#817-821)

#### pub fn [param](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.param)
<'a, T>(&'a self, n: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/result/enum.Result.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::result::Result")
<T, T::[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromParam.html#associatedtype.Error "type rocket::request::FromParam::Error")
\>>

where T: [FromParam](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromParam.html "trait rocket::request::FromParam")
<'a>,

Retrieves and parses into `T` the 0-indexed `n`th non-empty segment from the _routed_ request, that is, the `n`th segment _after_ the mount point. If the request has not been routed, then this is simply the `n`th non-empty request URI segment.

Returns `None` if `n` is greater than the number of non-empty segments. Returns `Some(Err(T::Error))` if the parameter type `T` failed to be parsed from the `n`th dynamic parameter.

This method exists only to be used by manual routing. To retrieve parameters from a request, use Rocket’s code generation facilities.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-23)
Example

    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").param(0), Some(Ok("a")));
    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").param(1), Some(Ok("b")));
    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").param(2), Some(Ok("c")));
    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").param::<&str>(3), None);
    
    assert_eq!(get("/1/b/3").param(0), Some(Ok(1)));
    assert!(get("/1/b/3").param::<usize>(1).unwrap().is_err());
    assert_eq!(get("/1/b/3").param(2), Some(Ok(3)));
    
    assert_eq!(get("/").param::<&str>(0), None);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#855-859)

#### pub fn [segments](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.segments)
<'a, T>(&'a self, n: [RangeFrom](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/struct.RangeFrom.html "struct rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::RangeFrom")
<[usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html)
\>) -> [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/result/enum.Result.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::result::Result")
<T, T::[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromSegments.html#associatedtype.Error "type rocket::request::FromSegments::Error")
\>

where T: [FromSegments](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromSegments.html "trait rocket::request::FromSegments")
<'a>,

Retrieves and parses into `T` all of the path segments in the request URI beginning and including the 0-indexed `n`th non-empty segment _after_ the mount point.,that is, the `n`th segment _after_ the mount point. If the request has not been routed, then this is simply the `n`th non-empty request URI segment.

`T` must implement [`FromSegments`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromSegments.html "trait rocket::request::FromSegments")
, which is used to parse the segments. If there are no non-empty segments, the `Segments` iterator will be empty.

This method exists only to be used by manual routing. To retrieve segments from a request, use Rocket’s code generation facilities.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-24)
Example

    use std::path::PathBuf;
    
    assert_eq!(get("/").segments(0..), Ok(PathBuf::new()));
    assert_eq!(get("/").segments(2..), Ok(PathBuf::new()));
    
    // Empty segments are skipped.
    assert_eq!(get("///").segments(2..), Ok(PathBuf::new()));
    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").segments(0..), Ok(PathBuf::from("a/b/c")));
    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").segments(1..), Ok(PathBuf::from("b/c")));
    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").segments(2..), Ok(PathBuf::from("c")));
    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").segments(3..), Ok(PathBuf::new()));
    assert_eq!(get("/a/b/c").segments(4..), Ok(PathBuf::new()));

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/request/request.rs.html#904-918)

#### pub fn [query\_value](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.query_value)
<'a, T>(&'a self, name: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/type.Result.html "type rocket::form::Result")
<'a, T>>

where T: [FromForm](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html "trait rocket::form::FromForm")
<'a>,

Retrieves and parses into `T` the query value with field name `name`. `T` must implement [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html "trait rocket::form::FromForm")
, which is used to parse the query’s value. Key matching is performed case-sensitively.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#warning)
Warning

This method exists _only_ to be used by manual routing and should _never_ be used in a regular Rocket application. It is much more expensive to use this method than to retrieve query parameters via Rocket’s codegen. To retrieve query values from a request, _always_ prefer to use Rocket’s code generation facilities.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#error)
Error

If a query segment with name `name` isn’t present, returns `None`. If parsing the value fails, returns `Some(Err(_))`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-25)
Example

    use rocket::form::FromForm;
    
    #[derive(Debug, PartialEq, FromForm)]
    struct Dog<'r> {
        name: &'r str,
        age: usize
    }
    
    let req = get("/?a=apple&z=zebra&a=aardvark");
    assert_eq!(req.query_value::<&str>("a").unwrap(), Ok("apple"));
    assert_eq!(req.query_value::<&str>("z").unwrap(), Ok("zebra"));
    assert_eq!(req.query_value::<&str>("b"), None);
    
    let a_seq = req.query_value::<Vec<&str>>("a");
    assert_eq!(a_seq.unwrap().unwrap(), ["apple", "aardvark"]);
    
    let req = get("/?dog.name=Max+Fido&dog.age=3");
    let dog = req.query_value::<Dog>("dog");
    assert_eq!(dog.unwrap().unwrap(), Dog { name: "Max Fido", age: 3 });

<Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#)

Instruments this type with the [current](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/span/struct.Span.html#method.current "associated function tracing::span::Span::current")
 [`Span`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/span/struct.Span.html "struct tracing::span::Span")
, returning an `Instrumented` wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.Instrument.html#method.in_current_span)

[Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/convert/mod.rs.html#777)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.into)

#### fn [into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.From.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::From") <T> for U` chooses to do.
