### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#134)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#impl-Paint-for-T)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-26)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-27)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: BrightWhite`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-45)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bright_white());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-46)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-66)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-67)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: RapidBlink`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-72)
Example

    println!("{}", value.rapid_blink());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-76)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#example-84)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

#### fn [with\_subscriber](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_subscriber)
<S>(self, subscriber: S) -> [WithDispatch](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")
<Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#)

where S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Dispatch](https://docs.rs/tracing-core/0.1.36/tracing_core/dispatcher/struct.Dispatch.html "struct tracing_core::dispatcher::Dispatch")
\>,

Attaches the provided [`Subscriber`](https://docs.rs/tracing-core/0.1.36/tracing_core/subscriber/trait.Subscriber.html "trait tracing_core::subscriber::Subscriber")
 to this type, returning a [`WithDispatch`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.with_current_subscriber)

#### fn [with\_current\_subscriber](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_current_subscriber)
(self) -> [WithDispatch](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")
<Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#)

Attaches the current [default](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/dispatcher/index.html#setting-the-default-subscriber "mod tracing::dispatcher")
 [`Subscriber`](https://docs.rs/tracing-core/0.1.36/tracing_core/subscriber/trait.Subscriber.html "trait tracing_core::subscriber::Subscriber")
 to this type, returning a [`WithDispatch`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")
 wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_current_subscriber)

# delete in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[delete](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.delete.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Attribute Macro delete Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_codegen/lib.rs.html#275)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.delete?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    #[delete]

Expand description

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

This and all other route attributes can only be applied to free functions:

    #[get("/")]
    fn index() -> &'static str {
        "Hello, world!"
    }

There are 7 method-specific route attributes:

*   [`get`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.get.html)
     - `GET` specific route
*   [`put`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.put.html)
     - `PUT` specific route
*   [`post`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.post.html)
     - `POST` specific route
*   [`delete`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.delete.html)
     - `DELETE` specific route
*   [`head`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.head.html)
     - `HEAD` specific route
*   [`options`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.options.html)
     - `OPTIONS` specific route
*   [`patch`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.patch.html)
     - `PATCH` specific route

Additionally, [`route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html)
 allows the method and uri to be explicitly specified:

    #[route(GET, uri = "/")]
    fn index() -> &'static str {
        "Hello, world!"
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.delete.html#grammar)
Grammar
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

The grammar for all method-specific route attributes is defined as:

    route := '"' uri ('?' query)? '"' (',' parameter)*
    
    uri := ('/' segment)*
    
    query := segment ('&' segment)*
    
    segment := URI_SEG
             | SINGLE_PARAM
             | TRAILING_PARAM
    
    parameter := 'rank' '=' INTEGER
               | 'format' '=' '"' MEDIA_TYPE '"'
               | 'data' '=' '"' SINGLE_PARAM '"'
    
    SINGLE_PARAM := '<' IDENT '>'
    TRAILING_PARAM := '<' IDENT '..>'
    
    URI_SEG := valid, non-percent-encoded HTTP URI segment
    MEDIA_TYPE := valid HTTP media type or known shorthand
    
    INTEGER := unsigned integer, as defined by Rust
    IDENT := valid identifier, as defined by Rust

The generic route attribute is defined as:

    generic-route := METHOD ',' 'uri' '=' route

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.delete.html#typing-requirements)
Typing Requirements
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Every identifier, except for `_`, that appears in a dynamic parameter (`SINGLE_PARAM` or `TRAILING_PARAM`) must appear as an argument to the function. For example, the following route requires the decorated function to have the arguments `foo`, `baz`, `msg`, `rest`, and `form`:

    #[get("/<foo>/bar/<baz..>?<msg>&closed&<rest..>", data = "<form>")]

The type of each function argument corresponding to a dynamic parameter is required to implement one of Rocket’s guard traits. The exact trait that is required to be implemented depends on the kind of dynamic parameter (`SINGLE` or `TRAILING`) and where in the route attribute the parameter appears. The table below summarizes trait requirements:

| position | kind | trait |
| --- | --- | --- |
| path | `<ident>` | [`FromParam`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromParam.html) |
| path | `<ident..>` | [`FromSegments`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromSegments.html) |
| query | `<ident>` | [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html) |
| query | `<ident..>` | [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html) |
| data | `<ident>` | [`FromData`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.FromData.html) |

The type of each function argument that _does not_ have a corresponding dynamic parameter is required to implement the [`FromRequest`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromRequest.html)
 trait.

A route argument declared a `_` must _not_ appear in the function argument list and has no typing requirements.

The return type of the decorated function must implement the [`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html)
 trait.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.delete.html#semantics)
Semantics
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

The attribute generates three items:

1.  A route [`Handler`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/trait.Handler.html)
    .
    
    The generated handler validates and generates all arguments for the generated function according to the trait that their type must implement. The order in which arguments are processed is:
    
    1.  Request guards from left to right.
        
        If a request guard fails, the request is forwarded if the [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/outcome/enum.Outcome.html)
         is `Forward` or failed if the [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/outcome/enum.Outcome.html)
         is `Error`. See [`FromRequest` Outcomes](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromRequest.html#outcomes)
         for further detail.
        
    2.  Path and query guards in an unspecified order. If a path or query guard fails, the request is forwarded.
        
    3.  Data guard, if any.
        
        If a data guard fails, the request is forwarded if the [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/outcome/enum.Outcome.html)
         is `Forward` or failed if the [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/outcome/enum.Outcome.html)
         is `Error`. See [`FromData`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.FromData.html)
         for further detail.
        
    
    If all validation succeeds, the decorated function is called. The returned value is used to generate a [`Response`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html)
     via the type’s [`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html)
     implementation.
    
2.  A static structure used by [`routes!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/macro.routes.html)
     to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html)
    .
    
    The static structure (and resulting [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html)
    ) is populated with the name (the function’s name), path, query, rank, and format from the route attribute. The handler is set to the generated handler.
    
3.  A macro used by [`uri!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/macro.uri.html)
     to type-check and generate an [`Origin`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Origin.html)
    .

---

# launch in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[launch](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.launch.html#)

--------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Attribute Macro launch Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_codegen/lib.rs.html#478)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.launch?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    #[launch]

Expand description

Generates a `main` function that launches a returned `Rocket<Build>`.

When applied to a function that returns a `Rocket<Build>` instance, `#[launch]` automatically initializes an `async` runtime and launches the function’s returned instance:

    use rocket::{Rocket, Build};
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> Rocket<Build> {
        rocket::build()
    }

This generates code equivalent to the following:

    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() {
        // Recall that an uninspected `Error` will cause a pretty-printed panic,
        // so rest assured errors do not go undetected when using `#[launch]`.
        let _ = rocket().launch().await;
    }

To avoid needing to import _any_ items in the common case, the `launch` attribute will infer a return type written as `_` as `Rocket<Build>`:

    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
    }

The attributed function may be `async`:

    #[launch]
    async fn rocket() -> _ {
        some_async_work().await;
        rocket::build()
    }

---

Summary

    #[route]

Expand description

Attribute to generate a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html)
 and associated metadata.

This and all other route attributes can only be applied to free functions:

    #[get("/")]
    fn index() -> &'static str {
        "Hello, world!"
    }

There are 7 method-specific route attributes:

*   [`get`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.get.html)
     - `GET` specific route
*   [`put`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.put.html)
     - `PUT` specific route
*   [`post`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.post.html)
     - `POST` specific route
*   [`delete`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.delete.html)

*   [`head`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.head.html)

*   [`options`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.options.html)
     - `OPTIONS` specific route
*   [`patch`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.patch.html)
     - `PATCH` specific route

Additionally, [`route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html)
 allows the method and uri to be explicitly specified:

    #[route(GET, uri = "/")]
    fn index() -> &'static str {
        "Hello, world!"
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html#grammar)
Grammar
----------------------------------------------------------------------

The grammar for all method-specific route attributes is defined as:

    route := '"' uri ('?' query)? '"' (',' parameter)*
    
    uri := ('/' segment)*
    
    query := segment ('&' segment)*
    
    segment := URI_SEG
             | SINGLE_PARAM
             | TRAILING_PARAM
    
    parameter := 'rank' '=' INTEGER
               | 'format' '=' '"' MEDIA_TYPE '"'
               | 'data' '=' '"' SINGLE_PARAM '"'
    
    SINGLE_PARAM := '<' IDENT '>'
    TRAILING_PARAM := '<' IDENT '..>'
    
    URI_SEG := valid, non-percent-encoded HTTP URI segment
    MEDIA_TYPE := valid HTTP media type or known shorthand
    
    INTEGER := unsigned integer, as defined by Rust
    IDENT := valid identifier, as defined by Rust

The generic route attribute is defined as:

    generic-route := METHOD ',' 'uri' '=' route

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html#typing-requirements)
Typing Requirements
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Every identifier, except for `_`, that appears in a dynamic parameter (`SINGLE_PARAM` or `TRAILING_PARAM`) must appear as an argument to the function. For example, the following route requires the decorated function to have the arguments `foo`, `baz`, `msg`, `rest`, and `form`:

    #[get("/<foo>/bar/<baz..>?<msg>&closed&<rest..>", data = "<form>")]

The type of each function argument corresponding to a dynamic parameter is required to implement one of Rocket’s guard traits. The exact trait that is required to be implemented depends on the kind of dynamic parameter (`SINGLE` or `TRAILING`) and where in the route attribute the parameter appears. The table below summarizes trait requirements:

| position | kind | trait |
| --- | --- | --- |
| path | `<ident>` | [`FromParam`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromParam.html) |
| path | `<ident..>` | [`FromSegments`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromSegments.html) |
| query | `<ident>` | [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html) |
| query | `<ident..>` | [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html) |
| data | `<ident>` | [`FromData`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.FromData.html) |

The type of each function argument that _does not_ have a corresponding dynamic parameter is required to implement the [`FromRequest`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromRequest.html)
 trait.

A route argument declared a `_` must _not_ appear in the function argument list and has no typing requirements.

The return type of the decorated function must implement the [`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html)
 trait.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.route.html#semantics)
Semantics
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

The attribute generates three items:

1.  A route [`Handler`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/trait.Handler.html)
    .
    
    The generated handler validates and generates all arguments for the generated function according to the trait that their type must implement. The order in which arguments are processed is:
    
    1.  Request guards from left to right.
        
        If a request guard fails, the request is forwarded if the [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/outcome/enum.Outcome.html)
         is `Forward` or failed if the [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/outcome/enum.Outcome.html)
         is `Error`. See [`FromRequest` Outcomes](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/trait.FromRequest.html#outcomes)
         for further detail.
        
    2.  Path and query guards in an unspecified order. If a path or query guard fails, the request is forwarded.
        
    3.  Data guard, if any.
        
        If a data guard fails, the request is forwarded if the [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/outcome/enum.Outcome.html)
         is `Forward` or failed if the [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/outcome/enum.Outcome.html)
         is `Error`. See [`FromData`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/trait.FromData.html)
         for further detail.
        
    
    If all validation succeeds, the decorated function is called. The returned value is used to generate a [`Response`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Response.html)
     via the type’s [`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html)

    .

Summary

Expand description

Types and traits for routes and their request handlers and return types.

Structs[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/index.html#structs)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[Route](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::route::Route")

A request handling route.

[RouteUri](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/struct.RouteUri.html "struct rocket::route::RouteUri")

A route URI which is matched against requests.

Traits[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/index.html#traits)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

[Cloneable](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/trait.Cloneable.html "trait rocket::route::Cloneable")

Helper trait to make a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::Route")
’s `Box<dyn Handler>` `Clone`.

[Handler](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/trait.Handler.html "trait rocket::route::Handler")

Trait implemented by [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::Route")
 request handlers.

Type Aliases[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/index.html#types)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[BoxFuture](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/type.BoxFuture.html "type rocket::route::BoxFuture")

Type alias for the return type of a _raw_ [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::Route")
’s [`Handler`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/trait.Handler.html "trait rocket::route::Handler")
.

[Outcome](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/type.Outcome.html "type rocket::route::Outcome")

Type alias for the return type of a [`Route`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Route.html "struct rocket::Route")
’s [`Handler::handle()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/route/trait.Handler.html#tymethod.handle "method rocket::route::Handler::handle")
.

# Config in rocket - Rust

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

[Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#)

----------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/all.html "show sidebar")

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Struct Config Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#60-138)

[Search](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config?search=)

[Settings](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/settings.html)

[Help](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/help.html)

Summary

    pub struct Config {Show 15 fields
        pub profile: Profile,
        pub address: IpAddr,
        pub port: u16,
        pub workers: usize,
        pub max_blocking: usize,
        pub ident: Ident,
        pub ip_header: Option<Uncased<'static>>,
        pub limits: Limits,
        pub temp_dir: RelativePathBuf,
        pub keep_alive: u32,
        pub tls: Option<TlsConfig>,
        pub secret_key: SecretKey,
        pub shutdown: Shutdown,
        pub log_level: LogLevel,
        pub cli_colors: bool,
        /* private fields */
    }

Expand description

Rocket server configuration.

See the [module level docs](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/index.html "mod rocket::config")
 as well as the [configuration guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/)
 for further details.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#defaults)
Defaults
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

All configuration values have a default, documented in the [fields](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#fields)
 section below. [`Config::debug_default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.debug_default "associated function rocket::Config::debug_default")
 returns the default values for the debug profile while [`Config::release_default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.release_default "associated function rocket::Config::release_default")
 the default values for the release profile. The [`Config::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default "associated function rocket::Config::default")
 method automatically selects the appropriate of the two based on the selected profile. With the exception of `log_level`, which is `normal` in `debug` and `critical` in `release`, and `secret_key`, which is regenerated from a random value if not set in “debug” mode only, all default values are identical in all profiles.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#provider-details)
Provider Details
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`Config` is a Figment [`Provider`](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/provider/trait.Provider.html "trait figment::provider::Provider")
 with the following characteristics:

*   **Profile**
    
    The profile is set to the value of the `profile` field.
    
*   **Metadata**
    
    This provider is named `Rocket Config`. It does not specify a [`Source`](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/metadata/enum.Source.html "enum figment::metadata::Source")
     and uses default interpolation.
    
*   **Data**
    
    The data emitted by this provider are the keys and values corresponding to the fields and values of the structure. The dictionary is emitted to the “default” meta-profile.
    

Note that these behaviors differ from those of [`Config::figment()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.figment "associated function rocket::Config::figment")
.

Fields[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#fields)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.profile)
`profile: [Profile](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/profile/struct.Profile.html "struct figment::profile::Profile") `

The selected profile. **(default: _debug_ `debug` / _release_ `release`)**

_**Note:** This field is never serialized nor deserialized. When a `Config` is merged into a `Figment` as a `Provider`, this profile is selected on the `Figment`. When a `Config` is extracted, this field is set to the extracting Figment’s selected `Profile`._

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.address)
`address: [IpAddr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/net/ip_addr/enum.IpAddr.html "enum core::net::ip_addr::IpAddr") `

IP address to serve on. **(default: `127.0.0.1`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.port)
`port: [u16](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u16.html) `

Port to serve on. **(default: `8000`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.workers)
`workers: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

Number of threads to use for executing futures. **(default: `num_cores`)**

_**Note:** Rocket only reads this value from sources in the [default provider](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.figment "associated function rocket::Config::figment")
._

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.max_blocking)
`max_blocking: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

Limit on threads to start for blocking tasks. **(default: `512`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.ident)
`ident: [Ident](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html "struct rocket::config::Ident") `

How, if at all, to identify the server via the `Server` header. **(default: `"Rocket"`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.ip_header)
`ip_header: [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option") <[Uncased](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uncased/struct.Uncased.html "struct rocket::http::uncased::Uncased") <'static>>`

The name of a header, whose value is typically set by an intermediary server or proxy, which contains the real IP address of the connecting client. Used internally and by [`Request::client_ip()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.client_ip "method rocket::Request::client_ip")
 and [`Request::real_ip()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.real_ip "method rocket::Request::real_ip")
.

To disable using any header for this purpose, set this value to `false`. Deserialization semantics are identical to those of [`Ident`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html "struct rocket::config::Ident")
 except that the value must syntactically be a valid HTTP header name.

**(default: `"X-Real-IP"`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.limits)
`limits: [Limits](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/struct.Limits.html "struct rocket::data::Limits") `

Streaming read size limits. **(default: [`Limits::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/struct.Limits.html#method.default "associated function rocket::data::Limits::default")
)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.temp_dir)
`temp_dir: [RelativePathBuf](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/value/magic/struct.RelativePathBuf.html "struct figment::value::magic::RelativePathBuf") `

Directory to store temporary files in. **(default: [`std::env::temp_dir()`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/env/fn.temp_dir.html "fn std::env::temp_dir")
)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.keep_alive)
`keep_alive: [u32](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u32.html) `

Keep-alive timeout in seconds; disabled when `0`. **(default: `5`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.tls)
`tls: [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option") <[TlsConfig](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.TlsConfig.html "struct rocket::config::TlsConfig") >`

Available on **crate feature `tls`** only.

The TLS configuration, if any. **(default: `None`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.secret_key)
`secret_key: [SecretKey](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey.html "struct rocket::config::SecretKey") `

Available on **crate feature `secrets`** only.

The secret key for signing and encrypting. **(default: `0`)**

_**Note:** This field _always_ serializes as a 256-bit array of `0`s to aid in preventing leakage of the secret key._

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.shutdown)
`shutdown: [Shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::config::Shutdown") `

Graceful shutdown configuration. **(default: [`Shutdown::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html#method.default "associated function rocket::config::Shutdown::default")
)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.log_level)
`log_level: [LogLevel](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/enum.LogLevel.html "enum rocket::config::LogLevel") `

Max level to log. **(default: _debug_ `normal` / _release_ `critical`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.cli_colors)
`cli_colors: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html) `

Whether to use colors and emoji when logging. **(default: `true`)**

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#implementations)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#158-469)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#impl-Config)

### impl [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#183-204)

#### pub fn [debug\_default](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.debug_default)
() -> [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Returns the default configuration for the `debug` profile, _irrespective of the Rust compilation profile_ and `ROCKET_PROFILE`.

This may differ from the configuration used by default, [`Config::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default "associated function rocket::Config::default")
, which is selected based on the Rust compilation profile. See [defaults](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#defaults)
 and [provider details](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#provider-details)
 for specifics.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    
    let config = Config::debug_default();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#221-227)

#### pub fn [release\_default](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.release_default)
() -> [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Returns the default configuration for the `release` profile, _irrespective of the Rust compilation profile_ and `ROCKET_PROFILE`.

This may differ from the configuration used by default, [`Config::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default "associated function rocket::Config::default")
, which is selected based on the Rust compilation profile. See [defaults](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#defaults)
 and [provider details](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#provider-details)
 for specifics.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    
    let config = Config::release_default();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#257-262)

#### pub fn [figment](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.figment)
() -> [Figment](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/figment/struct.Figment.html "struct figment::figment::Figment")

Returns the default provider figment used by [`rocket::build()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fn.build.html "fn rocket::build")
.

The default figment reads from the following sources, in ascending priority order:

1.  [`Config::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default "associated function rocket::Config::default")
     (see [defaults](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#defaults)
    )
2.  `Rocket.toml` _or_ filename in `ROCKET_CONFIG` environment variable
3.  `ROCKET_` prefixed environment variables

The profile selected is the value set in the `ROCKET_PROFILE` environment variable. If it is not set, it defaults to `debug` when compiled in debug mode and `release` when compiled in release mode.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-2)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    use serde::Deserialize;
    
    #[derive(Deserialize)]
    struct MyConfig {
        app_key: String,
    }
    
    let my_config = Config::figment().extract::<MyConfig>();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#280-285)

#### pub fn [try\_from](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.try_from)
<T: [Provider](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/provider/trait.Provider.html "trait figment::provider::Provider")
\>(provider: T) -> [Result](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/error/type.Result.html "type figment::error::Result")
<Self>

Attempts to extract a `Config` from `provider`, returning the result.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-3)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    use rocket::figment::providers::{Toml, Format, Env};
    
    // Use Rocket's default `Figment`, but allow values from `MyApp.toml`
    // and `MY_APP_` prefixed environment variables to supersede its values.
    let figment = Config::figment()
        .merge(("some-thing", 123))
        .merge(Env::prefixed("CONFIG_"));
    
    let config = Config::try_from(figment);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#308-310)

#### pub fn [from](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.from)
<T: [Provider](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/provider/trait.Provider.html "trait figment::provider::Provider")
\>(provider: T) -> Self

Extract a `Config` from `provider`, panicking if extraction fails.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#panics)
Panics

If extraction fails, prints an error message indicating the error and panics. For a version that doesn’t panic, use [`Config::try_from()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.try_from "associated function rocket::Config::try_from")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-4)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    use rocket::figment::providers::{Toml, Format, Env};
    
    // Use Rocket's default `Figment`, but allow values from `MyApp.toml`
    // and `MY_APP_` prefixed environment variables to supersede its values.
    let figment = Config::figment()
        .merge(Toml::file("MyApp.toml").nested())
        .merge(Env::prefixed("MY_APP_"));
    
    let config = Config::from(figment);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#329-335)

#### pub fn [tls\_enabled](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.tls_enabled)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if TLS is enabled.

TLS is enabled when the `tls` feature is enabled and TLS has been configured with at least one ciphersuite. Note that without changing defaults, all supported ciphersuites are enabled in the recommended configuration.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-5)
Example

    let config = rocket::Config::default();
    if config.tls_enabled() {
        println!("TLS is enabled!");
    } else {
        println!("TLS is disabled.");
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#353-363)

#### pub fn [mtls\_enabled](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.mtls_enabled)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if mTLS is enabled.

mTLS is enabled when TLS is enabled ([`Config::tls_enabled()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.tls_enabled "method rocket::Config::tls_enabled")
) _and_ the `mtls` feature is enabled _and_ mTLS has been configured with a CA certificate chain.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-6)
Example

    let config = rocket::Config::default();
    if config.mtls_enabled() {
        println!("mTLS is enabled!");
    } else {
        println!("mTLS is disabled.");
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#472-486)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#impl-Config-1)

### impl [Clone](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/clone/trait.Clone.html "trait core::clone::Clone")
 for [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#59)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.clone)

#### fn [clone](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/clone/trait.Clone.html#tymethod.clone)

### impl [Default](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/default/trait.Default.html "trait core::default::Default")
 for [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#152-155)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default)

#### fn [default](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/default/trait.Default.html#tymethod.default)
() -> [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Returns the default configuration based on the Rust compilation profile. This is [`Config::debug_default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.debug_default "associated function rocket::Config::debug_default")
 in `debug` and [`Config::release_default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.release_default "associated function rocket::Config::release_default")
 in `release`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-7)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    
    let config = Config::default();

#### fn [into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.From.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#134)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#impl-Paint-for-T)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-8)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.primary)

#### fn [primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-9)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-28)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-48)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.bold)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-58)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())
 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-59)
Example

    println!("{}", value.mask());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#example-66)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.new)

<Self>

where Self: [Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

Create a new [`Painted`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
 with a default [`Style`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
. [Read more](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)

<S>(&self, style: S) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<&Self>

where S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Style](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
\>,
