[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/serde/json.rs.html#300-304)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'static%3E-for-Value)

### impl<'r> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'static> for [Value](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/json/enum.Value.html "enum rocket::serde::json::Value")

Available on **crate feature `json`** only.

Serializes the value into JSON. Returns a response with Content-Type JSON and a fixed-size body with the serialized value.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/fs/named_file.rs.html#149-160)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'static%3E-for-NamedFile)

### impl<'r> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'static> for [NamedFile](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fs/struct.NamedFile.html "struct rocket::fs::NamedFile")

Streams the named file to the client. Sets or overrides the Content-Type in the response according to the file’s extension if the extension is recognized. See [`ContentType::from_extension()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html#method.from_extension "associated function rocket::http::ContentType::from_extension")
 for more information. If you would like to stream a file with a different Content-Type than that implied by its extension, use a [`File`](https://docs.rs/tokio/1.48.0/tokio/fs/file/struct.File.html "struct tokio::fs::file::File")
 directly.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/responder.rs.html#534-550)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'static%3E-for-Status)

### impl<'r> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'static> for [Status](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Status.html "struct rocket::http::Status")

The response generated by `Status` depends on the status code itself. The table below summarizes the functionality:

| Status Code Range | Response |
| --- | --- |
| \[400, 599\] | Forwards to catcher for given status. |
| 100, \[200, 205\] | Empty with status of `self`. |
| All others. | Invalid. Errors to `500` catcher. |

In short, a client or server error status codes will forward to the corresponding error catcher, a successful status code less than `206` or `100` responds with any empty body and the given status code, and all other status code emit an error message and forward to the `500` (internal server error) catcher.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/responder.rs.html#352-359)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'static%3E-for-Box%3Cstr%3E)

### impl<'r> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'static> for [Redirect](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Redirect.html "struct rocket::response::Redirect")

Constructs a response with the appropriate status code and the given URL in the `Location` header field. The body of the response is empty. If the URI value used to create the `Responder` is an invalid URI, an error of `Status::InternalServerError` is returned.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'o%3E-for-Created%3CR%3E)

### impl<'r, 'o: 'r, R: [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'o>> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'o> for [Created](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/status/struct.Created.html "struct rocket::response::status::Created")
<R>

Sets the status code of the response to 201 Created. Sets the `Location` header to the parameter in the [`Created::new()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/status/struct.Created.html#method.new "associated function rocket::response::status::Created::new")
 constructor.

The optional responder, set via [`Created::body()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/status/struct.Created.html#method.body "method rocket::response::status::Created::body")
 or [`Created::tagged_body()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/status/struct.Created.html#method.tagged_body "method rocket::response::status::Created::tagged_body")
 finalizes the response if it exists. The wrapped responder should write the body of the response so that it contains information about the created resource. If no responder is provided, the response body will be empty.

In addition to setting the status code, `Location` header, and finalizing the response with the `Responder`, the `ETag` header is set conditionally if a hashable `Responder` is provided via [`Created::tagged_body()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/status/struct.Created.html#method.tagged_body "method rocket::response::status::Created::tagged_body")
. The `ETag` header is set to a hash value of the responder.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/status.rs.html#236-243)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'o%3E-for-Custom%3CR%3E)

### impl<'r, 'o: 'r, R: [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'o>> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'o> for [Flash](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/struct.Flash.html "struct rocket::response::Flash")
<R>

Sets the message cookie and then uses the wrapped responder to complete the response. In other words, simply sets a cookie and delegates the rest of the response handling to the wrapped responder. As a result, the `Outcome` of the response is the `Outcome` of the wrapped `Responder`.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/response/responder.rs.html#440-445)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'o%3E-for-Box%3CT%3E)

### impl<'r, 'o: 'r, T: [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'o> + [Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
\> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'o> for [Box](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/boxed/struct.Box.html "struct rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::boxed::Box")
<T>

Returns the response generated by the inner `T`. Note that this explicitly does not support `Box<dyn Responder>`.

[ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder# "This example deliberately fails to compile")

    #[macro_use] extern crate rocket;
    
    use rocket::response::Responder;
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn f() -> Box<dyn Responder<'static,'static>> {
        Box::new(())
    }

However, this `impl` allows boxing sized responders:

    #[macro_use] extern crate rocket;
    
    #[derive(Responder)]
    enum Content {
        Redirect(Box<rocket::response::Redirect>),
        Text(String),
    }
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn f() -> Option<Box<String>> {
        None
    }
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn g() -> Content {
        Content::Text("hello".to_string())
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/data/capped.rs.html#207-211)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'o%3E-for-Capped%3CT%3E)

### impl<'r, T: [Serialize](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Serialize.html "trait rocket::serde::Serialize")
\> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'static> for [Json](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/json/struct.Json.html "struct rocket::serde::json::Json")
<T>

Available on **crate feature `json`** only.

Serializes the wrapped value into JSON. Returns a response with Content-Type JSON and a fixed-size body with the serialized value. If serialization fails, an `Err` of `Status::InternalServerError` is returned.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/serde/msgpack.rs.html#189-199)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'static%3E-for-MsgPack%3CT%3E)

### impl<'r, T: [Serialize](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Serialize.html "trait rocket::serde::Serialize")
\> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
<'r, 'static> for [MsgPack](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/msgpack/struct.MsgPack.html "struct rocket::serde::msgpack::MsgPack")
<T>

Available on **crate feature `msgpack`** only.

Serializes the wrapped value into MessagePack. Returns a response with Content-Type `MsgPack` and a fixed-size body with the serialization. If serialization fails, an `Err` of `Status::InternalServerError` is returned.

# Database in rocket_db_pools - Rust

[Database](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/derive.Database#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html)

Derive Macro Database Copy item path
====================================

    #[derive(Database)]
    {
        // Attributes available to this derive:
        #[database]
    }
    

Expand description

Automatic derive for the [`Database`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html)
 trait.

    use rocket_db_pools::Database;
    
    #[derive(Database)]
    #[database("database_name")]
    struct Db(PoolType);

The derive generates an implementation of [`Database`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html)
 as follows:

*   [`Database::NAME`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html#associatedconstant.NAME)
     is set to the value in the `#[database("name")]` attribute.
    
    This names the database, providing an anchor to configure the database via `Rocket.toml` or any other configuration source. Specifically, the configuration in `databases.name` is used to configure the driver.
    
*   [`Database::Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html#associatedtype.Pool)
     is set to the wrapped type: `PoolType` above. The type must implement [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html)
    .
    

To meet the required [`Database`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html)
 supertrait bounds, this derive also generates implementations for:

*   `From<Db::Pool>`
    
*   `Deref<Target = Db::Pool>`
    
*   `DerefMut<Target = Db::Pool>`
    
*   `FromRequest<'_> for &Db`
    
*   `Sentinel for &Db`
    

The `Deref` impls enable accessing the database pool directly from references `&Db` or `&mut Db`. To force a dereference to the underlying type, use `&db.0` or `&**db` or their `&mut` variants.

# context in rocket_dyn_templates - Rust

[context](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/macro.context#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_dyn\_templates](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html)

Macro context Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#357-392)

    macro_rules! context {
        ($($key:ident $(: $value:expr)?),*$(,)?) => { ... };
    }

Expand description

A macro to easily create a template rendering context.

Invocations of this macro expand to a value of an anonymous type which implements \[`serde::Serialize`\]. Fields can be literal expressions or variables captured from a surrounding scope, as long as all fields implement `Serialize`.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/macro.context#examples)
Examples
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following code:

    #[get("/<foo>")]
    fn render_index(foo: u64) -> Template {
        Template::render("index", context! {
            // Note that shorthand field syntax is supported.
            // This is equivalent to `foo: foo,`
            foo,
            bar: "Hello world",
        })
    }

is equivalent to the following, but without the need to manually define an `IndexContext` struct:

    #[derive(Serialize)]
    struct IndexContext<'a> {
        foo: u64,
        bar: &'a str,
    }
    
    #[get("/<foo>")]
    fn render_index(foo: u64) -> Template {
        Template::render("index", IndexContext {
            foo,
            bar: "Hello world",
        })
    }

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/macro.context#nesting)
Nesting

Nested objects can be created by nesting calls to `context!`:

    let ctx = context! {
        planet: "Earth",
        info: context! {
            mass: 5.97e24,
            radius: "6371 km",
            moons: 1,
        },
    };

---

# Pool in rocket_db_pools - Rust

[Pool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#)

---------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html)

Trait Pool Copy item path
=========================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/pool.rs.html#117-153)

    pub trait Pool:
        Sized
        + Send
        + Sync
        + 'static {
        type Connection;
        type Error: Error;
    
        // Required methods
        fn init<'life0, 'async_trait>(
            figment: &'life0 Figment,
        ) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Result<Self, Self::Error>> + Send + 'async_trait>>
           where Self: 'async_trait,
                 'life0: 'async_trait;
        fn get<'life0, 'async_trait>(
            &'life0 self,
        ) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Result<Self::Connection, Self::Error>> + Send + 'async_trait>>
           where Self: 'async_trait,
                 'life0: 'async_trait;
        fn close<'life0, 'async_trait>(
            &'life0 self,
        ) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = ()> + Send + 'async_trait>>
           where Self: 'async_trait,
                 'life0: 'async_trait;
    }

Expand description

Generic [`Database`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Database")
 driver connection pool trait.

This trait provides a generic interface to various database pooling implementations in the Rust ecosystem. It can be implemented by anyone, but this crate provides implementations for common drivers.

**Implementations of this trait outside of this crate should be rare. You _do not_ need to implement this trait or understand its specifics to use this crate.**

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#async-trait)
Async Trait

[`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")
 is an _async_ trait. Implementations of `Pool` must be decorated with an attribute of `#[async_trait]`:

    use rocket::figment::Figment;
    use rocket_db_pools::Pool;
    
    #[rocket::async_trait]
    impl Pool for MyPool {
        type Connection = Connection;
    
        type Error = Error;
    
        async fn init(figment: &Figment) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> {
            todo!("initialize and return an instance of the pool");
        }
    
        async fn get(&self) -> Result<Self::Connection, Self::Error> {
            todo!("fetch one connection from the pool");
        }
    
        async fn close(&self) {
            todo!("gracefully shutdown connection pool");
        }
    }

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#implementing)
Implementing

Implementations of `Pool` typically trace the following outline:

1.  The `Error` associated type is set to [`Error`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/enum.Error.html "enum rocket_db_pools::Error")
    .
    
2.  A [`Config`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Config")
     is [extracted](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/figment/struct.Figment.html#method.extract "method rocket_db_pools::figment::Figment::extract")
     from the `figment` passed to init.
    
3.  The pool is initialized and returned in `init()`, wrapping initialization errors in [`Error::Init`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/enum.Error.html#variant.Init "variant rocket_db_pools::Error::Init")
    .
    
4.  A connection is retrieved in `get()`, wrapping errors in [`Error::Get`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/enum.Error.html#variant.Get "variant rocket_db_pools::Error::Get")
    .
    

Concretely, this looks like:

    use rocket::figment::Figment;
    use rocket_db_pools::{Pool, Config, Error};
    
    #[rocket::async_trait]
    impl Pool for MyPool {
        type Connection = Connection;
    
        type Error = Error<InitError, GetError>;
    
        async fn init(figment: &Figment) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> {
            // Extract the config from `figment`.
            let config: Config = figment.extract()?;
    
            // Read config values, initialize `MyPool`. Map errors of type
            // `InitError` to `Error<InitError, _>` with `Error::Init`.
            let pool = MyPool::new(config).map_err(Error::Init)?;
    
            // Return the fully initialized pool.
            Ok(pool)
        }
    
        async fn get(&self) -> Result<Self::Connection, Self::Error> {
            // Get one connection from the pool, here via an `acquire()` method.
            // Map errors of type `GetError` to `Error<_, GetError>`.
            self.acquire().map_err(Error::Get)
        }
    
        async fn close(&self) {
            self.shutdown().await;
        }
    }

Required Associated Types[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#required-associated-types)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/pool.rs.html#119)

#### type [Connection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#associatedtype.Connection)

The connection type managed by this pool, returned by [`Self::get()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html#tymethod.get)
.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/pool.rs.html#122)

#### type [Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#associatedtype.Error)
: [Error](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/error/trait.Error.html "trait core::error::Error")

The error type returned by [`Self::init()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html#tymethod.init)
 and [`Self::get()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html#tymethod.get)
.

Required Methods[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#required-methods)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/pool.rs.html#136)

#### fn [init](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#tymethod.init)
<'life0, 'async\_trait>( figment: &'life0 [Figment](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/figment/struct.Figment.html "struct rocket_db_pools::figment::Figment")
, ) -> [Pin](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/pin/struct.Pin.html "struct core::pin::Pin")
<[Box](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/boxed/struct.Box.html "struct alloc::boxed::Box")
<dyn [Future](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/future/future/trait.Future.html "trait core::future::future::Future")
<Output = [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<Self, Self::[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html#associatedtype.Error "type rocket_db_pools::Pool::Error")
\>> + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'async\_trait>>

where Self: 'async\_trait, 'life0: 'async\_trait,

Constructs a pool from a [Value](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/figment/value/enum.Value.html "enum rocket_db_pools::figment::value::Value")
.

It is up to each implementor of `Pool` to define its accepted configuration value(s) via the `Config` associated type. Most integrations provided in `rocket_db_pools` use [`Config`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Config")
, which accepts a (required) `url` and an (optional) `pool_size`.

###### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#errors)
Errors

This method returns an error if the configuration is not compatible, or if creating a pool failed due to an unavailable database server, insufficient resources, or another database-specific error.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/pool.rs.html#145)

#### fn [get](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#tymethod.get)
<'life0, 'async\_trait>( &'life0 self, ) -> [Pin](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/pin/struct.Pin.html "struct core::pin::Pin")
<[Box](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/boxed/struct.Box.html "struct alloc::boxed::Box")
<dyn [Future](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/future/future/trait.Future.html "trait core::future::future::Future")
<Output = [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<Self::[Connection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html#associatedtype.Connection "type rocket_db_pools::Pool::Connection")
, Self::[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html#associatedtype.Error "type rocket_db_pools::Pool::Error")
\>> + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'async\_trait>>

where Self: 'async\_trait, 'life0: 'async\_trait,

Asynchronously retrieves a connection from the factory or pool.

###### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#errors-1)
Errors

This method returns an error if a connection could not be retrieved, such as a preconfigured timeout elapsing or when the database server is unavailable.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/pool.rs.html#152)

#### fn [close](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#tymethod.close)
<'life0, 'async\_trait>( &'life0 self, ) -> [Pin](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/pin/struct.Pin.html "struct core::pin::Pin")
<[Box](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/boxed/struct.Box.html "struct alloc::boxed::Box")
<dyn [Future](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/future/future/trait.Future.html "trait core::future::future::Future")
<Output = [()](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.unit.html)
\> + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'async\_trait>>

where Self: 'async\_trait, 'life0: 'async\_trait,

Shutdown the connection pool, disallowing any new connections from being retrieved and waking up any tasks with active connections.

The returned future may either resolve when all connections are known to have closed or at any point prior. Details are implementation specific.

Dyn Compatibility[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool#dyn-compatibility)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This trait is **not** [dyn compatible](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/reference/items/traits.html#dyn-compatibility)
.

_In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety", so this trait is not object safe._

# Poolable in rocket_sync_db_pools - Rust

[Poolable](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable#)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_sync\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html)

Trait Poolable Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/poolable.rs.html#100-111)

    pub trait Poolable:
        Send
        + Sized
        + 'static {
        type Manager: ManageConnection<Connection = Self>;
        type Error: Debug;
    
        // Required method
        fn pool(db_name: &str, rocket: &Rocket<Build>) -> PoolResult<Self>;
    }

Expand description

Trait implemented by `r2d2`\-based database adapters.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable#provided-implementations)
Provided Implementations
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Implementations of `Poolable` are provided for the following types:

*   `diesel::MysqlConnection`
*   `diesel::PgConnection`
*   `diesel::SqliteConnection`
*   `postgres::Connection`
*   `rusqlite::Connection`

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable#implementation-guide)
Implementation Guide
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As an r2d2-compatible database (or other resource) adapter provider, implementing `Poolable` in your own library will enable Rocket users to consume your adapter with its built-in connection pooling support.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable#example)
Example

Consider a library `foo` with the following types:

*   `foo::ConnectionManager`, which implements [`r2d2::ManageConnection`](https://docs.rs/r2d2/0.8/r2d2/trait.ManageConnection.html "trait r2d2::ManageConnection")
    
*   `foo::Connection`, the `Connection` associated type of `foo::ConnectionManager`
*   `foo::Error`, errors resulting from manager instantiation

In order for Rocket to generate the required code to automatically provision a r2d2 connection pool into application state, the `Poolable` trait needs to be implemented for the connection type. The following example implements `Poolable` for `foo::Connection`:

    use std::time::Duration;
    use rocket::{Rocket, Build};
    use rocket_sync_db_pools::{r2d2, Error, Config, Poolable, PoolResult};
    
    impl Poolable for foo::Connection {
        type Manager = foo::ConnectionManager;
        type Error = foo::Error;
    
        fn pool(db_name: &str, rocket: &Rocket<Build>) -> PoolResult<Self> {
            let config = Config::from(db_name, rocket)?;
            let manager = foo::ConnectionManager::new(&config.url).map_err(Error::Custom)?;
            Ok(r2d2::Pool::builder()
                .max_size(config.pool_size)
                .connection_timeout(Duration::from_secs(config.timeout as u64))
                .build(manager)?)
        }
    }

In this example, `ConnectionManager::new()` method returns a `foo::Error` on failure. The [`Error`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/enum.Error.html "enum rocket_sync_db_pools::Error")
 enum consolidates this type, the `r2d2::Error` type that can result from `r2d2::Pool::builder()`, and the [`figment::Error`](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/error/struct.Error.html "struct figment::error::Error")
 type from `database::Config::from()`.

In the event that a connection manager isn’t fallible (as is the case with Diesel’s r2d2 connection manager, for instance), the associated error type for the `Poolable` implementation should be `std::convert::Infallible`.

For more concrete example, consult Rocket’s existing implementations of [`Poolable`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable.html "trait rocket_sync_db_pools::Poolable")
.

Required Associated Types[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable#required-associated-types)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/poolable.rs.html#102)

#### type [Manager](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable#associatedtype.Manager)
: [ManageConnection](https://docs.rs/r2d2/0.8/r2d2/trait.ManageConnection.html "trait r2d2::ManageConnection")

: [Debug](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/fmt/trait.Debug.html "trait core::fmt::Debug")

#### fn [pool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable#tymethod.pool)
(db\_name: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)

<[Build](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/enum.Build.html "enum rocket::phase::Build")

<Self>

Creates an `r2d2` connection pool for `Manager::Connection`, returning the pool on success.

Dyn Compatibility[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/trait.Poolable#dyn-compatibility)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This trait is **not** [dyn compatible](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/reference/items/traits.html#dyn-compatibility)
.

_In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety", so this trait is not object safe._

### impl [ExampleDb](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb.html "struct rocket_sync_db_pools::example::ExampleDb")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/lib.rs.html#433-438)

#### pub fn [fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.fairing)
() -> impl [Fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")

Returns a fairing that initializes the database connection pool associated with `Self`.

The fairing _must_ be attached before `Self` can be used as a request guard.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example)
Example

    use rocket_sync_db_pools::diesel;
    
    #[database("my_db")]
    struct MyConn(diesel::SqliteConnection);
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().attach(MyConn::fairing())
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/lib.rs.html#489-496)

#### pub fn [pool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.pool)
<P: [Phase](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::phase::Phase")
\>( \_\_rocket: &[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/rkt/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::rkt::Rocket")
<P>, ) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<&ConnectionPool<Self, [SqliteConnection](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/sqlite/connection/struct.SqliteConnection.html "struct diesel::sqlite::connection::SqliteConnection")
\>>

Returns an opaque type that represents the connection pool backing connections of type `Self` _as long as_ the fairing returned by [`Self::fairing()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb.html#method.fairing "associated function rocket_sync_db_pools::example::ExampleDb::fairing")
 is attached and has run on `__rocket`.

The returned pool is `Clone`. Values of type `Self` can be retrieved from the pool by calling `pool.get().await` which has the same signature and semantics as [`Self::get_one()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb.html#method.get_one "associated function rocket_sync_db_pools::example::ExampleDb::get_one")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::tokio::{task, time};
    use rocket::fairing::AdHoc;
    use rocket_sync_db_pools::diesel;
    
    #[database("my_db")]
    struct MyConn(diesel::SqliteConnection);
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
            .attach(MyConn::fairing())
            .attach(AdHoc::try_on_ignite("Background DB", |rocket| async {
                let pool = match MyConn::pool(&rocket) {
                    Some(pool) => pool.clone(),
                    None => return Err(rocket)
                };
    
                // Start a background task that runs some database
                // operation every 10 seconds. If a connection isn't
                // available, retries 10 + timeout seconds later.
                tokio::task::spawn(async move {
                    loop {
                        time::sleep(time::Duration::from_secs(10)).await;
                        if let Some(conn) = pool.get().await {
                            conn.run(|c| { /* perform db ops */ }).await;
                        }
                    }
                });
    
                Ok(rocket)
            }))
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/lib.rs.html#524-530)

#### pub async fn [run](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.run)
<F, R>(&self, \_\_f: F) -> R

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&mut [SqliteConnection](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/sqlite/connection/struct.SqliteConnection.html "struct diesel::sqlite::connection::SqliteConnection")
) -> R + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'static, R: [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'static,

Runs the provided function `__f` in an async-safe blocking thread. The function is supplied with a mutable reference to the raw connection (a value of type `&mut Self.0`). `.await`ing the return value of this function yields the value returned by `__f`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example-2)
Example

    use rocket_sync_db_pools::diesel;
    
    #[database("my_db")]
    struct MyConn(diesel::SqliteConnection);
    
    #[get("/")]
    async fn f(conn: MyConn) {
        // The type annotation is illustrative and isn't required.
        let result = conn.run(|c: &mut diesel::SqliteConnection| {
            // Use `c`.
        }).await;
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/lib.rs.html#535-543)

#### pub async fn [get\_one](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.get_one)
<P: [Phase](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::phase::Phase")
\>(\_\_rocket: &[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/rkt/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::rkt::Rocket")
<P>) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<Self>

Retrieves a connection of type `Self` from the `rocket` instance. Returns `Some` as long as `Self::fairing()` has been attached and there is a connection available within at most `timeout` seconds.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.into)

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "trait core::convert::From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example-3)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.primary)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example-4)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example-23)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

    println!("{}", value.on_bright_white());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example-43)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example-53)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/example/struct.ExampleDb#example-61)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)

<Self>

,

Create a new [`Painted`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
 with a default [`Style`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
. [Read more](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#126)

#### fn [paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.paint)
<S>(&self, style: S) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<&Self>

where S: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[Style](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
\>,

# Database in rocket_db_pools - Rust

[Database](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html)

Trait Database Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/database.rs.html#36-131)

    pub trait Database:
        From<Self::Pool>
        + DerefMut<Target = Self::Pool>
        + Send
        + Sync
        + 'static {
        type Pool: Pool;
    
        const NAME: &'static str;
    
        // Provided methods
        fn init() -> Initializer<Self> { ... }
        fn fetch<P: Phase>(rocket: &Rocket<P>) -> Option<&Self> { ... }
    }

Expand description

Derivable trait which ties a database [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")
 with a configuration name.

This trait should rarely, if ever, be implemented manually. Instead, it should be derived:

    use rocket_db_pools::{deadpool_redis, Database};
    
    #[derive(Database)]
    #[database("memdb")]
    struct Db(deadpool_redis::Pool);
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().attach(Db::init())
    }

See the [`Database` derive](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/derive.Database.html "derive rocket_db_pools::Database")
 for details.

Required Associated Constants[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#required-associated-consts)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/database.rs.html#47)

#### const [NAME](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#associatedconstant.NAME)
: &'static [str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)

The configuration name for this database.

When `Database` is derived, this takes the value `"name"` in the `#[database("name")]` attribute.

Required Associated Types[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#required-associated-types)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/database.rs.html#41)

#### type [Pool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#associatedtype.Pool)
: [Pool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")

The [`Pool`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")
 type of connections to this database.

When `Database` is derived, this takes the value of the `Inner` type in `struct Db(Inner)`.

Provided Methods[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#provided-methods)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/database.rs.html#68-70)

#### fn [init](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#method.init)
() -> [Initializer](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Initializer.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Initializer")
<Self>

Returns a fairing that initializes the database and its connection pool.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#example)
Example

    use rocket_db_pools::{deadpool_postgres, Database};
    
    #[derive(Database)]
    #[database("pg_db")]
    struct Db(deadpool_postgres::Pool);
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().attach(Db::init())
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/database.rs.html#120-130)

#### fn [fetch](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#method.fetch)
<P: [Phase](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/trait.Phase.html "trait rocket::phase::Phase")
\>(rocket: &[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/rkt/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::rkt::Rocket")
<P>) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<&Self>

Returns a reference to the initialized database in `rocket`. The initializer fairing returned by `init()` must have already executed for `Option` to be `Some`. This is guaranteed to be the case if the fairing is attached and either:

*   Rocket is in the [`Orbit`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/enum.Orbit.html "enum rocket::phase::Orbit")
     phase. That is, the application is running. This is always the case in request guards and liftoff fairings,
*   _or_ Rocket is in the [`Build`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/enum.Build.html "enum rocket::phase::Build")
     or [`Ignite`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/enum.Ignite.html "enum rocket::phase::Ignite")
     phase and the `Initializer` fairing has already been run. This is the case in all fairing callbacks corresponding to fairings attached _after_ the `Initializer` fairing.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#example-1)
Example

Run database migrations in an ignite fairing. It is imperative that the migration fairing be registered _after_ the `init()` fairing.

    use rocket::{Rocket, Build};
    use rocket::fairing::{self, AdHoc};
    
    use rocket_db_pools::{sqlx, Database};
    
    #[derive(Database)]
    #[database("sqlite_db")]
    struct Db(sqlx::SqlitePool);
    
    async fn run_migrations(rocket: Rocket<Build>) -> fairing::Result {
        if let Some(db) = Db::fetch(&rocket) {
            // run migrations using `db`. get the inner type with &db.0.
            Ok(rocket)
        } else {
            Err(rocket)
        }
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
            .attach(Db::init())
            .attach(AdHoc::try_on_ignite("DB Migrations", run_migrations))
    }

Dyn Compatibility[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#dyn-compatibility)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This trait is **not** [dyn compatible](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/reference/items/traits.html#dyn-compatibility)
.

_In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety", so this trait is not object safe._

Implementors[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database#implementors)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

---

# Template in rocket_dyn_templates - Rust

[Template](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_dyn\_templates](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html)

Struct Template Copy item path
==============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#27-30)

    pub struct Template { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

Responder that renders a dynamic template.

`Template` serves as a _proxy_ type for rendering a template and _does not_ contain the rendered template itself. The template is lazily rendered, at response time. To render a template greedily, use [`Template::show()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.show "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::show")
.

See the [crate root](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html "mod rocket_dyn_templates")
 for usage details.

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#implementations)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#42-256)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#impl-Template)

### impl [Template](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Template")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#73-75)

#### pub fn [fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.fairing)
() -> impl [Fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")

Returns a fairing that initializes and maintains templating state.

This fairing, or the one returned by [`Template::custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.custom "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::custom")
, _must_ be attached to any `Rocket` instance that wishes to render templates. Failure to attach this fairing will result in a “Uninitialized template context: missing fairing.” error message when a template is attempted to be rendered.

If you wish to customize the internal templating engines, use [`Template::custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.custom "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::custom")
 instead.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example)
Example

To attach this fairing, simple call `attach` on the application’s `Rocket` instance with `Template::fairing()`:

    extern crate rocket;
    extern crate rocket_dyn_templates;
    
    use rocket_dyn_templates::Template;
    
    fn main() {
        rocket::build()
            // ...
            .attach(Template::fairing())
            // ...
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#104-108)

#### pub fn [custom](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.custom)
<F>(f: F) -> impl [Fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")

where F: [Fn](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.Fn.html "trait core::ops::function::Fn")
(&mut [Engines](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Engines.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Engines")
) + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + 'static,

Returns a fairing that initializes and maintains templating state.

Unlike [`Template::fairing()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.fairing "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::fairing")
, this method allows you to configure templating engines via the function `f`. Note that only the enabled templating engines will be accessible from the `Engines` type.

This method does not allow the function `f` to fail. If `f` is fallible, use [`Template::try_custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.try_custom "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::try_custom")
 instead.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-1)
Example

    extern crate rocket;
    extern crate rocket_dyn_templates;
    
    use rocket_dyn_templates::Template;
    
    fn main() {
        rocket::build()
            // ...
            .attach(Template::custom(|engines| {
                // engines.handlebars.register_helper ...
            }))
            // ...
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#136-140)

#### pub fn [try\_custom](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.try_custom)
<F>(f: F) -> impl [Fairing](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fairing/trait.Fairing.html "trait rocket::fairing::Fairing")

where F: [Fn](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.Fn.html "trait core::ops::function::Fn")
(&mut [Engines](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Engines.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Engines")
) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[()](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.unit.html)
, [Box](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/boxed/struct.Box.html "struct alloc::boxed::Box")
<dyn [Error](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/error/trait.Error.html "trait core::error::Error")
\>> + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + [Sync](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sync.html "trait core::marker::Sync")
 + 'static,

Returns a fairing that initializes and maintains templating state.

This variant of [`Template::custom()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.custom "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::custom")
 allows a fallible `f`. If `f` returns an error during initialization, it will cancel the launch. If `f` returns an error during template reloading (in debug mode), then the newly-reloaded templates are discarded.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-2)
Example

    extern crate rocket;
    extern crate rocket_dyn_templates;
    
    use rocket_dyn_templates::Template;
    
    fn main() {
        rocket::build()
            // ...
            .attach(Template::try_custom(|engines| {
                // engines.handlebars.register_helper ...
                Ok(())
            }))
            // ...
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#174-181)

#### pub fn [render](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.render)
<S, C>(name: S, context: C) -> [Template](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Template")

where S: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[Cow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/enum.Cow.html "enum alloc::borrow::Cow")
<'static, [str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>, C: [Serialize](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/serde_core/ser/trait.Serialize.html "trait serde_core::ser::Serialize")
,

Render the template named `name` with the context `context`. The `context` is typically created using the \[`context!`\] macro, but it can be of any type that implements `Serialize`, such as `HashMap` or a custom `struct`.

To render a template directly into a string, use \[`Metadata::render()`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#examples)
Examples

Using the `context` macro:

    use rocket_dyn_templates::{Template, context};
    
    let template = Template::render("index", context! {
        foo: "Hello, world!",
    });

Using a `HashMap` as the context:

    use std::collections::HashMap;
    use rocket_dyn_templates::Template;
    
    // Create a `context` from a `HashMap`.
    let mut context = HashMap::new();
    context.insert("foo", "Hello, world!");
    
    let template = Template::render("index", context);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#217-228)

#### pub fn [show](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.show)
<S, C>(rocket: &[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/rkt/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::rkt::Rocket")
<[Orbit](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/enum.Orbit.html "enum rocket::phase::Orbit")
\>, name: S, context: C) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<[String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
\>

where S: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[Cow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/enum.Cow.html "enum alloc::borrow::Cow")
<'static, [str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>, C: [Serialize](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/serde_core/ser/trait.Serialize.html "trait serde_core::ser::Serialize")
,

Render the template named `name` with the context `context` into a `String`. This method should **not** be used in any running Rocket application. This method should only be used during testing to validate `Template` responses. For other uses, use [`render()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.render)
 instead.

The `context` can be of any type that implements `Serialize`. This is typically a `HashMap` or a custom `struct`.

Returns `Some` if the template could be rendered. Otherwise, returns `None`. If rendering fails, error output is printed to the console. `None` is also returned if a `Template` fairing has not been attached.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-3)
Example

    use std::collections::HashMap;
    
    use rocket_dyn_templates::Template;
    use rocket::local::blocking::Client;
    
    fn main() {
        let rocket = rocket::build().attach(Template::fairing());
        let client = Client::untracked(rocket).expect("valid rocket");
    
        // Create a `context`. Here, just an empty `HashMap`.
        let mut context = HashMap::new();
        let template = Template::show(client.rocket(), "index", context);
    }

Trait Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#trait-implementations)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#26)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#impl-Debug-for-Template)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.fmt)

(&self, f: &mut [Formatter](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/fmt/struct.Formatter.html "struct core::fmt::Formatter")
<'\_>) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/fmt/type.Result.html "type core::fmt::Result")

Formats the value using the given formatter. [Read more](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/fmt/trait.Debug.html#tymethod.fmt)

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#261-274)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#impl-Responder%3C'r,+'static%3E-for-Template)

### impl<'r> [Responder](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/responder/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::responder::Responder")
<'r, 'static> for [Template](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Template")

Returns a response with the Content-Type derived from the template’s extension and a fixed-size body containing the rendered template. If rendering fails, an `Err` of `Status::InternalServerError` is returned.

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/template.rs.html#262-273)

<'\_>) -> [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/type.Result.html "type rocket::response::Result")
<'static>

 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#impl-Paint-for-T)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-4)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.primary)

#### fn [primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-5)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.fixed)

#### fn [fixed](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fixed)
(&self, color: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Fixed`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-6)
Example

    println!("{}", value.fixed(color));

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.rgb)

#### fn [rgb](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.rgb)
(&self, r: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)
, g: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)
, b: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Rgb`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-7)
Example

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-24)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-44)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-54)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

\>

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())
 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

    println!("{}", value.mask());

#### fn [on\_bright](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.on_bright)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

 set to \[`Quirk :: OnBright`\].

Example

    println!("{}", value.on_bright());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template#example-62)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);
