# Metadata in rocket_dyn_templates - Rust

[Metadata](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_dyn\_templates](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html)

Struct Metadata Copy item path
==============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/metadata.rs.html#42)

    pub struct Metadata<'a>(/* private fields */);

Expand description

Request guard for dynamically querying template metadata.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#usage)
Usage
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `Metadata` type implements Rocket’s [`FromRequest`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/from_request/trait.FromRequest.html "trait rocket::request::from_request::FromRequest")
 trait, so it can be used as a request guard in any request handler.

    use rocket_dyn_templates::{Template, Metadata, context};
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn homepage(metadata: Metadata) -> Template {
        // Conditionally render a template if it's available.
        if metadata.contains_template("some-template") {
            Template::render("some-template", &context)
        } else {
            Template::render("fallback", &context)
        }
    }
    
    fn main() {
        rocket::build()
            .attach(Template::fairing())
            // ...
    }

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#implementations)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/metadata.rs.html#44-126)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#impl-Metadata%3C'_%3E)

### impl [Metadata](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Metadata")
<'\_>

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/metadata.rs.html#62-64)

#### pub fn [contains\_template](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.contains_template)
(&self, name: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if the template with the given `name` is currently loaded. Otherwise, returns `false`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example)
Example

    use rocket_dyn_templates::Metadata;
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn handler(metadata: Metadata) {
        // Returns `true` if the template with name `"name"` was loaded.
        let loaded = metadata.contains_template("name");
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/metadata.rs.html#82-84)

#### pub fn [reloading](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.reloading)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if template reloading is enabled.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-1)
Example

    use rocket_dyn_templates::Metadata;
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn handler(metadata: Metadata) {
        // Returns `true` if template reloading is enabled.
        let reloading = metadata.reloading();
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_dyn_templates/metadata.rs.html#121-125)

#### pub fn [render](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.render)
<S, C>(&self, name: S, context: C) -> [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")
<(ContentType, [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String")
)>

where S: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[Cow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/borrow/enum.Cow.html "enum alloc::borrow::Cow")
<'static, [str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>>, C: [Serialize](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/serde_core/ser/trait.Serialize.html "trait serde_core::ser::Serialize")
,

Directly render the template named `name` with the context `context` into a `String`. Also returns the template’s detected `ContentType`. See [`Template::render()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Template.html#method.render "associated function rocket_dyn_templates::Template::render")
 for more details on rendering.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#examples)
Examples

    use rocket::http::ContentType;
    use rocket_dyn_templates::{Metadata, Template, context};
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn send_email(metadata: Metadata) -> Option<()> {
        let (mime, string) = metadata.render("email", context! {
            field: "Hello, world!"
        })?;
    
        send_email(mime, string).await?;
        Some(())
    }
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn raw_render(metadata: Metadata) -> Option<(ContentType, String)> {
        metadata.render("index", context! { field: "Hello, world!" })
    }
    
    // Prefer the following, however, which is nearly identical but pithier:
    
    #[get("/")]
    fn render() -> Template {
        Template::render("index", context! { field: "Hello, world!" })
    }

### impl<'r> [FromRequest](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/from_request/trait.FromRequest.html "trait rocket::request::from_request::FromRequest")
<'r> for [Metadata](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::Metadata")
<'r>

Retrieves the template metadata. If a template fairing hasn’t been attached, an error is printed and an empty `Err` with status `InternalServerError` (`500`) is returned.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-2)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.primary)

#### fn [primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-3)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Fixed`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-4)
Example

    println!("{}", value.fixed(color));

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

, g: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-6)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-22)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-42)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-52)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

👎Deprecated since 1.0.1: renamed to `resetting()` due to conflicts with `Vec::clear()`. The `clear()` method will be removed in a future release.

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())

#### fn [on\_bright](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.on_bright)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

 set to \[`Quirk :: OnBright`\].

Example

    println!("{}", value.on_bright());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#example-60)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/struct.Metadata#method.new)

#### fn [new](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)
(self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<Self>

where Self: [Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

# Ident in rocket::config - Rust

[Ident](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#)

----------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/index.html)

Struct Ident Copy item path
===========================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/ident.rs.html#78)

    pub struct Ident(/* private fields */);

Expand description

An identifier (or `None`) to send as the `Server` header.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#deserialization)
Deserialization
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

An `Ident` deserializes from any of the following:

*   `string`
    
    The string must be a valid `Ident`. See [`Ident::try_new()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html#method.try_new "associated function rocket::config::Ident::try_new")
     for details.
    
*   `boolean`
    
    The boolean must be `false`. The value will be [`Ident::none()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html#method.none "associated function rocket::config::Ident::none")
    .
    
*   `Option<string>`
    
    If `Some`, this is the same as deserializing from the inner string. If `None`, the value is [`Ident::none()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html#method.none "associated function rocket::config::Ident::none")
    .
    
*   `unit`
    
    Always deserializes as [`Ident::none()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html#method.none "associated function rocket::config::Ident::none")
    .
    

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#examples)
Examples
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

As with all Rocket configuration options, when using the default [`Config::figment()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.figment "associated function rocket::Config::figment")
, `Ident` can be configured via a `Rocket.toml` file. When no value for `ident` is provided, the value defaults to `"Rocket"`. Because a default is provided, configuration only needs to provided to customize or remove the value.

    use rocket::config::{Config, Ident};
    
    // If these are the contents of `Rocket.toml`...
    [default]
    ident = false
    
    // The config parses as follows:
    assert_eq!(config.ident, Ident::none());
    
    // If these are the contents of `Rocket.toml`...
    [default]
    ident = "My Server"
    
    // The config parses as follows:
    assert_eq!(config.ident, Ident::try_new("My Server").unwrap());

The following example illustrates manual configuration:

    use rocket::config::{Config, Ident};
    
    let figment = rocket::Config::figment().merge(("ident", false));
    let config = rocket::Config::from(figment);
    assert_eq!(config.ident, Ident::none());
    
    let figment = rocket::Config::figment().merge(("ident", "Fancy/1.0"));
    let config = rocket::Config::from(figment);
    assert_eq!(config.ident, Ident::try_new("Fancy/1.0").unwrap());

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#implementations)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/ident.rs.html#94-175)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#impl-Ident)

### impl [Ident](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html "struct rocket::config::Ident")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/ident.rs.html#128-136)

#### pub fn [try\_new](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.try_new)
<S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[String](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/string/struct.String.html "struct rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::string::String")
\>>(ident: S) -> [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/result/enum.Result.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::result::Result")
<[Ident](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html "struct rocket::config::Ident")
, [String](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/string/struct.String.html "struct rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::string::String")
\>

Returns a new `Ident` with the string `ident`.

When configured as the [`Config::ident`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.ident "field rocket::Config::ident")
, Rocket will set a `Server` header with the `ident` value on all responses.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#errors)
Errors

The string `ident` must be non-empty and may only contain visible ASCII characters. The first character cannot be whitespace. The only whitespace characters allowed are (space) and `\t` (horizontal tab). The string is returned wrapped in `Err` if it contains any invalid characters.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example)
Example

    use rocket::config::Ident;
    
    let ident = Ident::try_new("Rocket").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(ident.as_str(), Some("Rocket"));
    
    let ident = Ident::try_new("Rocket Run").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(ident.as_str(), Some("Rocket Run"));
    
    let ident = Ident::try_new(" Rocket");
    assert!(ident.is_err());
    
    let ident = Ident::try_new("Rocket\nRun");
    assert!(ident.is_err());
    
    let ident = Ident::try_new("\tShip");
    assert!(ident.is_err());

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/ident.rs.html#152-154)

#### pub const fn [none](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.none)
() -> [Ident](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html "struct rocket::config::Ident")

Returns a new `Ident` which is `None`.

When configured as the [`Config::ident`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#structfield.ident "field rocket::Config::ident")
, Rocket will not set a `Server` header on any response. Any `Server` header emitted by the application will still be written out.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::config::Ident;
    
    let ident = Ident::none();
    assert_eq!(ident.as_str(), None);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/ident.rs.html#172-174)

#### pub fn [as\_str](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.as_str)
(&self) -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<&[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
\>

Returns `self` as an `Option<&str>`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-2)
Example

    use rocket::config::Ident;
    
    let ident = Ident::try_new("Rocket").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(ident.as_str(), Some("Rocket"));
    
    let ident = Ident::try_new("Rocket/1 (Unix)").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(ident.as_str(), Some("Rocket/1 (Unix)"));
    
    let ident = Ident::none();
    assert_eq!(ident.as_str(), None);

 for T

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-3)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-4)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-23)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.on_primary)

#### fn [on\_primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.on_primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`bg()`](self::bg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-24)
Example

    println!("{}", value.on_primary());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-43)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.bold)

(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-44)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-53)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.mask)

#### fn [mask](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.mask)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())
 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-54)
Example

    println!("{}", value.mask());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#example-61)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)

#### fn [with\_subscriber](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_subscriber)
<S>(self, subscriber: S) -> [WithDispatch](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")
<Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#)

where S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Dispatch](https://docs.rs/tracing-core/0.1.36/tracing_core/dispatcher/struct.Dispatch.html "struct tracing_core::dispatcher::Dispatch")
\>,

Attaches the provided [`Subscriber`](https://docs.rs/tracing-core/0.1.36/tracing_core/subscriber/trait.Subscriber.html "trait tracing_core::subscriber::Subscriber")
 to this type, returning a [`WithDispatch`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")
 wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_subscriber)

<Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident#)

Attaches the current [default](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/dispatcher/index.html#setting-the-default-subscriber "mod tracing::dispatcher")
 [`Subscriber`](https://docs.rs/tracing-core/0.1.36/tracing_core/subscriber/trait.Subscriber.html "trait tracing_core::subscriber::Subscriber")
 to this type, returning a [`WithDispatch`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/struct.WithDispatch.html "struct tracing::instrument::WithDispatch")
 wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.WithSubscriber.html#method.with_current_subscriber)

# Catcher in rocket::catcher - Rust

[Catcher](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[catcher](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/index.html)

Struct Catcher Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/catcher/catcher.rs.html#112-124)

    pub struct Catcher {
        pub name: Option<Cow<'static, str>>,
        pub base: Origin<'static>,
        pub code: Option<u16>,
        pub handler: Box<dyn Handler>,
    }

Expand description

An error catching route.

Catchers are routes that run when errors are produced by the application. They consist of a [`Handler`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/trait.Handler.html "trait rocket::catcher::Handler")
 and an optional status code to match against arising errors. Errors arise from the the following sources:

*   A failing guard.
*   A failing responder.
*   A forwarding guard.
*   Routing failure.

Each error or forward is paired with a status code. Guards and responders indicate the status code themselves via their `Err` and `Outcome` return value. A complete routing failure is always a `404`. Rocket invokes the error handler for the catcher with an error’s status code, or in the case of every route resulting in a forward, the last forwarded status code.

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#error-handler-restrictions)
Error Handler Restrictions

Because error handlers are a last resort, they should not fail to produce a response. If an error handler _does_ fail, Rocket invokes its default `500` error catcher. Error handlers cannot forward.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#routing)
Routing
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

If a route fails by returning an error [`Outcome`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/request/type.Outcome.html "type rocket::request::Outcome")
, Rocket routes the erroring request to the highest precedence catcher among all the catchers that match. Precedence is determined by the catcher’s _base_, which is provided as the first argument to [`Rocket::register()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.register "method rocket::Rocket::register")
. Catchers with more non-empty segments have a higher precedence.

Rocket provides [built-in defaults](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#built-in-default)
, but _default_ catchers can also be registered. A _default_ catcher is a catcher with no explicit status code: `None`.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#collisions)
Collisions
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Two catchers are said to _collide_ if there exists an error that matches both catchers. Colliding catchers present a routing ambiguity and are thus disallowed by Rocket. Because catchers can be constructed dynamically, collision checking is done at [`ignite`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Rocket.html#method.ignite "method rocket::Rocket::ignite")
 time, after it becomes statically impossible to register any more catchers on an instance of `Rocket`.

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#built-in-default)
Built-In Default

Rocket’s provides a built-in default catcher that can handle all errors. It produces HTML or JSON, depending on the value of the `Accept` header. As such, catchers only need to be registered if an error needs to be handled in a custom fashion. The built-in default never conflicts with any user-registered catchers.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#code-generation)
Code Generation
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Catchers should rarely be constructed or used directly. Instead, they are typically generated via the [`catch`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.catch.html "attr rocket::catch")
 attribute, as follows:

    #[macro_use] extern crate rocket;
    
    use rocket::Request;
    use rocket::http::Status;
    
    #[catch(500)]
    fn internal_error() -> &'static str {
        "Whoops! Looks like we messed up."
    }
    
    #[catch(404)]
    fn not_found(req: &Request) -> String {
        format!("I couldn't find '{}'. Try something else?", req.uri())
    }
    
    #[catch(default)]
    fn default(status: Status, req: &Request) -> String {
        format!("{} ({})", status, req.uri())
    }
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build().register("/", catchers![internal_error, not_found, default])
    }

A function decorated with `#[catch]` may take zero, one, or two arguments. It’s type signature must be one of the following, where `R:`[`Responder`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/trait.Responder.html "trait rocket::response::Responder")
:

*   `fn() -> R`
*   `fn(`[`&Request`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html "struct rocket::Request")
    `) -> R`
*   `fn(`[`Status`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Status.html "struct rocket::http::Status")
    `,` [`&Request`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html "struct rocket::Request")
    `) -> R`

See the [`catch`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.catch.html "attr rocket::catch")
 documentation for full details.

Fields[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#fields)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#impl-Catcher)

### impl [Catcher](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Catcher.html "struct rocket::Catcher")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/catcher/catcher.rs.html#163-177)

#### pub fn [new](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.new)
<S, H>(code: S, handler: H) -> [Catcher](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Catcher.html "struct rocket::Catcher")

where S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[u16](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u16.html)
\>>, H: [Handler](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/trait.Handler.html "trait rocket::catcher::Handler")
,

Creates a catcher for the given `status`, or a default catcher if `status` is `None`, using the given error handler. This should only be used when routing manually.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#examples)
Examples

    use rocket::request::Request;
    use rocket::catcher::{Catcher, BoxFuture};
    use rocket::response::Responder;
    use rocket::http::Status;
    
    fn handle_404<'r>(status: Status, req: &'r Request<'_>) -> BoxFuture<'r> {
       let res = (status, format!("404: {}", req.uri()));
       Box::pin(async move { res.respond_to(req) })
    }
    
    fn handle_500<'r>(_: Status, req: &'r Request<'_>) -> BoxFuture<'r> {
        Box::pin(async move{ "Whoops, we messed up!".respond_to(req) })
    }
    
    fn handle_default<'r>(status: Status, req: &'r Request<'_>) -> BoxFuture<'r> {
       let res = (status, format!("{}: {}", status, req.uri()));
       Box::pin(async move { res.respond_to(req) })
    }
    
    let not_found_catcher = Catcher::new(404, handle_404);
    let internal_server_error_catcher = Catcher::new(500, handle_500);
    let default_error_catcher = Catcher::new(None, handle_default);

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#panics)
Panics

Panics if `code` is not in the HTTP status code error range `[400, 600)`.\
\
[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/catcher/catcher.rs.html#212-221)\
\
#### pub fn [map\_base](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.map_base)\
<'a, F>(self, mapper: F) -> [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/result/enum.Result.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::result::Result")\
<Self, [Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Error.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Error")\
<'static>>\
\
where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")\
([Origin](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/struct.Origin.html "struct rocket::http::uri::Origin")\
<'a>) -> [String](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/string/struct.String.html "struct rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::string::String")\
,\
\
Maps the `base` of this catcher using `mapper`, returning a new `Catcher` with the returned base.\
\
`mapper` is called with the current base. The returned `String` is used as the new base if it is a valid URI. If the returned base URI contains a query, it is ignored. Returns an error if the base produced by `mapper` is not a valid origin URI.\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example)\
Example\
\
    use rocket::request::Request;\
    use rocket::catcher::{Catcher, BoxFuture};\
    use rocket::response::Responder;\
    use rocket::http::Status;\
    \
    fn handle_404<'r>(status: Status, req: &'r Request<'_>) -> BoxFuture<'r> {\
       let res = (status, format!("404: {}", req.uri()));\
       Box::pin(async move { res.respond_to(req) })\
    }\
    \
    let catcher = Catcher::new(404, handle_404);\
    assert_eq!(catcher.base.path(), "/");\
    \
    let catcher = catcher.map_base(|_| format!("/bar")).unwrap();\
    assert_eq!(catcher.base.path(), "/bar");\
    \
    let catcher = catcher.map_base(|base| format!("/foo{}", base)).unwrap();\
    assert_eq!(catcher.base.path(), "/foo/bar");\
    \
    let catcher = catcher.map_base(|base| format!("/foo ? {}", base));\
    assert!(catcher.is_err());\
\

#### fn [instrument](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.Instrument.html#method.instrument)\
(self, span: [Span](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/span/struct.Span.html "struct tracing::span::Span")\
) -> [Instrumented](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/struct.Instrumented.html "struct tracing::instrument::Instrumented")\
<Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#)\
\
Instruments this type with the provided [`Span`](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/span/struct.Span.html "struct tracing::span::Span")\
, returning an `Instrumented` wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.Instrument.html#method.instrument)\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/src/tracing/instrument.rs.html#128)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.in_current_span)\
\

<Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#)\
\

, returning an `Instrumented` wrapper. [Read more](https://docs.rs/tracing/0.1.44/tracing/instrument/trait.Instrument.html#method.in_current_span)\
\

#### fn [into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)\
(self) -> U\
\
Calls `U::from(self)`.\
\
That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.From.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::From") <T> for U` chooses to do.\
\

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")\
 for T\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.into_either)\
\
#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)\
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)\
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")\
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#)\
\
Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")\
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")\
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")\
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")\
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.into_either_with)\
\
#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)\
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")\
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#)\
\
where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")\
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)\
,\
\
Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")\
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")\
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")\
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")\
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#134)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#impl-Paint-for-T)\
\
### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")\
 for T\
\
where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")\
,\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.fg)\
\
#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)\
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")\
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")\
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.\
\
This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())\
 and [`green()`](self::green())\
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-1)\
Example\
\
Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:\
\
    use yansi::{Paint, Color};\
    \
    painted.fg(Color::White);\
\
Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())\
.\
\
    use yansi::Paint;\
    \
    painted.white();\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.primary)\
\
#### fn [primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.primary)\
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")\
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())\
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-2)\
Example\
\
    println!("{}", value.primary());\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.fixed)\
\
#### fn [fixed](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fixed)\
(&self, color: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")\
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())\
 set to \[`Color :: Fixed`\].\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-3)\
Example\
\
    println!("{}", value.fixed(color));\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.rgb)\
\
#### fn [rgb](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.rgb)\
(&self, r: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\
, g: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\
, b: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\

#### fn [yellow](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.yellow)\

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())\
 set to \[`Color :: Yellow`\].\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-8)\
Example\
\
    println!("{}", value.yellow());\
\

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.bg)\
\
#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)\
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")\
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")\
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.\
\
This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())\
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())\
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-21)\
Example\
\
Set background color to red using `fg()`:\
\
    use yansi::{Paint, Color};\
    \
    painted.bg(Color::Red);\
\
Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())\
.\
\
    use yansi::Paint;\
    \
    painted.on_red();\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.on_primary)\

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.attr)\
\
#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)\
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")\
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")\
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")\
 `value`.\
\
This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())\
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())\
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-41)\
Example\
\
Make text bold using `attr()`:\
\
    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};\
    \
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);\
\
Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())\
.\
\
    use yansi::Paint;\
    \
    painted.bold();\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.bold)\
\

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())\
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-42)\
Example\
\
    println!("{}", value.bold());\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)\

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.quirk)\
\
#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)\
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")\
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")\
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")\
 `value`.\
\
This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())\
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())\
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-51)\
Example\
\
Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:\
\
    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};\
    \
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);\
\
Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())\
.\
\
    use yansi::Paint;\
    \
    painted.wrap();\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.mask)\

\>\
\

 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].\
\

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)\
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")\
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")\
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)\
\>\
\
Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")\
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.\
\
See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")\
 for more details.\
\
##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#example-59)\
Example\
\
Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:\
\
    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};\
    \
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);\
\
[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)\
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/catcher/struct.Catcher#method.new-1)\
\

,\

. [Read more](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)\
\

# rocket_db_pools::diesel - Rust

[Module diesel](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/#)

---------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html)

Module diesel Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/diesel.rs.html#1-149)

Expand description

Re-export of [`diesel`](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/index.html "mod diesel")
 with prelude types overridden with `async` variants from [`diesel_async`](https://docs.rs/diesel-async/0.4.1/diesel_async/index.html "mod diesel_async")
.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/#usage)
Usage
-------------------------------------------------------------------

To use `async` `diesel` support provided here, enable the following dependencies in your `Cargo.toml`:

    [dependencies]
    rocket = "0.5.1"
    diesel = "2"
    
    [dependencies.rocket_db_pools]
    version = "0.2.0"
    features = ["diesel_mysql"]

Then, import `rocket_db_pools::diesel::prelude::*` as well as the appropriate pool type and, optionally, [`QueryResult`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/type.QueryResult.html "type rocket_db_pools::diesel::QueryResult")
. To use macros or `diesel` functions, use `diesel::` directly. That is, _do not_ import `rocket_db_pools::diesel`. Doing so will, by design, cause import errors.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/#example)
Example
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

    use rocket_db_pools::{Database, Connection};
    use rocket_db_pools::diesel::{QueryResult, MysqlPool, prelude::*};
    
    #[derive(Database)]
    #[database("diesel_mysql")]
    struct Db(MysqlPool);
    
    #[derive(Queryable, Insertable)]
    #[diesel(table_name = posts)]
    struct Post {
        id: i64,
        title: String,
        published: bool,
    }
    
    diesel::table! {
        posts (id) {
            id -> BigInt,
            title -> Text,
            published -> Bool,
        }
    }
    
    #[get("/")]
    async fn list(mut db: Connection<Db>) -> QueryResult<String> {
        let post_ids: Vec<i64> = posts::table
            .select(posts::id)
            .load(&mut db)
            .await?;
    
        Ok(format!("{post_ids:?}"))
    }

Modules[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/#modules)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[prelude](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/prelude/index.html "mod rocket_db_pools::diesel::prelude")

The [`diesel`](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/index.html "mod diesel")
 prelude with `sync`\-only traits replaced with their [`diesel_async`](https://docs.rs/diesel-async/0.4.1/diesel_async/index.html "mod diesel_async")
 variants.

Structs[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/#structs)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[AsyncMysqlConnection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/struct.AsyncMysqlConnection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::diesel::AsyncMysqlConnection")

A connection to a MySQL database. Connection URLs should be in the form `mysql://[user[:password]@]host/database_name`

[AsyncPgConnection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/struct.AsyncPgConnection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::diesel::AsyncPgConnection")

A connection to a PostgreSQL database.

Type Aliases[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/#types)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[MysqlPool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/type.MysqlPool.html "type rocket_db_pools::diesel::MysqlPool")

Type alias for an `async` pool of MySQL connections for `async` [diesel](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/index.html "mod diesel")
.

[PgPool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/type.PgPool.html "type rocket_db_pools::diesel::PgPool")

Type alias for an `async` pool of Postgres connections for `async` [diesel](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/index.html "mod diesel")
.

[Pool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/type.Pool.html "type rocket_db_pools::diesel::Pool")

Type alias for using [`deadpool::managed::Pool`](https://docs.rs/deadpool/0.12.3/deadpool/managed/struct.Pool.html "struct deadpool::managed::Pool")
 with \[`diesel-async`\]

[QueryResult](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/type.QueryResult.html "type rocket_db_pools::diesel::QueryResult")

Alias of a `Result` with an error type of [`Debug`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/response/debug/struct.Debug.html "struct rocket::response::debug::Debug")
 for a `diesel::Error`.

# Config in rocket::config - Rust

::[config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/index.html)

Struct Config Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#60-138)

        pub workers: usize,

        pub limits: Limits,
        pub temp_dir: RelativePathBuf,
        pub keep_alive: u32,

Expand description

Rocket server configuration.

See the [module level docs](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/index.html "mod rocket::config")
 as well as the [configuration guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/)
 for further details.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#defaults)
Defaults
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

All configuration values have a default, documented in the [fields](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#fields)
 section below. [`Config::debug_default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.debug_default "associated function rocket::Config::debug_default")
 returns the default values for the debug profile while [`Config::release_default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.release_default "associated function rocket::Config::release_default")
 the default values for the release profile. The [`Config::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default "associated function rocket::Config::default")
 method automatically selects the appropriate of the two based on the selected profile. With the exception of `log_level`, which is `normal` in `debug` and `critical` in `release`, and `secret_key`, which is regenerated from a random value if not set in “debug” mode only, all default values are identical in all profiles.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#provider-details)
Provider Details
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`Config` is a Figment [`Provider`](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/provider/trait.Provider.html "trait figment::provider::Provider")
 with the following characteristics:

*   **Profile**
    
    The profile is set to the value of the `profile` field.
    
*   **Metadata**
    
    This provider is named `Rocket Config`. It does not specify a [`Source`](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/metadata/enum.Source.html "enum figment::metadata::Source")
     and uses default interpolation.
    
*   **Data**
    
    The data emitted by this provider are the keys and values corresponding to the fields and values of the structure. The dictionary is emitted to the “default” meta-profile.
    

Note that these behaviors differ from those of [`Config::figment()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.figment "associated function rocket::Config::figment")
.

Fields[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#fields)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.profile)
`profile: [Profile](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/profile/struct.Profile.html "struct figment::profile::Profile") `

The selected profile. **(default: _debug_ `debug` / _release_ `release`)**

_**Note:** This field is never serialized nor deserialized. When a `Config` is merged into a `Figment` as a `Provider`, this profile is selected on the `Figment`. When a `Config` is extracted, this field is set to the extracting Figment’s selected `Profile`._

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.address)
`address: [IpAddr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/net/ip_addr/enum.IpAddr.html "enum core::net::ip_addr::IpAddr") `

IP address to serve on. **(default: `127.0.0.1`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.port)
`port: [u16](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u16.html) `

Port to serve on. **(default: `8000`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.workers)
`workers: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

Number of threads to use for executing futures. **(default: `num_cores`)**

_**Note:** Rocket only reads this value from sources in the [default provider](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.figment "associated function rocket::Config::figment")
._

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.max_blocking)
`max_blocking: [usize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.usize.html) `

Limit on threads to start for blocking tasks. **(default: `512`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.ident)
`ident: [Ident](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html "struct rocket::config::Ident") `

How, if at all, to identify the server via the `Server` header. **(default: `"Rocket"`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.ip_header)
`ip_header: [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option") <[Uncased](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uncased/struct.Uncased.html "struct rocket::http::uncased::Uncased") <'static>>`

The name of a header, whose value is typically set by an intermediary server or proxy, which contains the real IP address of the connecting client. Used internally and by [`Request::client_ip()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.client_ip "method rocket::Request::client_ip")
 and [`Request::real_ip()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Request.html#method.real_ip "method rocket::Request::real_ip")
.

To disable using any header for this purpose, set this value to `false`. Deserialization semantics are identical to those of [`Ident`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Ident.html "struct rocket::config::Ident")
 except that the value must syntactically be a valid HTTP header name.

**(default: `"X-Real-IP"`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.limits)
`limits: [Limits](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/struct.Limits.html "struct rocket::data::Limits") `

Streaming read size limits. **(default: [`Limits::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/data/struct.Limits.html#method.default "associated function rocket::data::Limits::default")
)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.temp_dir)
`temp_dir: [RelativePathBuf](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/value/magic/struct.RelativePathBuf.html "struct figment::value::magic::RelativePathBuf") `

Directory to store temporary files in. **(default: [`std::env::temp_dir()`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/env/fn.temp_dir.html "fn std::env::temp_dir")
)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.keep_alive)
`keep_alive: [u32](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u32.html) `

Keep-alive timeout in seconds; disabled when `0`. **(default: `5`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.tls)
`tls: [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option") <[TlsConfig](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.TlsConfig.html "struct rocket::config::TlsConfig") >`

Available on **crate feature `tls`** only.

The TLS configuration, if any. **(default: `None`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.secret_key)
`secret_key: [SecretKey](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey.html "struct rocket::config::SecretKey") `

Available on **crate feature `secrets`** only.

The secret key for signing and encrypting. **(default: `0`)**

_**Note:** This field _always_ serializes as a 256-bit array of `0`s to aid in preventing leakage of the secret key._

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.shutdown)
`shutdown: [Shutdown](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html "struct rocket::config::Shutdown") `

Graceful shutdown configuration. **(default: [`Shutdown::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Shutdown.html#method.default "associated function rocket::config::Shutdown::default")
)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.log_level)
`log_level: [LogLevel](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/enum.LogLevel.html "enum rocket::config::LogLevel") `

Max level to log. **(default: _debug_ `normal` / _release_ `critical`)**

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#structfield.cli_colors)
`cli_colors: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html) `

Whether to use colors and emoji when logging. **(default: `true`)**
