### impl [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#183-204)

#### pub fn [debug\_default](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.debug_default)
() -> [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Returns the default configuration for the `debug` profile, _irrespective of the Rust compilation profile_ and `ROCKET_PROFILE`.

This may differ from the configuration used by default, [`Config::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default "associated function rocket::Config::default")
, which is selected based on the Rust compilation profile. See [defaults](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#defaults)
 and [provider details](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#provider-details)
 for specifics.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    
    let config = Config::debug_default();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#221-227)

#### pub fn [release\_default](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.release_default)
() -> [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Returns the default configuration for the `release` profile, _irrespective of the Rust compilation profile_ and `ROCKET_PROFILE`.

This may differ from the configuration used by default, [`Config::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default "associated function rocket::Config::default")
, which is selected based on the Rust compilation profile. See [defaults](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#defaults)
 and [provider details](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#provider-details)
 for specifics.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    
    let config = Config::release_default();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#257-262)

#### pub fn [figment](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.figment)
() -> [Figment](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/figment/struct.Figment.html "struct figment::figment::Figment")

Returns the default provider figment used by [`rocket::build()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/fn.build.html "fn rocket::build")
.

The default figment reads from the following sources, in ascending priority order:

1.  [`Config::default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.default "associated function rocket::Config::default")
     (see [defaults](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#defaults)
    )
2.  `Rocket.toml` _or_ filename in `ROCKET_CONFIG` environment variable
3.  `ROCKET_` prefixed environment variables

The profile selected is the value set in the `ROCKET_PROFILE` environment variable. If it is not set, it defaults to `debug` when compiled in debug mode and `release` when compiled in release mode.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-2)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    use serde::Deserialize;
    
    #[derive(Deserialize)]
    struct MyConfig {
        app_key: String,
    }
    
    let my_config = Config::figment().extract::<MyConfig>();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#280-285)

#### pub fn [try\_from](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.try_from)
<T: [Provider](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/provider/trait.Provider.html "trait figment::provider::Provider")
\>(provider: T) -> [Result](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/error/type.Result.html "type figment::error::Result")
<Self>

Attempts to extract a `Config` from `provider`, returning the result.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-3)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    use rocket::figment::providers::{Toml, Format, Env};
    
    // Use Rocket's default `Figment`, but allow values from `MyApp.toml`
    // and `MY_APP_` prefixed environment variables to supersede its values.
    let figment = Config::figment()
        .merge(("some-thing", 123))
        .merge(Env::prefixed("CONFIG_"));
    
    let config = Config::try_from(figment);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#308-310)

#### pub fn [from](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.from)
<T: [Provider](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/provider/trait.Provider.html "trait figment::provider::Provider")
\>(provider: T) -> Self

Extract a `Config` from `provider`, panicking if extraction fails.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#panics)
Panics

If extraction fails, prints an error message indicating the error and panics. For a version that doesn’t panic, use [`Config::try_from()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.try_from "associated function rocket::Config::try_from")
.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-4)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    use rocket::figment::providers::{Toml, Format, Env};
    
    // Use Rocket's default `Figment`, but allow values from `MyApp.toml`
    // and `MY_APP_` prefixed environment variables to supersede its values.
    let figment = Config::figment()
        .merge(Toml::file("MyApp.toml").nested())
        .merge(Env::prefixed("MY_APP_"));
    
    let config = Config::from(figment);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#329-335)

#### pub fn [tls\_enabled](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.tls_enabled)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if TLS is enabled.

TLS is enabled when the `tls` feature is enabled and TLS has been configured with at least one ciphersuite. Note that without changing defaults, all supported ciphersuites are enabled in the recommended configuration.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-5)
Example

    let config = rocket::Config::default();
    if config.tls_enabled() {
        println!("TLS is enabled!");
    } else {
        println!("TLS is disabled.");
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#353-363)

#### pub fn [mtls\_enabled](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.mtls_enabled)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if mTLS is enabled.

mTLS is enabled when TLS is enabled ([`Config::tls_enabled()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.tls_enabled "method rocket::Config::tls_enabled")
) _and_ the `mtls` feature is enabled _and_ mTLS has been configured with a CA certificate chain.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-6)
Example

    let config = rocket::Config::default();
    if config.mtls_enabled() {
        println!("mTLS is enabled!");
    } else {
        println!("mTLS is disabled.");
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#472-486)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#impl-Config-1)

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/config.rs.html#152-155)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.default)

#### fn [default](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/default/trait.Default.html#tymethod.default)
() -> [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html "struct rocket::Config")

Returns the default configuration based on the Rust compilation profile. This is [`Config::debug_default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.debug_default "associated function rocket::Config::debug_default")
 in `debug` and [`Config::release_default()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/struct.Config.html#method.release_default "associated function rocket::Config::release_default")
 in `release`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-7)
Example

    use rocket::Config;
    
    let config = Config::default();

#### fn [into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-8)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.primary)

#### fn [primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-9)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

#### fn [white](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.white)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: White`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-19)
Example

    println!("{}", value.white());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-28)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-48)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-58)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

\>

 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-59)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.Config#example-66)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)

 with a default [`Style`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")

<&Self>

where S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Style](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")

# Deserialize in rocket::serde - Rust

[Deserialize](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[serde](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/index.html)

Trait Deserialize Copy item path
================================

[Source](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/src/serde_core/de/mod.rs.html#554)

    pub trait Deserialize<'de>: Sized {
        // Required method
        fn deserialize<D>(
            deserializer: D,
        ) -> Result<Self, <D as Deserializer<'de>>::Error>
           where D: Deserializer<'de>;
    }

Expand description

A **data structure** that can be deserialized from any data format supported by Serde.

Serde provides `Deserialize` implementations for many Rust primitive and standard library types. The complete list is [here](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/serde_core/de/index.html "mod serde_core::de")
. All of these can be deserialized using Serde out of the box.

Additionally, Serde provides a procedural macro called `serde_derive` to automatically generate `Deserialize` implementations for structs and enums in your program. See the [derive section of the manual](https://serde.rs/derive.html)
 for how to use this.

In rare cases it may be necessary to implement `Deserialize` manually for some type in your program. See the [Implementing `Deserialize`](https://serde.rs/impl-deserialize.html)
 section of the manual for more about this.

Third-party crates may provide `Deserialize` implementations for types that they expose. For example the `linked-hash-map` crate provides a `LinkedHashMap<K, V>` type that is deserializable by Serde because the crate provides an implementation of `Deserialize` for it.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize#lifetime)
Lifetime
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `'de` lifetime of this trait is the lifetime of data that may be borrowed by `Self` when deserialized. See the page [Understanding deserializer lifetimes](https://serde.rs/lifetimes.html)
 for a more detailed explanation of these lifetimes.

Required Methods[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize#required-methods)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/src/serde_core/de/mod.rs.html#561-563)

#### fn [deserialize](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize#tymethod.deserialize)
<D>( deserializer: D, ) -> [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/result/enum.Result.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::result::Result")
<Self, <D as [Deserializer](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserializer.html "trait rocket::serde::Deserializer")
<'de>>::[Error](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserializer.html#associatedtype.Error "type rocket::serde::Deserializer::Error")
\>

where D: [Deserializer](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserializer.html "trait rocket::serde::Deserializer")
<'de>,

Deserialize this value from the given Serde deserializer.

See the [Implementing `Deserialize`](https://serde.rs/impl-deserialize.html)
 section of the manual for more information about how to implement this method.

Dyn Compatibility[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize#dyn-compatibility)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This trait is **not** [dyn compatible](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/reference/items/traits.html#dyn-compatibility)
.

_In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety", so this trait is not object safe._

### impl<'de, T> [Deserialize](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize.html "trait rocket::serde::Deserialize")
<'de> for [Rc](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/rc/struct.Rc.html "struct alloc::rc::Rc")
<T>

where [Box](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/boxed/struct.Box.html "struct rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::boxed::Box")
<T>: [Deserialize](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize.html "trait rocket::serde::Deserialize")
<'de>, T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

Available on **crate feature `rc` and (crate features `std` or `alloc`)** only.

This impl requires the [`"rc"`](https://serde.rs/feature-flags.html#-features-rc)
 Cargo feature of Serde.

Deserializing a data structure containing `Rc` will not attempt to deduplicate `Rc` references to the same data. Every deserialized `Rc` will end up with a strong count of 1.

[Source](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/src/serde_core/de/impls.rs.html#2063-2074)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize#method.deserialize-133)

<[Rc](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/rc/struct.Rc.html "struct alloc::rc::Rc")

Available on **crate feature `rc` and (crate features `std` or `alloc`)** only.

This impl requires the [`"rc"`](https://serde.rs/feature-flags.html#-features-rc)
 Cargo feature of Serde. The resulting `Weak<T>` has a reference count of 0 and cannot be upgraded.

# rocket::http - Rust

[Module http](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/#)

--------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Module http Copy item path
==========================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/lib.rs.html#141)

Expand description

Types that map to concepts in HTTP.

This module exports types that map to HTTP concepts or to the underlying HTTP library when needed.

Modules[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/#modules)

------------------------------------------------------------

[ext](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/ext/index.html "mod rocket::http::ext")

Extension traits implemented by several HTTP types.

[hyper](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/hyper/index.html "mod rocket::http::hyper")

Re-exported hyper HTTP library types.

[uncased](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uncased/index.html "mod rocket::http::uncased")

Case-preserving, ASCII case-insensitive string types.

[uri](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/index.html "mod rocket::http::uri")

Types for URIs and traits for rendering URI components.

Macros[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/#macros)

----------------------------------------------------------

[impl\_from\_uri\_param\_identity](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/macro.impl_from_uri_param_identity.html "macro rocket::http::impl_from_uri_param_identity")

Macro to automatically generate _identity_ [`FromUriParam`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/uri/fmt/trait.FromUriParam.html "trait rocket::http::uri::fmt::FromUriParam")
 trait implementations.

Structs[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/#structs)

------------------------------------------------------------

[Accept](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Accept.html "struct rocket::http::Accept")

The HTTP Accept header.

[ContentType](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.ContentType.html "struct rocket::http::ContentType")

Representation of HTTP Content-Types.

[Cookie](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Cookie.html "struct rocket::http::Cookie")

Representation of an HTTP cookie.

[CookieJar](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.CookieJar.html "struct rocket::http::CookieJar")

Collection of one or more HTTP cookies.

[Header](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Header.html "struct rocket::http::Header")

Simple representation of an HTTP header.

[HeaderMap](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.HeaderMap.html "struct rocket::http::HeaderMap")

A collection of headers, mapping a header name to its many ordered values.

[Iter](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Iter.html "struct rocket::http::Iter")

Iterator over all of the cookies in a jar.

[MediaType](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.MediaType.html "struct rocket::http::MediaType")

An HTTP media type.

[QMediaType](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.QMediaType.html "struct rocket::http::QMediaType")

A `MediaType` with an associated quality value.

[RawStr](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.RawStr.html "struct rocket::http::RawStr")

A reference to a string inside of a raw HTTP message.

[RawStrBuf](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.RawStrBuf.html "struct rocket::http::RawStrBuf")

An owned version of [`RawStr`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.RawStr.html "struct rocket::http::RawStr")
.

[Status](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/struct.Status.html "struct rocket::http::Status")

Structure representing an HTTP status: an integer code.

Enums[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/#enums)

--------------------------------------------------------

[Method](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/http/enum.Method.html "enum rocket::http::Method")

Representation of HTTP methods.

# rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja - Rust

[Crate minijinja](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_dyn\_templates](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/index.html)

Crate minijinja Copy item path
==============================

[Source](https://docs.rs/minijinja/2.14.0/src/minijinja/lib.rs.html#1-285)

Expand description

The minijinja templating engine library, reexported.

![](https://github.com/mitsuhiko/minijinja/raw/main/artwork/logo.png)

**MiniJinja: a powerful template engine for Rust with minimal dependencies**

MiniJinja is a powerful but minimal dependency template engine for Rust which is based on the syntax and behavior of the [Jinja2](https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/)
 template engine for Python. It’s implemented on top of [`serde`](https://docs.rs/serde/1.0.228/serde/index.html "mod serde")
. The goal is to be able to render a large chunk of the Jinja2 template ecosystem from Rust with a minimal engine and to leverage an already existing ecosystem of editor integrations.

    {% for user in users %}
      <li>{{ user.name }}</li>
    {% endfor %}

You can play with MiniJinja online [in the browser playground](https://mitsuhiko.github.io/minijinja-playground/)
 powered by a WASM build of MiniJinja.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#why-minijinja)
Why MiniJinja
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MiniJinja by its name wants to be a good default choice if you need a little bit of templating with minimal dependencies. It has the following goals:

*   Well documented, compact API
*   Minimal dependencies, reasonable compile times and decent runtime performance
*   Stay close as possible to Jinja2
*   Support for expression evaluation
*   Support for all `serde` compatible types
*   Excellent test coverage
*   Support for dynamic runtime objects with methods and dynamic attributes

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#template-usage)
Template Usage
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To use MiniJinja one needs to create an [`Environment`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/struct.Environment.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::Environment")
 and populate it with templates. Afterwards templates can be loaded and rendered. To pass data one can pass any serde serializable value. The [`context!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/macro.context.html "macro rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::context")
 macro can be used to quickly construct a template context:

    use minijinja::{Environment, context};
    
    let mut env = Environment::new();
    env.add_template("hello", "Hello {{ name }}!").unwrap();
    let tmpl = env.get_template("hello").unwrap();
    println!("{}", tmpl.render(context!(name => "John")).unwrap());

    Hello John!

For super trivial cases where you need to render a string once, you can also use the [`render!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/macro.render.html "macro rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::render")
 macro which acts a bit like a replacement for the [`format!`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/macro.format.html "macro alloc::format")
 macro.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#expression-usage)
Expression Usage
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MiniJinja — like Jinja2 — allows to be used as expression language. This can be useful to express logic in configuration files or similar things. For this purpose the [`Environment::compile_expression`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/struct.Environment.html#method.compile_expression "method rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::Environment::compile_expression")
 method can be used. It returns an expression object that can then be evaluated, returning the result:

    use minijinja::{Environment, context};
    
    let env = Environment::new();
    let expr = env.compile_expression("number < 42").unwrap();
    let result = expr.eval(context!(number => 23)).unwrap();
    assert_eq!(result.is_true(), true);

This becomes particularly powerful when [dynamic objects](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/value/trait.Object.html "trait rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::value::Object")
 are exposed to templates.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#custom-filters)
Custom Filters
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MiniJinja lets you register functions as filter functions (see [`Function`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/filters/trait.Filter.html "trait rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::filters::Filter")
) with the engine. These can then be invoked directly from the template:

    use minijinja::{Environment, context};
    
    let mut env = Environment::new();
    env.add_filter("repeat", str::repeat);
    env.add_template("hello", "{{ 'Na '|repeat(3) }} {{ name }}!").unwrap();
    let tmpl = env.get_template("hello").unwrap();
    println!("{}", tmpl.render(context!(name => "Batman")).unwrap());

    Na Na Na Batman!

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#learn-more)
Learn more
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`Environment`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/struct.Environment.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::Environment")
    : the main API entry point. Teaches you how to configure the environment.
*   [`Template`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/struct.Template.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::Template")
    : the template object API. Shows you how templates can be rendered.
*   [`syntax`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/syntax/index.html "mod rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::syntax")
    : provides documentation of the template engine syntax.
*   [`filters`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/filters/index.html "mod rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::filters")
    : teaches you how to write custom filters and to see the list of built-in filters.
*   [`tests`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/tests/index.html "mod rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::tests")
    : teaches you how to write custom test functions and to see the list of built-in tests.
*   [`functions`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/functions/index.html "mod rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::functions")
    : teaches how to write custom functions and to see the list of built-in functions.
*   For auto reloading use the [`minijinja-autoreload`](https://docs.rs/minijinja-autoreload)
     crate.
*   For simpler embedding of templates use the [`minijinja-embed`](https://docs.rs/minijinja-embed)
     crate.

Additionally there is an [list of examples](https://github.com/mitsuhiko/minijinja/tree/main/examples)
 with many different small example programs on GitHub to explore.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#error-handling)
Error Handling
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MiniJinja tries to give you good errors out of the box. However if you use includes or template inheritance your experience will improve greatly if you ensure to render chained errors. For more information see [`Error`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/struct.Error.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::Error")
 with an example.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#size-and-compile-times)
Size and Compile Times
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MiniJinja attempts to compile fast so it can be used as a sensible template engine choice when compile times matter. Because of this it’s internally modular so unnecessary bits and pieces can be removed. The default set of features tries to strike a balance but in situations where only a subset is needed (eg: `build.rs`) all default features can be be disabled.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/#optional-features)
Optional Features
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MiniJinja comes with a lot of optional features, some of which are turned on by default. If you plan on using MiniJinja in a library, please consider turning off all default features and to opt-in explicitly into the ones you actually need.

**Configurable Features**

To cut down on size of the engine some default functionality can be removed:

*   **Engine Features:**
    
    *   `builtins`: if this feature is removed the default filters, tests and functions are not implemented.
    *   `macros`: when removed the `{% macro %}` tag is not included.
    *   `multi_template`: when removed the templates related to imports and extends are removed (`{% from %}`, `{% import %}`, `{% include %}`, and `{% extends %}` as well as `{% block %}`).
    *   `adjacent_loop_items`: when removed the `previtem` and `nextitem` attributes of the `loop` object no longer exist. Removing this feature can provide faster template execution when a lot of loops are involved.
    *   `unicode`: when added unicode identifiers are supported and the `sort` filter’s case insensitive comparison changes to using unicode and not ASCII rules. Without this features only ASCII identifiers can be used for variable names and attributes.
    *   `serde`: enables or disables serde support. In current versions of MiniJinja it’s not possible to disable serde but it will become possible. To prevent breakage, MiniJinja warns if this feature is disabled.
*   **Rust Functionality:**
    
    *   `debug`: if this feature is removed some debug functionality of the engine is removed as well. This mainly affects the quality of error reporting.
    *   `deserialization`: when removed this disables deserialization support for the [`Value`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/struct.Value.html "struct rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::Value")
         type, removes the `ViaDeserialize` type and the error type no longer implements `serde::de::Error`.
    *   `std_collections`: if this feature is removed some [`Object`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_dyn_templates/minijinja/value/trait.Object.html "trait rocket_dyn_templates::minijinja::value::Object")
         implementations for standard library collections are removed. Only the ones needed for the engine to function itself are retained.

There are some additional features that provide extra functionality:

*   `fuel`: enables the `fuel` feature which makes the engine track fuel consumption which can be used to better protect against expensive templates.
*   `loader`: enables owned and dynamic template loading of templates.
*   `custom_syntax`: when this feature is enabled, custom delimiters are supported by the parser.
*   `preserve_order`: When enable the internal value implementation uses an indexmap which preserves the original order of maps and structs.
*   `json`: When enabled the `tojson` filter is added as builtin filter as well as the ability to auto escape via `AutoEscape::Json`.
*   `urlencode`: When enabled the `urlencode` filter is added as builtin filter.
*   `loop_controls`: enables the `{% break %}` and `{% continue %}` loop control flow tags.

Performance and memory related features:

*   `stacker`: enables automatic stack growth which permits much larger levels of recursion at runtime. This does not affect the maximum recursion at parsing time which is always limited.
*   `speedups`: enables all speedups, in particular it turns on the `v_htmlescape` dependency for faster HTML escaping.

Internals:

*   `unstable_machinery`: exposes an unstable internal API (no semver guarantees) to parse templates and interact with the engine internals. If you need this functionality, please leave some feedback on GitHub.

# DeserializeOwned in rocket::serde - Rust

[DeserializeOwned](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.DeserializeOwned#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[serde](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/index.html)

Trait DeserializeOwned Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/src/serde_core/de/mod.rs.html#632)

    pub trait DeserializeOwned: for<'de> Deserialize<'de> { }

Expand description

A data structure that can be deserialized without borrowing any data from the deserializer.

This is primarily useful for trait bounds on functions. For example a `from_str` function may be able to deserialize a data structure that borrows from the input string, but a `from_reader` function may only deserialize owned data.

[ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.DeserializeOwned# "This example runs with edition 2021")

    fn from_str<'a, T>(s: &'a str) -> Result<T>
    where
        T: Deserialize<'a>;
    
    fn from_reader<R, T>(rdr: R) -> Result<T>
    where
        R: Read,
        T: DeserializeOwned;

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.DeserializeOwned#lifetime)
Lifetime
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The relationship between `Deserialize` and `DeserializeOwned` in trait bounds is explained in more detail on the page [Understanding deserializer lifetimes](https://serde.rs/lifetimes.html)
.

Dyn Compatibility[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.DeserializeOwned#dyn-compatibility)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This trait is **not** [dyn compatible](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/reference/items/traits.html#dyn-compatibility)
.

_In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety", so this trait is not object safe._

Implementors[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.DeserializeOwned#implementors)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://docs.rs/serde_core/1.0.228/src/serde_core/de/mod.rs.html#633)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.DeserializeOwned#impl-DeserializeOwned-for-T)

### impl<T> [DeserializeOwned](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.DeserializeOwned.html "trait rocket::serde::DeserializeOwned")
 for T

where T: for<'de> [Deserialize](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/serde/trait.Deserialize.html "trait rocket::serde::Deserialize")
<'de>,

# MutualTls in rocket::config - Rust

[MutualTls](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/index.html)

Struct MutualTls Copy item path
===============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/tls.rs.html#163-178)

    pub struct MutualTls {
        pub mandatory: bool,
        /* private fields */
    }

Available on **crate feature `mtls`** only.

Expand description

Mutual TLS configuration.

Configuration works in concert with the [`mtls`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/index.html "mod rocket::mtls")
 module, which provides a request guard to validate, verify, and retrieve client certificates in routes.

By default, mutual TLS is disabled and client certificates are not required, validated or verified. To enable mutual TLS, the `mtls` feature must be enabled and support configured via two `tls.mutual` parameters:

*   `ca_certs`
    
    A required path to a PEM file or raw bytes to a DER-encoded X.509 TLS certificate chain for the certificate authority to verify client certificates against. When a path is configured in a file, such as `Rocket.toml`, relative paths are interpreted as relative to the source file’s directory.
    
*   `mandatory`
    
    An optional boolean that control whether client authentication is required.
    
    When `true`, client authentication is required. TLS connections where the client does not present a certificate are immediately terminated. When `false`, the client is not required to present a certificate. In either case, if a certificate _is_ presented, it must be valid or the connection is terminated.
    

In a `Rocket.toml`, configuration might look like:

    [default.tls.mutual]
    ca_certs = "/ssl/ca_cert.pem"
    mandatory = true                # when absent, defaults to false

Programmatically, configuration might look like:

    use rocket::config::{Config, TlsConfig, MutualTls};
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        let tls_config = TlsConfig::from_paths("/ssl/certs.pem", "/ssl/key.pem")
            .with_mutual(MutualTls::from_path("/ssl/ca_cert.pem"));
    
        let config = Config {
            tls: Some(tls_config),
            ..Default::default()
        };
    
        rocket::custom(config)
    }

Once mTLS is configured, the [`mtls::Certificate`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/struct.Certificate.html "struct rocket::mtls::Certificate")
 request guard can be used to retrieve client certificates in routes.

Fields[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#fields)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#structfield.mandatory)
`mandatory: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html) `

Whether the client is required to present a certificate.

When `true`, the client is required to present a valid certificate to proceed with TLS. When `false`, the client is not required to present a certificate. In either case, if a certificate _is_ presented, it must be valid or the connection is terminated.

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#implementations)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/tls.rs.html#542-625)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#impl-MutualTls)

### impl [MutualTls](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls.html "struct rocket::config::MutualTls")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/tls.rs.html#558-563)

#### pub fn [from\_path](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.from_path)
<C: [AsRef](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.AsRef.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::AsRef")
<[Path](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/path/struct.Path.html "struct std::path::Path")
\>>(ca\_certs: C) -> Self

Available on **crate feature `tls`** only.

Constructs a `MutualTls` from a path to a PEM file with a certificate authority `ca_certs` DER-encoded X.509 TLS certificate chain. This method does no validation; it simply creates a structure suitable for passing into a [`TlsConfig`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.TlsConfig.html "struct rocket::config::TlsConfig")
.

These certificates will be used to verify client-presented certificates in TLS connections.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example)
Example

    use rocket::config::MutualTls;
    
    let tls_config = MutualTls::from_path("/ssl/ca_certs.pem");

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/tls.rs.html#581-586)

#### pub fn [from\_bytes](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.from_bytes)
(ca\_certs: &\[[u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\
\]) -> Self

Available on **crate feature `tls`** only.

Constructs a `MutualTls` from a byte buffer to a certificate authority `ca_certs` DER-encoded X.509 TLS certificate chain. This method does no validation; it simply creates a structure suitable for passing into a [`TlsConfig`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.TlsConfig.html "struct rocket::config::TlsConfig")
.

These certificates will be used to verify client-presented certificates in TLS connections.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::config::MutualTls;
    
    let mtls_config = MutualTls::from_bytes(ca_certs_buf);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/tls.rs.html#604-607)

#### pub fn [mandatory](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.mandatory)
(self, mandatory: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> Self

Available on **crate feature `tls`** only.

Sets whether client authentication is required. Disabled by default.

When `true`, client authentication will be required. TLS connections where the client does not present a certificate will be immediately terminated. When `false`, the client is not required to present a certificate. In either case, if a certificate _is_ presented, it must be valid or the connection is terminated.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-2)
Example

    use rocket::config::MutualTls;
    
    let mtls_config = MutualTls::from_bytes(ca_certs_buf).mandatory(true);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/tls.rs.html#619-624)

#### pub fn [ca\_certs](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.ca_certs)
(&self) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<[PathBuf](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/path/struct.PathBuf.html "struct std::path::PathBuf")
, &\[[u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\
\]> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#)

Available on **crate feature `tls`** only.

Returns the value of the `ca_certs` parameter.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-3)
Example

    use rocket::config::MutualTls;
    
    let mtls_config = MutualTls::from_bytes(ca_certs_buf).mandatory(true);
    assert_eq!(mtls_config.ca_certs().unwrap_right(), ca_certs_buf);

#### fn [into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#134)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#impl-Paint-for-T)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-4)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.primary)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-5)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

#### fn [bright\_cyan](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bright_cyan)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: BrightCyan`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-22)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bright_cyan());

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-24)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-44)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-45)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-54)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())
 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-55)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.MutualTls#example-62)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)

Type Alias PgPool Copy item path
================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/diesel.rs.html#149)

    pub type PgPool = Pool<AsyncPgConnection>;

Expand description

Type alias for an `async` pool of Postgres connections for `async` [diesel](https://docs.rs/diesel/2.1.6/diesel/index.html "mod diesel")
.

    use rocket_db_pools::{Database, Connection};
    use rocket_db_pools::diesel::{PgPool, prelude::*};
    
    #[derive(Database)]
    #[database("my_pg_db_name")]
    struct Db(PgPool);
    
    #[get("/")]
    async fn use_db(mut db: Connection<Db>) {
        /* .. */
    }

# Connection in rocket_db_pools - Rust

[Connection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html)

Struct Connection Copy item path
================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/database.rs.html#197)

    pub struct Connection<D: Database>(/* private fields */);

Expand description

A request guard which retrieves a single connection to a [`Database`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Database")
.

For a database type of `Db`, a request guard of `Connection<Db>` retrieves a single connection to `Db`.

The request guard succeeds if the database was initialized by the [`Initializer`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Initializer.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Initializer")
 fairing and a connection is available within [`connect_timeout`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Config.html#structfield.connect_timeout "field rocket_db_pools::Config::connect_timeout")
 seconds.

*   If the `Initializer` fairing was _not_ attached, the guard _fails_ with status `InternalServerError`. A [`Sentinel`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/sentinel/trait.Sentinel.html "trait rocket::sentinel::Sentinel")
     guards this condition, and so this type of error is unlikely to occur. A `None` error is returned.
*   If a connection is not available within `connect_timeout` seconds or another error occurs, the guard _fails_ with status `ServiceUnavailable` and the error is returned in `Some`.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#deref)
Deref

A type of `Connection<Db>` dereferences, mutably and immutably, to the native database connection type. The [driver table](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#supported-drivers "mod rocket_db_pools")
 lists the concrete native `Deref` types.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#example)
Example
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    use rocket_db_pools::{Database, Connection};
    
    #[derive(Database)]
    #[database("db")]
    struct Db(Pool);
    
    #[get("/")]
    async fn db_op(db: Connection<Db>) {
        // use `&*db` to get an immutable borrow to the native connection type
        // use `&mut *db` to get a mutable borrow to the native connection type
    }

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#implementations)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/database.rs.html#217-246)

\> [Connection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection.html "struct rocket_db_pools::Connection")
<D>

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_db_pools/database.rs.html#243-245)

#### pub fn [into\_inner](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.into_inner)
(self) -> <D::[Pool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Database.html#associatedtype.Pool "type rocket_db_pools::Database::Pool")
 as [Pool](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html "trait rocket_db_pools::Pool")
\>::[Connection](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/trait.Pool.html#associatedtype.Connection "type rocket_db_pools::Pool::Connection")

Returns the internal connection value. See the [`Connection` Deref column](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/index.html#supported-drivers "mod rocket_db_pools")
 for the expected type of this value.

Note that `Connection<D>` derefs to the internal connection type, so using this method is likely unnecessary. See [deref](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection.html#deref "struct rocket_db_pools::Connection")
 for examples.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#example-1)
Example

    use rocket_db_pools::{Database, Connection};
    
    #[derive(Database)]
    #[database("db")]
    struct Db(Pool);
    
    #[get("/")]
    async fn db_op(db: Connection<Db>) {
        let inner = db.into_inner();
    }

 + 'async\_trait>>

where 'life0: 'async\_trait, C: 'async\_trait,

Creates a transaction that will never be committed. This is useful for tests. Panics if called while inside of a transaction or if called with a connection containing a broken transaction

[Source](https://docs.rs/diesel-async/0.4.1/src/diesel_async/lib.rs.html#127)

Executes the given function inside a transaction, but does not commit it. Panics if the given function returns an error. [Read more](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/diesel/prelude/trait.AsyncConnection.html#method.test_transaction)

[Source](https://docs.rs/redis/0.25.4/src/redis/commands/mod.rs.html#131-1968)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.zincr)

<RV, [RedisError](https://docs.rs/redis/0.25.4/redis/types/struct.RedisError.html "struct redis::types::RedisError")

where K: [ToRedisArgs](https://docs.rs/redis/0.25.4/redis/types/trait.ToRedisArgs.html "trait redis::types::ToRedisArgs")

, RV: [FromRedisValue](https://docs.rs/redis/0.25.4/redis/types/trait.FromRedisValue.html "trait redis::types::FromRedisValue")
,

Increments the member in a sorted set at key by delta. If the member does not exist, it is added with delta as its score.

[Source](https://docs.rs/redis/0.25.4/src/redis/commands/mod.rs.html#131-1968)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#example-2)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.primary)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#example-3)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#example-22)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#example-42)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

\>

 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#example-52)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)

👎Deprecated since 1.0.1: renamed to `resetting()` due to conflicts with `Vec::clear()`. The `clear()` method will be removed in a future release.

Returns `self` with the [`quirk()`](self::quirk())

#### fn [on\_bright](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.on_bright)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

 set to \[`Quirk :: OnBright`\].

Example

    println!("{}", value.on_bright());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#example-60)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_db_pools/struct.Connection#method.new)

#### fn [new](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)
(self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<Self>

where Self: [Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

Create a new [`Painted`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")

, T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

Calls `.tap_deref_mut()` only in debug builds, and is erased in release builds.
