# WebSocket in rocket_ws - Rust

[WebSocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_ws](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/index.html)

Struct WebSocket Copy item path
===============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_ws/websocket.rs.html#34-37)

    pub struct WebSocket { /* private fields */ }

Expand description

A request guard identifying WebSocket requests. Converts into a [`Channel`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.Channel.html "struct rocket_ws::Channel")
 or [`MessageStream`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/stream/struct.MessageStream.html "struct rocket_ws::stream::MessageStream")
.

For example usage, see the [crate docs](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/index.html#usage "mod rocket_ws")
.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#details)
Details

This is the entrypoint to the library. Every WebSocket response _must_ initiate via the `WebSocket` request guard. The guard identifies valid WebSocket connection requests and, if the request is valid, succeeds to be converted into a streaming WebSocket response via [`Stream!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/macro.Stream.html "macro rocket_ws::Stream")
, [`WebSocket::channel()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html#method.channel "method rocket_ws::WebSocket::channel")
, or [`WebSocket::stream()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html#method.stream "method rocket_ws::WebSocket::stream")
. The connection can be configured via [`WebSocket::config()`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html#method.config "method rocket_ws::WebSocket::config")
; see [`Config`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_ws::Config")
 for details on configuring a connection.

#### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#forwarding)
Forwarding

If the incoming request is not a valid WebSocket request, the guard forwards with a status of `BadRequest`. The guard never fails.

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#implementations)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_ws/websocket.rs.html#39-195)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#impl-WebSocket)

### impl [WebSocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket.html "struct rocket_ws::WebSocket")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_ws/websocket.rs.html#66-69)

#### pub fn [config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.config)
(self, config: [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_ws::Config")
) -> Self

Change the default connection configuration to `config`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example)
Example

    #[get("/echo")]
    fn echo_stream(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Stream!['static] {
        let ws = ws.config(ws::Config {
            max_send_queue: Some(5),
            ..Default::default()
        });
    
        ws::Stream! { ws =>
            for await message in ws {
                yield message?;
            }
        }
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_ws/websocket.rs.html#117-121)

#### pub fn [channel](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.channel)
<'r, F>(self, handler: F) -> [Channel](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.Channel.html "struct rocket_ws::Channel")
<'r>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
([DuplexStream](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/stream/struct.DuplexStream.html "struct rocket_ws::stream::DuplexStream")
) -> [BoxFuture](https://docs.rs/futures-core/0.3.31/futures_core/future/type.BoxFuture.html "type futures_core::future::BoxFuture")
<'r, [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/result/type.Result.html "type rocket_ws::result::Result")
<[()](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.unit.html)
\>> + 'r + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
,

Create a read/write channel to the client and call `handler` with it.

This method takes a `FnOnce`, `handler`, that consumes a read/write WebSocket channel, [`DuplexStream`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/stream/struct.DuplexStream.html "struct rocket_ws::stream::DuplexStream")
 to the client. See [`DuplexStream`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/stream/struct.DuplexStream.html "struct rocket_ws::stream::DuplexStream")
 for details on how to make use of the channel.

The `handler` must return a `Box`ed and `Pin`ned future: calling [`Box::pin()`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/boxed/struct.Box.html#method.pin "associated function alloc::boxed::Box::pin")
 with a future does just this as is the preferred mechanism to create a `Box<Pin<Future>>`. The future must return a [`Result<()>`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/result/type.Result.html "type rocket_ws::result::Result")
. The WebSocket connection is closed successfully if the future returns `Ok` and with an error if the future returns `Err`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#lifetimes)
Lifetimes

The `Channel` may borrow from the request. If it does, the lifetime should be specified as something other than `'static`. Otherwise, the `'static` lifetime should be used.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::futures::{SinkExt, StreamExt};
    
    #[get("/hello/<name>")]
    fn hello(ws: ws::WebSocket, name: &str) -> ws::Channel<'_> {
        ws.channel(move |mut stream| Box::pin(async move {
            let message = format!("Hello, {}!", name);
            let _ = stream.send(message.into()).await;
            Ok(())
        }))
    }
    
    #[get("/echo")]
    fn echo(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Channel<'static> {
        ws.channel(move |mut stream| Box::pin(async move {
            while let Some(message) = stream.next().await {
                let _ = stream.send(message?).await;
            }
    
            Ok(())
        }))
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_ws/websocket.rs.html#157-162)

#### pub fn [stream](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.stream)
<'r, F, S>(self, stream: F) -> [MessageStream](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/stream/struct.MessageStream.html "struct rocket_ws::stream::MessageStream")
<'r, S>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
([SplitStream](https://docs.rs/futures-util/0.3.31/futures_util/stream/stream/split/struct.SplitStream.html "struct futures_util::stream::stream::split::SplitStream")
<[DuplexStream](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/stream/struct.DuplexStream.html "struct rocket_ws::stream::DuplexStream")
\>) -> S + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'r, S: [Stream](https://docs.rs/futures-core/0.3.31/futures_core/stream/trait.Stream.html "trait futures_core::stream::Stream")
<Item = [Result](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/result/type.Result.html "type rocket_ws::result::Result")
<[Message](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/enum.Message.html "enum rocket_ws::Message")
\>> + [Send](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Send.html "trait core::marker::Send")
 + 'r,

Create a stream that consumes client [`Message`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/enum.Message.html "enum rocket_ws::Message")
s and emits its own.

This method takes a `FnOnce` `stream` that consumes a read-only stream and returns a stream of [`Message`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/enum.Message.html "enum rocket_ws::Message")
s. While the returned stream can be constructed in any manner, the [`Stream!`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/macro.Stream.html "macro rocket_ws::Stream")
 macro is the preferred method. In any case, the stream must be `Send`.

The returned stream must emit items of type `Result<Message>`. Items that are `Ok(Message)` are sent to the client while items of type `Err(Error)` result in the connection being closed and the remainder of the stream discarded.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-2)
Example

    
    // Use `Stream!`, which internally calls `WebSocket::stream()`.
    #[get("/echo?stream")]
    fn echo_stream(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Stream!['static] {
        ws::Stream! { ws =>
            for await message in ws {
                yield message?;
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Use a raw stream.
    #[get("/echo?compose")]
    fn echo_compose(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Stream!['static] {
        ws.stream(|io| io)
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_ws/websocket.rs.html#191-193)

#### pub fn [accept\_key](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.accept_key)
(&self) -> &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)

Returns the server’s fully computed and encoded WebSocket handshake accept key.

> The server takes the value of the `Sec-WebSocket-Key` sent in the handshake request, appends `258EAFA5-E914-47DA-95CA-C5AB0DC85B11`, SHA-1 of the new value, and is then base64 encoded.
> 
> – [`Sec-WebSocket-Accept`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Sec-WebSocket-Accept)

This is the value returned via the [`Sec-WebSocket-Accept`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Sec-WebSocket-Accept)
 header during the acceptance response.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-3)
Example

    #[get("/echo")]
    fn echo_stream(ws: ws::WebSocket) -> ws::Stream!['static] {
        let accept_key = ws.accept_key();
        ws.stream(|io| io)
    }

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "trait core::convert::From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-4)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-24)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

#### fn [on\_primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.on_primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-25)
Example

    println!("{}", value.on_primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-44)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.bold)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`attr()`](self::attr())
 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-45)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-54)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_ws/struct.WebSocket#example-62)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

# launch in rocket - Rust

[launch](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.launch?search=#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Attribute Macro launch Copy item path
=====================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_codegen/lib.rs.html#478)

    #[launch]

Expand description

Generates a `main` function that launches a returned `Rocket<Build>`.

When applied to a function that returns a `Rocket<Build>` instance, `#[launch]` automatically initializes an `async` runtime and launches the function’s returned instance:

    use rocket::{Rocket, Build};
    
    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> Rocket<Build> {
        rocket::build()
    }

This generates code equivalent to the following:

    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() {
        // Recall that an uninspected `Error` will cause a pretty-printed panic,
        // so rest assured errors do not go undetected when using `#[launch]`.
        let _ = rocket().launch().await;
    }

To avoid needing to import _any_ items in the common case, the `launch` attribute will infer a return type written as `_` as `Rocket<Build>`:

    #[launch]
    fn rocket() -> _ {
        rocket::build()
    }

The attributed function may be `async`:

    #[launch]
    async fn rocket() -> _ {
        some_async_work().await;
        rocket::build()
    }

---

# Config in rocket_sync_db_pools - Rust

[Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket\_sync\_db\_pools](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/index.html)

Struct Config Copy item path
============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/config.rs.html#33-42)

    pub struct Config {
        pub url: String,
        pub pool_size: u32,
        pub timeout: u8,
    }

Expand description

A base `Config` for any `Poolable` type.

For the following configuration:

    [global.databases.my_database]
    url = "postgres://root:root@localhost/my_database"
    pool_size = 10
    timeout = 5

…`Config::from("my_database", rocket)` would return the following struct:

    Config {
        url: "postgres://root:root@localhost/my_database".into(),
        pool_size: 10,
        timeout: 5
    };

If you want to implement your own custom database adapter (or other database-like struct that can be pooled by `r2d2`) and need some more configurations options, you may need to define a custom `Config` struct. Note, however, that the configuration values in `Config` are required.

Fields[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#fields)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#structfield.url)
`url: [String](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/alloc/string/struct.String.html "struct alloc::string::String") `

Connection URL specified in the Rocket configuration.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#structfield.pool_size)
`pool_size: [u32](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u32.html) `

Initial pool size. Defaults to the number of Rocket workers \* 4.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#structfield.timeout)
`timeout: [u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html) `

How long to wait, in seconds, for a new connection before timing out. Defaults to `5`.

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#implementations)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/config.rs.html#44-119)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#impl-Config)

### impl [Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_sync_db_pools::Config")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/config.rs.html#85-87)

#### pub fn [from](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.from)
(db\_name: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
, rocket: &[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/rkt/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::rkt::Rocket")
<[Build](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/enum.Build.html "enum rocket::phase::Build")
\>) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")
<[Config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config.html "struct rocket_sync_db_pools::Config")
, [Error](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/error/struct.Error.html "struct figment::error::Error")
\>

Retrieves the database configuration for the database named `name`.

This function is primarily used by the generated code from the `#[database]` attribute.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#example)
Example

    // Assume that these are the contents of `Rocket.toml`:
    [default.databases]
    my_db = { url = "db/db.sqlite", pool_size = 25 }
    my_other_db = { url = "mysql://root:root@localhost/database" }
    
    use rocket::{Rocket, Build};
    use rocket_sync_db_pools::Config;
    
    fn pool(rocket: &Rocket<Build>) {
        let config = Config::from("my_db", rocket).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(config.url, "db/db.sqlite");
        assert_eq!(config.pool_size, 25);
    
        let config = Config::from("my_other_db", rocket).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(config.url, "mysql://root:root@localhost/database");
    
        let workers = rocket.figment().extract_inner::<u32>(rocket::Config::WORKERS);
        assert_eq!(config.pool_size, (workers.unwrap() * 4));
    
        let config = Config::from("unknown_db", rocket);
        assert!(config.is_err())
    }

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_sync_db_pools/config.rs.html#103-118)

#### pub fn [figment](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.figment)
(db\_name: &[str](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.str.html)
, rocket: &[Rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/rkt/struct.Rocket.html "struct rocket::rkt::Rocket")
<[Build](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/phase/enum.Build.html "enum rocket::phase::Build")
\>) -> [Figment](https://docs.rs/figment/0.10.19/figment/figment/struct.Figment.html "struct figment::figment::Figment")

Returns a `Figment` focused on the configuration for the database with name `db_name`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::{Rocket, Build};
    use rocket_sync_db_pools::Config;
    
    fn pool(rocket: &Rocket<Build>) {
        let my_db_figment = Config::figment("my_db", rocket);
        let mysql_prod_figment = Config::figment("mysql_prod", rocket);
    }

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#impl-Into%3CU%3E-for-T)

### impl<T, U> [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<U> for T

where U: [From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "trait core::convert::From")
<T>,

[Source](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/src/core/convert/mod.rs.html#777)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.into)

#### fn [into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html#tymethod.into)
(self) -> U

Calls `U::from(self)`.

That is, this conversion is whatever the implementation of `[From](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.From.html "trait core::convert::From") <T> for U` chooses to do.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#impl-IntoEither-for-T)

### impl<T> [IntoEither](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html "trait either::into_either::IntoEither")
 for T

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#29)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self>

where F: [FnOnce](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ops/function/trait.FnOnce.html "trait core::ops::function::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

,

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#impl-Paint-for-T)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#example-2)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

Example

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#example-22)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#example-42)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.bold)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#example-43)
Example

    println!("{}", value.bold());

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#example-52)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

 set to \[`Quirk :: Mask`\].

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#example-60)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket_sync_db_pools/struct.Config#method.new)

<Self>

where Self: [Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

Create a new [`Painted`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
 with a default [`Style`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
. [Read more](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)

<S>(&self, style: S) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<&Self>

where S: [Into](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/convert/trait.Into.html "trait core::convert::Into")
<[Style](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
\>,

# SecretKey in rocket::config - Rust

[SecretKey](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[config](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/index.html)

Struct SecretKey Copy item path
===============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/secret_key.rs.html#78-81)

    pub struct SecretKey { /* private fields */ }

Available on **crate feature `secrets`** only.

Expand description

A cryptographically secure secret key.

A `SecretKey` is primarily used by [private cookies](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/requests/#private-cookies)
. See the [configuration guide](https://rocket.rs/v0.5/guide/configuration/#secret-key)
 for further details. It can be configured from 256-bit random material or a 512-bit master key, each as either a base64-encoded string or raw bytes.

    use rocket::config::Config;
    
    // NOTE: Don't (!) use this key! Generate your own and keep it private!
    //       e.g. via `head -c64 /dev/urandom | base64`
    let figment = Config::figment()
        .merge(("secret_key", "hPrYyЭRiMyµ5sBB1π+CMæ1køFsåqKvBiQJxBVHQk="));
    
    let config = Config::from(figment);
    assert!(!config.secret_key.is_zero());

When configured in the debug profile with the `secrets` feature enabled, a key set as `0` is automatically regenerated at launch time from the OS’s random source if available.

    use rocket::config::Config;
    use rocket::local::blocking::Client;
    
    let figment = Config::figment()
        .merge(("secret_key", vec![0u8; 64]))
        .select("debug");
    
    let rocket = rocket::custom(figment);
    let client = Client::tracked(rocket).expect("okay in debug");
    assert!(!client.rocket().config().secret_key.is_zero());

When running in any other profile with the `secrets` feature enabled, providing a key of `0` or not provided a key at all results in an error at launch-time:

    use rocket::config::Config;
    use rocket::figment::Profile;
    use rocket::local::blocking::Client;
    use rocket::error::ErrorKind;
    
    let profile = Profile::const_new("staging");
    let figment = Config::figment()
        .merge(("secret_key", vec![0u8; 64]))
        .select(profile.clone());
    
    let rocket = rocket::custom(figment);
    let error = Client::tracked(rocket).expect_err("error in non-debug");
    assert!(matches!(error.kind(), ErrorKind::InsecureSecretKey(profile)));

Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#implementations)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/secret_key.rs.html#83-181)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#impl-SecretKey)

### impl [SecretKey](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey.html "struct rocket::config::SecretKey")

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/secret_key.rs.html#104-106)

#### pub fn [from](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.from)
(master: &\[[u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\
\]) -> [SecretKey](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey.html "struct rocket::config::SecretKey")

Creates a `SecretKey` from a 512-bit `master` key. For security, `master` _must_ be cryptographically random.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#panics)
Panics

Panics if `master` < 64 bytes.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example)
Example

    use rocket::config::SecretKey;
    
    let key = SecretKey::from(&master);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/secret_key.rs.html#123-125)

#### pub fn [derive\_from](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.derive_from)
(material: &\[[u8](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u8.html)\
\]) -> [SecretKey](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey.html "struct rocket::config::SecretKey")

Derives a `SecretKey` from 256 bits of cryptographically random `material`. For security, `material` _must_ be cryptographically random.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#panics-1)
Panics

Panics if `material` < 32 bytes.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-1)
Example

    use rocket::config::SecretKey;
    
    let key = SecretKey::derive_from(&material);

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/secret_key.rs.html#137-139)

#### pub fn [generate](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.generate)
() -> [Option](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/option/enum.Option.html "enum rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::option::Option")
<[SecretKey](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey.html "struct rocket::config::SecretKey")
\>

Attempts to generate a `SecretKey` from randomness retrieved from the OS. If randomness from the OS isn’t available, returns `None`.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-2)
Example

    use rocket::config::SecretKey;
    
    let key = SecretKey::generate();

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/secret_key.rs.html#152-154)

#### pub fn [is\_zero](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.is_zero)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if `self` is the `0`\-key.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-3)
Example

    use rocket::config::SecretKey;
    
    let master = vec![0u8; 64];
    let key = SecretKey::from(&master);
    assert!(key.is_zero());

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/config/secret_key.rs.html#171-173)

#### pub fn [is\_provided](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.is_provided)
(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns `true` if `self` was not automatically generated and is not zero.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-4)
Example

    use rocket::config::SecretKey;
    
    let master = vec![0u8; 64];
    let key = SecretKey::generate().unwrap();
    assert!(!key.is_provided());
    
    let master = vec![0u8; 64];
    let key = SecretKey::from(&master);
    assert!(!key.is_provided());

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-5)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.primary)

#### fn [primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-6)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-25)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-45)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

 set to \[`Attribute :: Bold`\].

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.quirk)

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-55)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

Example

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#example-63)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.new)

#### fn [new](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)
(self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<Self>

where Self: [Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

Create a new [`Painted`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
 with a default [`Style`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
. [Read more](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#126)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#method.paint)

#### fn [paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.paint)
<S>(&self, style: S) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<&Self>

where S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Style](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
\>,

Apply a style wholesale to `self`. Any previous style is replaced. [Read more](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.paint)

[Source](https://docs.rs/typenum/1.19.0/src/typenum/type_operators.rs.html#34)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/config/struct.SecretKey#impl-Same-for-T)

### impl<T> [Same](https://docs.rs/typenum/1.19.0/typenum/type_operators/trait.Same.html "trait typenum::type_operators::Same")

# main in rocket - Rust

[main](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.main?search=#)

-------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Attribute Macro main Copy item path
===================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_codegen/lib.rs.html#419)

    #[main]

Expand description

Retrofits `async fn` support in `main` functions.

A `main` `async fn` function decorated with `#[rocket::main]` is transformed into a regular `main` function that internally initializes a Rocket-specific tokio runtime and runs the attributed `async fn` inside of it:

    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rocket::Error> {
        let _rocket = rocket::build()
            .ignite().await?
            .launch().await?;
    
        Ok(())
    }

It should be used only when the return values of `ignite()` or `launch()` are to be inspected:

    #[rocket::main]
    async fn main() -> Result<(), rocket::Error> {
        let rocket = rocket::build().ignite().await?;
        println!("Hello, Rocket: {:?}", rocket);
    
        let rocket = rocket.launch().await?;
        println!("Welcome back, Rocket: {:?}", rocket);
    
        Ok(())
    }

For all other cases, use [`#[launch]`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/launch)
 instead.

The function attributed with `#[rocket::main]` _must_ be `async` and _must_ be called `main`. Violation of either results in a compile-time error.

---

# XssFilter in rocket::shield - Rust

[XssFilter](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#)

----------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)
::[shield](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/index.html)

Enum XssFilter Copy item path
=============================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket/shield/policy.rs.html#388-399)

    pub enum XssFilter {
        Disable,
        Enable,
        EnableBlock,
    }

Expand description

The [X-XSS-Protection](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/X-XSS-Protection)
 header: filters some forms of reflected [XSS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Cross-site_scripting)
 attacks. Modern browsers do not support or enforce this header.

Variants[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#variants)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#variant.Disable)

### Disable

Disables XSS filtering.

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#variant.Enable)

### Enable

Enables XSS filtering. If XSS is detected, the browser will sanitize before rendering the page (_Shield default_).

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#variant.EnableBlock)

### EnableBlock

Enables XSS filtering. If XSS is detected, the browser will not render the page.

Trait Implementations[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#trait-implementations)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#example)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#method.primary)

#### fn [primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#example-1)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#method.bg)

#### fn [bg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.bg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the background set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
 and [`on_green()`](self::on_green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#example-20)
Example

Set background color to red using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.bg(Color::Red);

Set background color to red using [`on_red()`](self::on_red())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.on_red();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)

\>

 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#method.attr)

#### fn [attr](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.attr)
(&self, value: [Attribute](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the styling [`Attribute`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Attribute.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Attribute")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use attribute-specific builder methods like [`bold()`](self::bold())
 and [`underline()`](self::underline())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#example-40)
Example

Make text bold using `attr()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Attribute};
    
    painted.attr(Attribute::Bold);

Make text bold using using [`bold()`](self::bold())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.bold();

#### fn [quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.quirk)
(&self, value: [Quirk](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Enables the `yansi` [`Quirk`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/attr_quirk/enum.Quirk.html "enum yansi::attr_quirk::Quirk")
 `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use quirk-specific builder methods like [`mask()`](self::mask())
 and [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#example-50)
Example

Enable wrapping using `.quirk()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Quirk};
    
    painted.quirk(Quirk::Wrap);

Enable wrapping using [`wrap()`](self::wrap())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.wrap();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#method.whenever)

#### fn [whenever](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.whenever)
(&self, value: [Condition](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Conditionally enable styling based on whether the [`Condition`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/condition/struct.Condition.html "struct yansi::condition::Condition")
 `value` applies. Replaces any previous condition.

See the [crate level docs](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/index.html#per-style "mod yansi")
 for more details.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#example-58)
Example

Enable styling `painted` only when both `stdout` and `stderr` are TTYs:

    use yansi::{Paint, Condition};
    
    painted.red().on_yellow().whenever(Condition::STDOUTERR_ARE_TTY);

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#104)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#method.new)

<Self>

where Self: [Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

Create a new [`Painted`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
 with a default [`Style`](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
. [Read more](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.new)

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#126)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/shield/enum.XssFilter#method.paint)

#### fn [paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.paint)
<S>(&self, style: S) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<&Self>

where S: [Into](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/convert/trait.Into.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::convert::Into")
<[Style](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/style/struct.Style.html "struct yansi::style::Style")
\>,

Apply a style wholesale to `self`. Any previous style is replaced. [Read more](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#method.paint)

# async_test in rocket - Rust

[async\_test](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/attr.async_test?search=#)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Attribute Macro async\_test Copy item path
==========================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_codegen/lib.rs.html#377)

    #[async_test]

Expand description

Retrofits supports for `async fn` in unit tests.

Simply decorate a test `async fn` with `#[async_test]` instead of `#[test]`:

    #[cfg(test)]
    mod tests {
        #[async_test]
        async fn test() {
            /* .. */
        }
    }

The attribute rewrites the function to execute inside of a Rocket-compatible async runtime.

---

# FromForm in rocket - Rust

[FromForm](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/derive.FromForm?search=#)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[rocket](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/index.html)

Derive Macro FromForm Copy item path
====================================

[Source](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/src/rocket_codegen/lib.rs.html#746)

    #[derive(FromForm)]
    {
        // Attributes available to this derive:
        #[form]
        #[field]
    }
    

Expand description

Derive for the [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html)
 trait.

The [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html)
 derive can be applied to structures with named or unnamed fields:

    use rocket::form::FromForm;
    
    #[derive(FromForm)]
    struct MyStruct<'r> {
        field: usize,
        #[field(name = "renamed_field")]
        #[field(name = uncased("RenamedField"))]
        other: &'r str,
        #[field(validate = range(1..), default = 3)]
        r#type: usize,
        #[field(default = None)]
        is_nice: bool,
    }
    
    #[derive(FromForm)]
    #[field(validate = len(6..))]
    #[field(validate = neq("password"))]
    struct Password<'r>(&'r str);

Each field type is required to implement [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html)
.

The derive generates an implementation of the [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html)
 trait.

**Named Fields**

If the structure has named fields, the implementation parses a form whose field names match the field names of the structure on which the derive was applied. Each field’s value is parsed with the [`FromForm`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/trait.FromForm.html)
 implementation of the field’s type. The `FromForm` implementation succeeds only when all fields parse successfully or return a default. Errors are collected into a [`form::Errors`](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/form/struct.Errors.html)
 and returned if non-empty after parsing all fields.

**Unnamed Fields**

If the structure is a tuple struct, it must have exactly one field. The implementation parses a form exactly when the internal field parses a form _and_ any `#[field]` validations succeed.

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/derive.FromForm?search=#syntax)
Syntax

The derive accepts one field attribute: `field`, and one container attribute, `form`, with the following syntax:

    field := name? default? validate*
    
    name := 'name' '=' name_val ','?
    name_val :=  '"' FIELD_NAME '"'
             | 'uncased(' '"' FIELD_NAME '"' ')
    
    default := 'default' '=' EXPR ','?
             | 'default_with' '=' EXPR ','?
    
    validate := 'validate' '=' EXPR ','?
    
    FIELD_NAME := valid field name, according to the HTML5 spec
    EXPR := valid expression, as defined by Rust

`#[field]` can be applied any number of times on a field. `default` and `default_with` are mutually exclusive: at most _one_ of `default` or `default_with` can be present per field.

    #[derive(FromForm)]
    struct MyStruct {
        #[field(name = uncased("number"))]
        #[field(default = 42)]
        field: usize,
        #[field(name = "renamed_field")]
        #[field(name = uncased("anotherName"))]
        #[field(validate = eq("banana"))]
        #[field(validate = neq("orange"))]
        other: String
    }

For tuples structs, the `field` attribute can be applied to the structure itself:

    #[derive(FromForm)]
    #[field(default = 42, validate = eq(42))]
    struct Meaning(usize);

### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/derive.FromForm?search=#field-attribute-parameters)
Field Attribute Parameters

*   **`name`**
    
    A `name` attribute changes the name to match against when parsing the form field. The value is either an exact string to match against (`"foo"`), or `uncased("foo")`, which causes the match to be case-insensitive but case-preserving. When more than one `name` attribute is applied, the field will match against _any_ of the names.
    
*   **`validate = expr`**
    
    The validation `expr` is run if the field type parses successfully. The expression must return a value of type `Result<(), form::Errors>`. On `Err`, the errors are added to the thus-far collected errors. If more than one `validate` attribute is applied, _all_ validations are run.
    
*   **`default = expr`**
    
    If `expr` is not literally `None`, the parameter sets the default value of the field to be `expr.into()`. If `expr` _is_ `None`, the parameter _unsets_ the default value of the field, if any. The expression is only evaluated if the attributed field is missing in the incoming form.
    
    Except when `expr` is `None`, `expr` must be of type `T: Into<F>` where `F` is the field’s type.
    
*   **`default_with = expr`**
    
    The parameter sets the default value of the field to be exactly `expr` which must be of type `Option<F>` where `F` is the field’s type. If the expression evaluates to `None`, there is no default. Otherwise the value wrapped in `Some` is used. The expression is only evaluated if the attributed field is missing in the incoming form.
    
        use std::num::NonZeroUsize;
        
        #[derive(FromForm)]
        struct MyForm {
            // `NonZeroUsize::new()` return an `Option<NonZeroUsize>`.
            #[field(default_with = NonZeroUsize::new(42))]
            num: NonZeroUsize,
        }
    

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/derive.FromForm?search=#generics)
Generics
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The derive accepts any number of type generics and at most one lifetime generic. If a type generic is present, the generated implementation will require a bound of `FromForm<'r>` for the field type containing the generic. For example, for a struct `struct Foo<T>(Json<T>)`, the bound `Json<T>: FromForm<'r>` will be added to the generated implementation.

    use rocket::form::FromForm;
    use rocket::serde::json::Json;
    
    // The bounds `A: FromForm<'r>`, `B: FromForm<'r>` will be required.
    #[derive(FromForm)]
    struct FancyForm<A, B> {
        first: A,
        second: B,
    };
    
    // The bound `Json<T>: FromForm<'r>` will be required.
    #[derive(FromForm)]
    struct JsonToken<T> {
        token: Json<T>,
        id: usize,
    }

If a lifetime generic is present, it is replaced with `'r` in the generated implementation `impl FromForm<'r>`:

    // Generates `impl<'r> FromForm<'r> for MyWrapper<'r>`.
    #[derive(FromForm)]
    struct MyWrapper<'a>(&'a str);

Both type generics and one lifetime generic may be used:

    use rocket::form::{self, FromForm};
    
    // The bound `form::Result<'r, T>: FromForm<'r>` will be required.
    #[derive(FromForm)]
    struct SomeResult<'o, T>(form::Result<'o, T>);

The special bounds on `Json` and `Result` are required due to incomplete and incorrect support for lifetime generics in `async` blocks in Rust. See [rust-lang/#64552](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/64552)
 for further details.

---

    pub struct LoadedEntry {
        pub name: String,
        pub oid: String,

        pub description: String,

Available on **crate feature `mtls`** only.

Expand description

Temporary structure, created when reading a file containing OID declarations

[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/struct.LoadedEntry#method.into_either)

#### fn [into\_either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)
(self, into\_left: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/struct.LoadedEntry#)

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left` is `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either)

[Source](https://docs.rs/either/1/src/either/into_either.rs.html#55-57)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/struct.LoadedEntry#method.into_either_with)

#### fn [into\_either\_with](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)
<F>(self, into\_left: F) -> [Either](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
<Self, Self> [ⓘ](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/struct.LoadedEntry#)

where F: [FnOnce](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/asn1_rs/nom/lib/std/ops/trait.FnOnce.html "trait rocket::mtls::oid::asn1_rs::nom::lib::std::ops::FnOnce")
(&Self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)
,

Converts `self` into a [`Left`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Left "variant either::Either::Left")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 if `into_left(&self)` returns `true`. Converts `self` into a [`Right`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html#variant.Right "variant either::Either::Right")
 variant of [`Either<Self, Self>`](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/enum.Either.html "enum either::Either")
 otherwise. [Read more](https://docs.rs/either/1/either/into_either/trait.IntoEither.html#method.into_either_with)

### impl<T> [Paint](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html "trait yansi::paint::Paint")
 for T

where T: ?[Sized](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/marker/trait.Sized.html "trait core::marker::Sized")
,

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/struct.LoadedEntry#method.fg)

#### fn [fg](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.fg)
(&self, value: [Color](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/color/enum.Color.html "enum yansi::color::Color")
) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns a styled value derived from `self` with the foreground set to `value`.

This method should be used rarely. Instead, prefer to use color-specific builder methods like [`red()`](self::red())
 and [`green()`](self::green())
, which have the same functionality but are pithier.

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/struct.LoadedEntry#example)
Example

Set foreground color to white using `fg()`:

    use yansi::{Paint, Color};
    
    painted.fg(Color::White);

Set foreground color to white using [`white()`](self::white())
.

    use yansi::Paint;
    
    painted.white();

[Source](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/src/yansi/paint.rs.html#135)
[§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/struct.LoadedEntry#method.primary)

#### fn [primary](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/trait.Paint.html#tymethod.primary)
(&self) -> [Painted](https://docs.rs/yansi/1.0.1/yansi/paint/struct.Painted.html "struct yansi::paint::Painted")
<[&T](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.reference.html)
\>

Returns `self` with the [`fg()`](self::fg())
 set to \[`Color :: Primary`\].

##### [§](https://api.rocket.rs/v0.5/rocket/mtls/oid/struct.LoadedEntry#example-1)
Example

    println!("{}", value.primary());
